title: About Improve ME Institute | KHDA-Approved Tutoring Dubai
description: Learn about Improve ME Institute, Dubai's trusted tutoring centre at Gold and Diamond Park. Qualified tutors, small groups, and proven results for students aged 3–18.
html:
OUR STORY

How Improve ME Began

Improve ME Institute started as a family-run business, built from the ground up by the Daswani family with a single driving purpose: to raise student attainment levels in Dubai.

What began as a small tutoring operation has grown into one of Dubai's most trusted academic centres — KHDA-approved, operating since 2010, and serving students from over 30 schools across the city.

From the very beginning, the focus was never on volume. It was on results. Small groups, consistent tutors, structured learning, and honest communication with parents. That hasn't changed.

Today Improve ME supports students from age 3 all the way through to A-Level and IB Diploma — a complete academic journey, under one roof, with one consistent approach.

1,000+Students
40+Specialist Tutors
2010Established
4.8★Google Rating
“
For over thirty years, I have dedicated my career to helping students learn better and achieve outstanding results. Improve ME Institute was built on the same promise: raise standards, build future-ready skills, and support every child to thrive in the real world.

Today, we stand strong with 40 faculty members and thousands of students enrolled annually, as we combine high-quality tutoring, life-skills programmes, and proven teaching methods to improve student attainment.

I am especially proud that the next generation of my family has joined this mission. My sons, Shaun and Jason, now lead our Upper and Lower Secondary departments, bringing fresh perspectives and deep expertise to serve families across Dubai.

We are excited to support your child's academic journey and help them reach their full potential.

Neeta Daswani

Founder, Improve ME Institute

LEADERSHIP

Meet Our Leadership Team

A family-run institute with three generations of educational expertise, led by experienced educators who are passionate about student success.

Neeta Daswani

Founder & Director

For over thirty years, I have dedicated my career to helping students learn better and achieve outstanding results. Improve ME Institute was built on the same promise: raise standards, build future-ready skills, and support every child to thrive in the real world.

Shaun Daswani

Upper Secondary Department Head & Co-Founder

MSc Mathematics, Imperial College London + BSc Mathematics and Economics, UCL

Leading our upper secondary team with expertise in GCSE, A-Level, and IB curriculum. Shaun brings fresh perspectives and deep subject knowledge to support students through their most critical exam years.

LinkedIn

Jason Daswani

Lower Secondary Department Head & Co-Founder

BSc Mathematics, London School of Economics

Leading our lower secondary department with a focus on student outcomes and exam preparation. Jason's approach combines proven teaching methods with modern learning techniques.

LinkedIn

WHY IMPROVE ME

What Makes Us Different

Built on relationships, not transactions.

Family-run, genuinely invested

Improve ME was built by the Daswani family. Every student matters personally — not as a number on a spreadsheet.

Tutors who stay

We don't rotate staff. Your child works with the same tutor consistently, so they build trust, momentum, and real progress.

We tell you the truth

If your child needs more work in a specific area, we tell you — clearly, and early. No vague reassurances.

We measure what actually matters

Progress reports, test results, and honest conversations — not just end-of-term grades.

WHO WE ARE

A tutoring centre built on results, not promises

Structured, curriculum-aligned tutoring

Maths, English, and Sciences from EYFS through Key Stage 1–3, GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, and IB.

Small groups that bring out the best in every student

Maximum 6 students per class, with an average of just 2–3 — more attention and faster progress.

Future-ready skills programmes

Financial Literacy, Chess Mastery, CAT4 Test Preparation, and AI Literacy.

A place built for learning

7,000 sq ft in Gold and Diamond Park with UK-themed classrooms designed to inspire.

Trusted by families from 30+ schools across Dubai including Dubai College, Repton, Jumeirah College, JESS, and Kings.

Our Approach to Learning

Alongside our core academic programme, we offer skills-based enrichment programmes designed to develop well-rounded individuals — not just high achievers in the exam room. We combine structured curriculum support with skills that prepare students for real-world success.

Academic Excellence

Small group tutoring in Maths, English, and Sciences. Every session is tailored to your child's year group, exam board, and school curriculum.

Skills-Based Learning

Chess for strategic thinking, Financial Literacy for real-world skills, CAT4 prep for cognitive development, and AI Literacy for future readiness.

Exam-Focused

Our tutors know what examiners expect across GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, and IB. We teach exam technique, time management, and how to maximize marks.

A Space Built for Ambition

Our Gold & Diamond Park centre includes a dedicated area for future careers, universities, and industries. It's designed to let students dream beyond the next exam — to explore pathways, understand the world they're entering, and stay motivated about why their education matters.

This isn't a typical tuition centre. It's a place where students come to work hard and leave with more than just better grades.

Impact & Results

FAQ

title: Tutoring FAQs | Improve ME Institute Dubai
description: Got questions about tutoring at Improve ME Institute in Dubai? Find answers about our subjects, age groups, curriculum support, session structure, and how to get started.
html:
1. Home
  2. /FAQ

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about tutoring at Improve ME Institute, Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai.

Filter by category or search keywords like "pricing", "online", or "IB".

About Improve ME

Improve ME Institute is a KHDA-approved tutoring centre based at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai. Established in 2010, we support over 1,000 students every year aged 3 to 18 across a wide range of subjects and qualification levels — from EYFS and Key Stage 1 through to GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, IB Diploma, and MYP. We teach exclusively in small groups, with a maximum of six students and most groups averaging two to three. Every child receives focused, individual attention in every session.
We are located at Suites 3010–3017, Building 3, Gold and Diamond Park, Sheikh Zayed Road, Dubai. The centre sits directly next to the Equiti Metro Station and free parking is available on site.
Yes — Improve ME Institute is fully approved by the Knowledge and Human Development Authority (KHDA), Dubai's regulatory body for private education. KHDA approval confirms that our teaching standards, premises, and operations meet the requirements set for educational centres in Dubai.
We support students from age 3 (FS1/Nursery) through to age 18 (Year 13), covering the full school journey from EYFS and primary through to GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, IB Diploma, and MYP. Our Primary Department covers KG1 to Year 6, and our Secondary Department covers Year 7 to Year 13.
Improve ME Institute was established in 2010. We have been supporting Dubai families for over 15 years, earning a 4.8-star rating from more than 200 Google reviews. Over 1,000 students trust us with their education every year.
No — we support students across all major curricula taught in Dubai, including the British curriculum (GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level), the International Baccalaureate (MYP and Diploma), and early years frameworks including EYFS. If you are unsure whether we cover your child's curriculum, contact us and we will advise you directly.

Subjects & Curriculum

We offer tuition across a comprehensive range of subjects including Mathematics, English, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Business Studies, Economics, and Psychology. At primary level, we deliver our proprietary MaThesis (Mathematics) and EngLit (English) programmes, along with Science for Years 4–6. We also offer specialist programmes including Chess Mastery, AI Literacy, Financial Literacy, CAT4 Test Prep, 7+/11+ Entrance Preparation, and Educational Counselling. See our full subject list on the Our Courses page.
We cover the core IB Diploma and MYP subjects including Mathematics (Analysis & Approaches and Applications & Interpretation at HL and SL), Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Economics, Business Management, and Psychology. For IB subjects not listed, contact us and we will advise on availability.
Yes — we support both GCSE (AQA, Edexcel, and OCR) and IGCSE (Cambridge International and Edexcel International). The two qualifications have different assessment structures and we tailor our preparation accordingly. If you are unsure which your child is studying, check with their school or share their subject specification with us.
Yes — we support primary students across EYFS, Key Stage 1, and Key Stage 2 in Mathematics, English, and Science. Our primary programmes — MaThesis and EngLit — are structured around the British curriculum and designed to build strong foundations while developing genuine confidence and ability.
Yes — Chess Mastery, AI Literacy, and Financial Literacy are all available at Improve ME. These programmes develop strategic thinking, critical reasoning, and future-facing skills alongside core academic subjects. They are suitable for students from age 6 upwards and run alongside our regular academic timetable.
Yes — we offer specialist 7+ and 11+ Entrance Preparation for students targeting competitive school admissions. Our programme covers the verbal reasoning, non-verbal reasoning, mathematics, and English components typically tested in these assessments, with structured practice and exam technique built in throughout.
At primary level and Key Stage 3, science is taught as a single integrated subject — exactly as it is in school at those stages. Our approach at KS3 is booster-focused: we expect the school to cover the core content, and our sessions concentrate on reinforcing understanding and building exam technique around what is already being taught. From GCSE onwards, the sciences separate into distinct qualifications — Physics, Chemistry, and Biology each have their own page and their own specialist tutor. Our Science tutors at GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, and IB level hold degrees in their specific field and teach only their specialist subject.
These subjects are offered from Year 10 onwards, beginning at GCSE or IGCSE level. If your child is in Years 7–9 and is interested in these subjects, we recommend exploring our Educational Counselling service, which can help with early subject planning and pathway decisions.

Booking & Assessments

You can book a free assessment by completing the enquiry form on our Contact page, calling us directly, or sending us a WhatsApp message. We will arrange a convenient time for your child to visit the centre. You do not need to complete any forms before the assessment itself — the enquiry form is optional but helps us prepare in advance and ensure we reach you quickly.
These are two separate forms used at different stages of the process. The enquiry form — labelled "Book a Free Assessment" on our Contact page — is your starting point. Completing it is optional but encouraged: it helps us understand your child's needs before you visit and allows us to reach out to confirm your appointment quickly. It is not a commitment of any kind. The enrolment form is completed after your child's assessment, once you have decided to join and confirmed your schedule. Every new student must complete the enrolment form before their first session — enrolment cannot be confirmed without it. You can find the enrolment form at improvemeinstitute.com/enrolment-page.
The free assessment is a short diagnostic evaluation carried out by one of our subject tutors. Each subject takes approximately 8 to 10 minutes, so if your child is being assessed in two subjects, you should allow around 20 minutes in total. The assessment helps us understand your child's current level, identify specific gaps, and recommend the most appropriate group and learning plan. It is not a test to pass or fail — it is simply a starting point.
Once the assessment is complete, our tutors will share their feedback alongside recommended session times that fit your child's level, curriculum, and schedule. Slots fill up quickly, so we recommend confirming your place as soon as possible. Once a schedule is agreed and payment is completed, your child's place is confirmed.
Yes — trial classes are available subject to availability. A trial session is charged at AED 350 per subject. If you decide to continue and enrol following the trial, the AED 350 is refunded and credited in full against your chosen subject package. The trial carries no further obligation if you decide not to proceed. To arrange a trial class, please mention it when you book your free assessment and we will advise on current availability.
No forms are required before the assessment itself. If you have not already completed the enquiry form, there is nothing to prepare — simply arrive for your scheduled appointment. The enrolment form is completed after the assessment, once you have confirmed your place and agreed your schedule. You can find it at improvemeinstitute.com/enrolment-page. Every enrolled student must complete this form before their first session begins.
We understand that schedules can change. If you need to move your assessment date, simply contact us and we will find the earliest available alternative slot.

Pricing & Payments

Session fees are charged per subject and vary by year group. At primary level, sessions start from AED 160 per session for KG1–KG2, rising to AED 180 for Years 4–6. At secondary level, sessions for Years 7–9 are AED 200 per subject. For Years 10–11 and 12–13, fees range from AED 180 to AED 200 per subject depending on year group. Multi-subject discounts are available for students booking across two or more subjects. All fees are subject to 5% VAT.
Yes. There is a one-time enrolment fee of AED 300 per student, payable on joining. This fee covers your child's assessment and onboarding at the institute and is valid for the lifetime of their enrolment at Improve ME. It is non-refundable.
Yes — package pricing is available for students booking a block of 10 or more sessions per subject. The package discount applies only when the full block is booked upfront; it does not apply to shorter bookings or individual sessions. At secondary level, additional discounts apply when two or more subjects are booked simultaneously. Only one discount or package can be applied per student at any one time. Contact us for a personalised quote based on your child's year group and subjects.
Payment plans are available upon request, provided the total invoiced amount exceeds AED 4,000. If approved, the first instalment is 60% of the total, with the remaining balance due once half of the sessions have been completed. Please speak to our team to request a payment plan.
Fees are payable via bank transfer or payment link. Full payment details are confirmed at the time of booking. Please speak to our admin team if you have any questions about payment.
No — all session fees are subject to an additional 5% VAT in line with UAE tax regulations. VAT is clearly shown on all invoices.
Yes. We run Summer and Winter School programmes for students from FS1 to Year 13. Holiday rates are structured by the number of sessions per week, with per-session rates decreasing as frequency increases. Holiday pricing is available to students enrolled in the most recent term. Contact us for current availability and rates.
Crash courses are intensive subject programmes for students in Years 10 and 11 who need focused exam preparation. Each crash course covers 12 hours of teaching per subject. For Year 10 the total is AED 1,440 per subject, and for Year 11 it is AED 1,800 per subject. The discounted rate applies when the full 12-hour block is booked. All fees are subject to 5% VAT.

Sessions & Format

Session length varies by age group. EYFS sessions are 45 minutes. From Year 2 onwards, standard sessions are 60 minutes per subject. CAT4 preparation sessions at primary level run for two hours, covering both Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning in a single block.
For students with ongoing support needs, we typically recommend one to two sessions per subject per week. For students approaching examinations, two to three sessions per week is common. Our tutors will advise on the recommended frequency following the initial assessment.
We teach exclusively in small groups, with a maximum of six students and most groups averaging two to three. We do not offer large group or classroom-style sessions. The small group format means every student receives individual attention and direct feedback in every session.
Small group sessions create a learning dynamic that private tutoring cannot replicate. Students who might hesitate to ask a question often find that a classmate asks it first — opening the explanation for everyone in the group. That peer dynamic builds academic confidence over time: students become more willing to attempt problems, challenge their thinking, and engage actively with the material. Our groups average two to three students — small enough that every child receives individual attention and direct feedback in every session. You get the focus and accountability of personalised support, without the pressure and isolation of sitting alone with a tutor.
Yes. Every student has a structured learning plan developed following their initial assessment. Sessions follow this plan, adapting as the student progresses. Every session has a clear objective and builds on the previous one.
Every student is assessed before joining a group. Sessions are then structured to match the level of the students in that group. For students with strong existing knowledge, sessions typically open with an independent problem or question, followed by targeted doubt-clearing and timed exam practice. For students building from the foundations, the session begins with content introduction and guided discussion before moving to practice and assignments. Groups are formed by year group, curriculum, exam board, and specification — so your child is always working alongside students at an equivalent stage.
If your child needs to miss a session, please notify us at least 24 hours in advance via phone, email, or WhatsApp. Students who notify us in advance are entitled to a make-up session, subject to teacher availability. Students who do not attend without prior notice will not be offered a make-up class.
Make-up sessions are available per subject per term, subject to teacher availability. Primary Department students are entitled to one make-up session per subject per term. Secondary Department students are entitled to two make-up sessions per subject per term. Make-up sessions are scheduled based on teacher availability or during half-term, must be completed before the end of the current term, and cannot be carried over to the following term. Unused make-up sessions expire at the end of each term.
Our sessions are primarily delivered in-person at our Gold & Diamond Park centre, and we recommend in-person attendance for the best outcomes. Online sessions are available in certain circumstances — please mention your preference when you enquire and we will advise on current availability.
If your child's regular tutor is unavailable, we will arrange for a qualified substitute tutor to cover the class. All of our tutors hold a bachelor's or master's degree in their subject and are trained to our teaching methodology, so your child's learning will not be disrupted. You will be notified in advance wherever possible.
Yes. New students are welcome to join at any point during the term. If your child starts after the mid-term point, fees are calculated on a pro-rated basis, with a minimum of half-term fees applicable.

Results & Progress

All of our tutors hold at least a bachelor's degree in their subject area, and many hold master's degrees. Our Secondary Department is led by our co-founders: Shaun Daswani, who holds an MSc in Mathematics from Imperial College London and a BSc in Mathematics and Economics from UCL; and Jason Daswani, who holds a BSc in Mathematics from the London School of Economics. Every tutor also completes structured in-house training to ensure full alignment with our teaching methodology and the specific requirements of each exam board.
We use a data-driven progress report that is updated by your child's tutor after every session. Parents can log in to view the report at any time. It records the topics covered in each session and whether homework has been submitted — homework completion is one of the most reliable live indicators of a student's engagement and progress trajectory. In addition to the report, parents receive direct contact — by phone, message, or meeting — if anything needs attention between sessions. For secondary students in exam years, progress is also monitored against grade targets, and parents are alerted promptly if preparation or expected work is falling behind.
Our students consistently achieve strong results at GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, and IB — with many securing places at leading UK and international universities. We support students from some of Dubai's top schools and hold a 4.8-star Google rating from over 200 families. Specific grade outcomes and university destinations are available on our results page.
Yes — mock exam preparation is a core part of our programme for exam-year students. We use timed past paper sessions, mark scheme feedback, and mock results to identify remaining gaps and adjust the revision plan for the final stretch.
Most parents and students begin to notice a visible improvement within a full term of consistent attendance. Progress depends on the student's starting point, how regularly they attend, and — crucially — how consistently homework is completed between sessions. Think of it like a relationship with a doctor: if you do not follow the advice given between appointments, improvement will be slower regardless of the quality of the consultation. Students who attend consistently and complete their work between sessions make the fastest progress. Our tutors will give you an honest picture of expected progress at the assessment stage — including a realistic timeline based on your child's current level and target grade.
If a student's progress is not on track, we will discuss this with you directly and make appropriate adjustments — whether that means modifying the learning plan, increasing session frequency, or reviewing the group allocation. We are committed to every student's outcomes and will not simply continue sessions that are not delivering results.

Still have questions?

Our team is happy to help. Reach out via WhatsApp, call us, or book a free assessment and we will answer everything in person.

Book Free AssessmentContact Us

title: Education Glossary | Improve ME Institute Dubai
description: Browse our A–Z education glossary covering GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, IB, MYP, and tutoring terms — helping Dubai families navigate the curriculum landscape.
html:
1. Home
  2. /Glossary

Glossary

Education Glossary

A–Z guide to exams, curricula, admissions, and tutoring terms — helping Dubai families navigate the education landscape.

Filter by category or search terms like "GCSE", "IB", or "differentiation".

Exams & Qualifications

#
General Certificate of Secondary Education. A UK qualification typically taken at age 16 (Year 11), covering a wide range of subjects. In Dubai, offered by exam boards such as AQA, Edexcel, and OCR.View our GCSE courses→
#
International General Certificate of Secondary Education. An internationally recognised alternative to GCSE, commonly used in Dubai and international schools. Offered by Cambridge and Edexcel International.View our IGCSE courses→
#
Advanced Level. UK qualifications taken at age 17–18 (Years 12–13), typically in three or four subjects. Required for university entry in the UK and recognised globally, including UAE universities.View our A-Level courses→
#
International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme. A two-year pre-university programme (Years 12–13) with six subjects, Theory of Knowledge (TOK), Extended Essay, and Creativity, Activity, Service (CAS). Widely offered in Dubai international schools.View our IB Diploma programme→
#
Abbreviation for IB Diploma Programme. See IB Diploma.View our IB Diploma programme→
#
Middle Years Programme. The International Baccalaureate curriculum for ages 11–16 (Years 7–11), designed to prepare students for the IB Diploma or other pathways.View our MYP programme→
#
Cognitive Abilities Test, 4th edition. A computer-adaptive assessment used by many Dubai schools for admissions and to identify learning potential. Tests verbal, non-verbal, quantitative, and spatial reasoning.View our CAT4 preparation course→
#
Practice examinations that mirror real exams in format and timing. Used to identify gaps, build exam technique, and predict likely grades before final assessments.
#
Previous years' exam papers from the relevant exam board. Essential for exam preparation, helping students understand question styles, timing, and mark scheme expectations.
#
One of the UK's largest exam boards, offering GCSE and A-Level qualifications across a wide range of subjects. AQA qualifications are recognised globally and are offered at a number of international schools in the UAE.
#
A UK exam board (part of Pearson) offering GCSE and A-Level qualifications taken at international schools across Dubai. Edexcel syllabuses are a popular alternative to Cambridge at many GEMS and other network schools.
#
One of the major UK exam boards offering GCSEs and A-Levels, particularly strong in sciences and humanities. Some international schools in Dubai follow OCR syllabuses alongside AQA and Edexcel.
#
Cambridge Assessment International Education — the exam board that administers Cambridge IGCSE, AS-Level, and A-Level qualifications internationally. CAIE qualifications are among the most widely recognised in Dubai's international schools and are accepted by universities worldwide.
#
The international version of the A-Level qualification, designed for students studying outside the UK. IALs follow the same standard as UK A-Levels and are offered by Edexcel/Pearson; they are widely accepted by universities globally.
#
The current GCSE grading system, replacing the old A*–G scale. Grade 9 is the highest achievable mark — broadly equivalent to a high A* — while Grade 4 is considered a standard pass and Grade 5 a strong pass.
#
A 9-point scoring scale used in CAT4 reports to show how a student's performance compares to their age group nationally. A stanine of 5 is average; scores of 7–9 indicate above-average cognitive ability in that area.
#
The instruction words used in exam questions that tell students exactly what kind of answer is expected — for example, describe, evaluate, or compare. Understanding command words is essential for exam technique, as using the wrong approach can cost marks even when the knowledge is there.
#
The official document used by examiners to award marks for each question in an exam. Studying mark schemes helps students understand exactly what is expected and is one of the most effective revision tools for improving exam performance.
#
A standardised test used primarily for admissions to undergraduate programmes at US universities, assessing reading, writing, and mathematics. Some students in Dubai take the SAT alongside or instead of A-Levels if they are targeting American universities.

Curriculum Frameworks

#
The curriculum framework used in England and Wales, divided into Key Stages (KS1–KS5). Many Dubai schools follow this or adapt it for an international context.
#
Key Stage 3. Covers Years 7–9 (ages 11–14) in the UK National Curriculum. Forms the foundation before GCSE/IGCSE and often includes broad subject coverage.View our KS3 courses→
#
See KS3. Covers Years 7–9 (ages 11–14) in the UK National Curriculum.View our KS3 courses→
#
Key Stage 4. Covers Years 10–11 (ages 14–16) when students typically study for GCSE or IGCSE qualifications.
#
The final stage of secondary education in the UK system, covering Years 12–13 (ages 16–18), commonly known as Sixth Form. Students typically study A-Levels or the IB Diploma during KS5 in preparation for university applications.View our Sixth Form courses→
#
The final two years of secondary school in the British system (Years 12–13, ages 16–18), during which students prepare for A-Levels or the IB Diploma. In Dubai, many British international schools use the term Sixth Form to refer to this stage.View our Sixth Form courses→
#
See Exams section. Also used to refer to the curriculum framework as a whole.View our IB Diploma programme→
#
Early Years Foundation Stage. The UK framework for children from birth to age 5, covering Nursery (FS1) and Reception (FS2). Focuses on play-based learning and developmental milestones.
#
The first year of early years education in the British curriculum, equivalent to Nursery (ages 3–4). FS1 focuses on play-based learning to develop early communication, social, and motor skills.
#
The second year of the Early Years Foundation Stage, equivalent to Reception (ages 4–5). FS2 is where children begin structured phonics, early maths, and literacy in preparation for Year 1.
#
KS1. Covers Years 1–2 (ages 5–7) in the UK National Curriculum. Lays foundations in literacy, numeracy, and science.
#
KS2. Covers Years 3–6 (ages 7–11) in the UK National Curriculum. Builds on KS1 and prepares students for secondary school.
#
The detailed syllabus document from an exam board (e.g. AQA, Edexcel, Cambridge) that defines exactly what will be examined and how. Essential for targeted revision.

Year Groups

#
The first year of early years education (ages 3–4), also known as FS1 in the British curriculum. Focus is on play-based learning, socialisation, and early communication skills.
#
The final year of EYFS (ages 4–5), also known as FS2. Children begin structured phonics, early numeracy, and school routines in preparation for Year 1.
#
The first year of Key Stage 1 (ages 5–6). Formal schooling begins with a core focus on reading, writing, and number skills.
#
The final year of Key Stage 1 (ages 6–7). Students may sit optional SATs assessments in English and Maths at the end of this year.
#
The first year of Key Stage 2 (ages 7–8). The curriculum broadens to include Science, History, Geography, and more alongside core literacy and numeracy.
#
The second year of Key Stage 2 (ages 8–9). The times tables check is typically taken this year to assess multiplication fluency.
#
The third year of Key Stage 2 (ages 9–10). Preparation begins to build toward end-of-primary assessments and the transition to secondary school.
#
The final year of primary school (ages 10–11). KS2 SATs are taken this year, and secondary school applications are typically in progress.
#
The first year of secondary school and Key Stage 3 (ages 11–12). New subjects are introduced and students adapt to a more independent learning environment.
#
The second year of Key Stage 3 (ages 12–13). Core and foundation subjects continue to develop in breadth and depth.
#
The final year of Key Stage 3 (ages 13–14). GCSE, IGCSE, or MYP subject choices are typically made this year, shaping the next two years of study.
#
The first year of the GCSE, IGCSE, or MYP programme (ages 14–15). Coursework begins and students work toward their first formal subject assessments.
#
The final GCSE or IGCSE year (ages 15–16). Main exam season takes place in May and June, and results determine Sixth Form options.
#
The first year of Sixth Form (ages 16–17). A-Level or IB Year 1 content begins, and students start preparing their UCAS university applications.
#
The final year of school (ages 17–18). A-Level or IB final exams are taken, and university applications are submitted through UCAS.

Subjects & IB Components

#
English as an Additional Language. Support for students whose first language is not English, common in Dubai's international school context.
#
Theory of Knowledge. A core IB Diploma subject exploring how we know what we claim to know. Assessed via an essay and an exhibition.View our IB Diploma programme→
#
See TOK. A core IB Diploma subject exploring how we know what we claim to know.View our IB Diploma programme→
#
A 4,000-word independent research essay required for the IB Diploma. Students choose a subject and topic, developing research and academic writing skills.View our IB Diploma programme→
#
Internal Assessment. Coursework component in IB and some GCSE/A-Level subjects, marked internally by the school and moderated externally. E.g. Biology IA, Mathematics IA.
#
Creativity, Activity, Service. A core requirement of the IB Diploma involving experiential learning outside the classroom. Students complete projects across these three areas.View our IB Diploma programme→
#
Higher Level. IB subjects can be taken at Higher Level (more depth and hours) or Standard Level (SL). Students typically take three HL and three SL subjects.
#
Standard Level. See HL. Standard Level subjects require fewer teaching hours and are often less demanding than Higher Level.
#
One of two IB Mathematics pathways (with Applications & Interpretation). More algebra and calculus focused, suited to students planning STEM or economics at university.
#
One of two IB Mathematics pathways (with Analysis & Approaches). More statistics and applied modelling, suited to students in social sciences or arts.
#
Ability to understand and reason using words and language. Tested in entrance exams such as 7+ and 11+, and in CAT4.
#
Ability to solve problems using shapes, patterns, and diagrams rather than words. Tested in school entrance exams and CAT4.

School & University Admissions

#
An assessment used by schools to select students for admission. Dubai schools often use 7+, 11+, or 13+ entrance exams, plus CAT4 or other standardised tests.
#
Entrance assessment for children moving into Year 3 (age 7). Typically tests maths, English, verbal reasoning, and non-verbal reasoning.View our 7+/11+ entrance prep→
#
Entrance assessment for children moving into Year 7 (age 11). Covers similar areas to 7+ but at a higher level; used by many selective Dubai schools.View our 7+/11+ entrance prep→
#
Combined term for primary and secondary school entrance assessments. 7+ is for Year 3 entry (age 7); 11+ is for Year 7 entry (age 11). Both typically assess maths, English, verbal reasoning, and non-verbal reasoning.View our 7+/11+ entrance prep→
#
A selective entry examination for students seeking admission to independent schools at Year 9 (age 13), common in the UK. Like the 7+ and 11+, preparation typically involves verbal reasoning, non-verbal reasoning, maths, and English assessments.View our entrance prep→
#
Shorthand for the 13+ entrance exam. See 13+ Entrance Exam.View our entrance prep→
#
A university or school offer that depends on achieving specified grades or requirements. Common for A-Level and IB students applying to UK universities.
#
When a student accepts a university place but postpones starting for a year (e.g. for a gap year). Often requires meeting conditions in the same exam session.
#
Universities and Colleges Admissions Service. The UK system for applying to undergraduate degrees. Dubai students applying to UK universities typically use UCAS.
#
Grades that a school predicts a student will achieve in final exams. Used for university applications (e.g. UCAS) before actual results are known.
#
A university offer with no further academic conditions. The student has met requirements and can take the place regardless of future exam results.
#
The Abu Dhabi Department of Education and Knowledge — the regulatory body overseeing schools in Abu Dhabi, equivalent to how KHDA regulates schools in Dubai. Families moving between the two emirates may encounter both bodies when enrolling their children.

Tutoring & Teaching Terms

#
Ongoing assessment during learning (e.g. quizzes, in-session checks) used to guide teaching and identify what students have grasped. Contrasts with summative assessment at the end of a unit or year.
#
Assessment at the end of a unit or course (e.g. end-of-term tests, GCSE exams) used to measure achievement and assign grades.
#
An assessment carried out at the start of tutoring or a new course to identify a student's specific strengths and gaps in knowledge. At IMI, diagnostic assessments are used to build a personalised learning plan for each student rather than taking a one-size-fits-all approach.Book a free diagnostic session→
#
Teaching technique where support is provided initially (e.g. worked examples, sentence starters) and gradually removed as the student gains independence.
#
Adapting teaching content, process, or product to meet different students' needs, levels, and learning styles within the same group or lesson.
#
Special Educational Needs and Disabilities. Support and adaptations for students who need additional help to access the curriculum. Common in UK-style and international schools.
#
Knowledge and Human Development Authority. Dubai's regulatory body for private education. KHDA-approved centres meet required standards for teaching and operations.
#
Extra teaching outside school, usually one-to-one or in small groups. In Dubai, often delivered at tutoring centres or in-home.
#
One-to-one tutoring delivered at a student's home by a private tutor. Unlike a tuition centre, home tuition takes place in a familiar environment but typically lacks the structured facilities and peer learning of a dedicated academic centre.
#
The organisation that sets and marks exams (e.g. AQA, Edexcel, OCR, Cambridge). Different boards have slightly different syllabuses and exam styles.
#
Identifying the difference between a student's current level and their target. Used to plan focused tutoring and prioritise areas for improvement.

Need more help?

Browse our FAQs for common questions, or contact us to book a free assessment and discuss your child's needs.

View FAQContact Us

OUR CURRICULUM

A full academic journey, ages 3 to 18

Personalised learning across every stage — from EYFS to IB Diploma

Book Free AssessmentView All Subjects

Trusted by families from Dubai College · Kings · JESS · Nord Anglia · GEMS Jumeirah Primary · 30+ schools

WHY IMPROVE ME

A structured system that gets children ahead of their peers

Improve ME does not just match tutors to curricula — we run a structured, proprietary approach to learning. From EYFS and Key Stage 1 through to Key Stage 2, GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, and IB Diploma, we teach ahead of school level where it helps, use booster sessions to build academic confidence early, and keep your child on one consistent journey from age 3 to 18 with no gaps.

Small groups, weekly sessions, reverse-engineering of concepts, practice papers, worksheets, and progress reports to parents are all part of the system. Our tutors know their exam boards and mark schemes, but the real differentiator is how we structure learning so children get ahead and stay ahead.

Beyond the curriculum

TESTIMONIALS

What Students and Parents Say

“

I truly struggled in MYP chemistry and was continuously scoring low marks, however after joining improve me my grade went from a three to a six as MYP is marked out of seven. The tutors always go above and beyond to make sure you understand the content being taught and always ensure you leave understanding everything that you went over during the session.”

WHY CHOOSE US

One System. One Provider. Age 3 to 18.

Most tutoring centres patch gaps. We build the whole journey.

Structured from the start

From EYFS phonics to IB extended essays — every stage follows a structured, sequenced approach designed to keep children ahead of their peers.

We teach ahead of school level

Students don't come to us to catch up. They come to stay ahead. Our curriculum runs slightly ahead of school so children arrive in class already familiar with the concept.

No gaps between stages

When a student moves from KS2 to KS3, or GCSE to A-Level, we already know them. There's no handover, no re-assessment, no starting over.

One centre, every stage

Primary, secondary, enrichment — all under one roof. Families who join us in EYFS are still with us at IB.

FOR DUBAI PARENTS

Sound Familiar?

These are the most common reasons Dubai parents come to us. If any of these resonate, a free assessment is the right first step.

We don't just catch up — we stretch

Many of our students aren't struggling. They're capable children whose parents want to make sure foundations are genuinely secure before the academic pace increases — whether that's at a British, IB, American, CBSE, or any other school in Dubai.

New to Dubai? We've done this before

Changing schools — and often changing curriculum entirely — is one of the most common reasons families come to us. We assess where your child actually is, regardless of what they were studying before arriving in Dubai, and build from there.

This isn't a homework club

We run our own structured programmes that go further than any single school curriculum. Sessions are planned, progressive, and designed to build genuine understanding — not just get through tonight's worksheet.

Homework help isn't the same as learning

Getting answers done and understanding the method are two very different things. We focus on the second — so your child can work independently and confidently, across whatever curriculum their school follows.

1,000+

students

Since 2010

Established in Dubai

4.8★

From 206 Google reviews

WHY IT MATTERS

Why Improve ME Matters in Primary School

What makes the difference in primary education — and how we deliver it.

We catch gaps before they grow

Primary school is where learning habits form — for better or worse. Our tutors identify exactly where your child is struggling and address it before it becomes a bigger problem at secondary level.

Small groups where every child is seen

From one-to-one at EYFS to small groups (average 2–3 students) at KS2, every session is sized so your child gets real attention. No lost questions, no waiting — just focused teaching at the right level.

We build on what school teaches — and go further

Our tutors work within the curriculum frameworks used across Dubai's leading schools, but sessions follow Improve ME's own structured programme — designed to stretch your child academically and develop well-rounded, confident learners, not just cover what's due next week in class.

Confidence, not just correct answers

We focus on how your child thinks, not just what they score. Students leave our sessions more willing to attempt problems, ask questions, and back themselves — skills that last well beyond primary school.

FAQ

Primary Curriculum — Frequently Asked Questions

About This Stage

We support children from age 3 through to age 11, covering EYFS, Key Stage 1, and Key Stage 2. Each stage has its own structured programme and appropriate group sizes.
We work with students across all major curricula taught in Dubai — British, IB (including PYP), American, CBSE, and others. Our programmes build deep foundational skills that transfer across any curriculum your child's school follows.
Yes. Improve ME Institute is KHDA approved and has been operating in Dubai since 2010, supporting over 1,000 students a year across primary and secondary levels.

Our Sessions

Group sizes vary by stage. EYFS sessions are always one-to-one — one tutor, one child. KS1 sessions are one teacher to two students. KS2 sessions are small groups (average 2–3 students). These ratios are not flexible — they are central to how we teach.
Most primary students attend once or twice a week. We recommend starting with one session and reviewing after the first month once we have a clearer picture of your child's needs and pace.
Yes. Many families use holiday periods to consolidate learning from the previous term or prepare for the term ahead. We run sessions throughout the year including summer.

Subjects & Curriculum

No. We run our own structured MaThesis and EngLit programmes that align with your child's school framework but go further. Sessions build genuine understanding that classroom time alone, in any school, rarely has space to develop fully.
Yes — this is one of the most common situations we support. Curriculum transitions create gaps that are not always immediately visible. We assess exactly where your child is, identify what needs attention, and build a programme around their specific situation.
Absolutely. Strong foundations travel with your child regardless of which school or country comes next. Many of our families have been with us across multiple postings precisely because consistent support makes transitions much smoother.

Progress & Results

Most parents notice a change in confidence within four to six weeks. Academic improvement typically becomes visible in school reports within a term. We track progress every session and share updates regularly so you are never left guessing.
We provide regular updates covering what your child has worked on, where improvement has happened, and what the focus is next. You will always know exactly how sessions are going.

Getting Started

We spend time with your child to understand their current level, identify any gaps, and get a sense of how they approach problems. It is relaxed and informal — not a test. Afterwards we share our findings and recommend a programme if we think we can help.
Common signs include losing confidence in a subject, rushing through homework to get it done, or performing well in class but struggling to explain their thinking independently. If any of these sound familiar, a free assessment is the right first step.

GET STARTED

Ready to Begin?

Book a free assessment and discover how we can support your child's learning journey.

  • Free initial assessment
  • Expert specialist tutors
Book Free AssessmentBack to Courses

The Journey

Your Child's Secondary Journey

Understanding the pathway from Year 7 to university

Secondary education in Dubai's British curriculum schools spans seven years and three distinct stages. Each stage builds on the last — and each one matters.

1

Key Stage 3

The first three years of secondary school. Your child builds the core knowledge, study habits, and independent thinking that everything else depends on. KS3 covers Mathematics, English, and Science across Years 7, 8, and 9.

Ages 11–14Years 7–9
Explore KS3
2

GCSE / IGCSE / MYP

The exam years. Your child sits formal qualifications across 8–10 subjects. GCSE (AQA, Edexcel, OCR), IGCSE (Cambridge, Edexcel International), or MYP (IB Middle Years Programme) — depending on their school. Results directly determine A-Level and IB entry.

Ages 14–16GCSEIGCSEMYP
GCSEIGCSEMYP
3

A-Level / IB Diploma

The final stage before university. Your child specialises in 3–4 subjects at A-Level or follows the broader IB Diploma programme. Grades at this stage determine university offers — including Oxbridge, Russell Group, and top international universities.

Ages 16–18A-LevelIB Diploma
A-LevelIB
1

Key Stage 3

The first three years of secondary school. Your child builds the core knowledge, study habits, and independent thinking that everything else depends on. KS3 covers Mathematics, English, and Science across Years 7, 8, and 9.

Ages 11–14Years 7–9

GCSE / IGCSE / MYP

The exam years. Your child sits formal qualifications across 8–10 subjects. GCSE (AQA, Edexcel, OCR), IGCSE (Cambridge, Edexcel International), or MYP (IB Middle Years Programme) — depending on their school. Results directly determine A-Level and IB entry.

Ages 14–16GCSEIGCSEMYP
GCSEIGCSEMYP

A-Level / IB Diploma

Qualifications

Choose Your Qualification Stage

Select your child's current stage to see subjects, exam boards, and how we prepare them

Ages 11–14 · Years 7–9

Key Stage 3

UK National Curriculum. Building foundations before GCSE with structured teaching across Maths, English, and Science.

View KS3 →
Ages 14–16 · Years 10–11

GCSE

AQA, Edexcel, OCR. Formal UK qualifications graded 9–1. Terminal exams across 8–10 subjects at the end of Year 11.

View GCSE →
Ages 14–16 · Years 10–11

Cambridge International, Edexcel International. The international equivalent of GCSE, used by Dubai's international schools. Graded A*–G or 9–1.

View IGCSE →

IB Middle Years Programme. Criterion-based assessment across eight subject groups. The pathway into the IB Diploma.

AQA, Edexcel, OCR, Cambridge International. 3–4 specialist subjects studied over two years. The standard route to UK universities.

Not Sure Which Stage Your Child Is At?

It's completely normal — especially if your child is new to the UK curriculum, changing schools, or you're comparing different qualification pathways. We'll help you work it out.

Book a free assessment and we'll identify exactly where your child stands, which qualification they're following, and where they need support.

Book Free Assessment

Subjects

Subjects We Cover

Specialist tutoring across every core secondary subject — from Year 7 through to A-Level and IB

Mathematics

From KS3 foundations to A-Level and IB calculus

English

Language, Literature, and exam writing across all levels

Physics

Conceptual understanding and mathematical rigour

Chemistry

Atomic structure to organic chemistry, all exam boards

Biology

Cells to ecosystems, with exam-style practice throughout

Business Studies

Entrepreneurship, finance, and operations

Economics

Micro, macro, and analytical skills for top grades

Psychology

Research methods, cognitive processes, and beyond

Teaching Approach

Our Teaching Approach

01

Small Groups

Maximum 6 students per class, with most sessions averaging 2–3. Your child gets the focused attention that large school classes can't provide.

02

Exam Board Expertise

AQA, Edexcel, OCR, Cambridge International, IB — we teach to the exact specification your child is being examined on. No generic content.

03

Data-Driven Progress

Regular diagnostic assessments and progress reports so you always know where your child stands and what we're working on next.

04

Structured Sessions

Every session follows a clear plan — topic coverage, exam-style practice, and targeted feedback. No wasted time, no improvised lessons.

Why Improve ME

Why Improve ME for Secondary?

KHDA-approved tutoring centre in Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai

16+ years preparing secondary students across every qualification level

40+ specialist subject tutors with proven track records

Small groups (a maximum of six students) for focused, personalised teaching

All major exam boards: AQA, Edexcel, OCR, Cambridge International, IB

Our own revision books and study materials built from real exam analysis

Data-driven progress reports aligned to your child's specification

Mock exam preparation matched to Dubai school calendars

Trusted by 1,000+ students

Testimonials

What Parents Say

“

FAQ

Secondary Curriculum — Frequently Asked Questions

We support secondary students aged 11–18, from Year 7 (Grade 6) through to Year 13 (Grade 12). This spans Key Stage 3, GCSE, IGCSE, MYP, A-Level, and IB Diploma.
We cover all major exam boards used in Dubai: AQA, Edexcel (UK and International), OCR, Cambridge International (CAIE), and the IB. Our tutors teach to the exact specification your child is being examined on — we never mix exam board content.
GCSEs are set by UK exam boards (AQA, Edexcel, OCR) and use a 9–1 grading scale. IGCSEs are set by Cambridge International or Edexcel International for schools outside the UK, and use either A*–G or 9–1 grading. Both are internationally recognised, but the syllabus content and exam structure differ. We tutor for both — with separate teaching for each.
Yes. We offer tutoring for the IB Middle Years Programme (MYP) at ages 14–16 and the IB Diploma Programme (SL and HL) at ages 16–18. Our tutors understand IB assessment criteria, internal assessments, and the specific demands of the IB approach.
Your child's school will determine which qualification pathway they follow. Most British curriculum schools in Dubai offer GCSE or IGCSE at ages 14–16, and A-Level at ages 16–18. IB schools offer MYP and the IB Diploma. If you're unsure, ask us — we're familiar with the qualifications offered by most British-style schools in Dubai.
At secondary level, we offer Mathematics, English, Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Business Studies, Economics, and Psychology. Subject availability varies by qualification stage — explore the individual stage pages for full details.
Our groups have a maximum of six students, with most sessions averaging 2–3. Students are grouped by subject, qualification, exam board, and level to ensure every session is directly relevant.
Yes. Many students attend Improve ME for two or three subjects. We'll start with a diagnostic assessment to identify priorities and build a plan that covers the subjects where support will have the greatest impact.
It's never too late to make a difference, but the sooner you start, the more we can achieve. Even a few weeks of targeted preparation — focused on high-impact topics and exam technique — can shift results. Book a free assessment and we'll be honest about what's realistic.

Curriculum & Assessment

EYFS has no formal tests — we focus on development and readiness

The EYFS framework does not use formal exams or national curriculum tests. Instead, practitioners observe children and assess their progress against the EYFS early learning goals. At Improve ME, we use similar approaches: we observe, note progress, and share updates with parents so you know how your child is developing in number, shape, phonics, and communication.

Our goal is to ensure your child is confident and ready for the transition to Key Stage 1 — where more structured learning begins in Year 1.

What Parents Say About Our EYFS Programme

"
"My daughter was shy and reluctant to try new things. After a few months at Improve ME she's so much more confident with numbers and letters. She's ready for Reception now."

Parent of EYFS student

"The play-based approach is perfect. My son doesn't even realise he's learning — he just has fun. But his phonics has come on so much."

Parent of EYFS student

"One-to-one sessions and patient tutors made all the difference. Our child went from avoiding 'school work' to asking to do more. Highly recommend."

Parent of EYFS student

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about EYFS at Improve ME

About This Stage

EYFS covers Nursery and Reception, typically ages 3 to 5. It is the foundation stage of the British curriculum framework and the starting point for formal education.
We offer Mathematics and English at EYFS level. Both subjects follow our structured programmes — MaThesis for Maths and EngLit for English — designed specifically for this age group.
Not at all. EYFS is precisely when foundational habits form. Our sessions are age-appropriate and play-informed while being structured — children engage naturally at this stage when the approach is right.

Our Sessions

Always one. EYFS sessions are one tutor, one child — no exceptions. At this age, that ratio is not a luxury, it is the only way to teach effectively.
Sessions are structured to match the concentration span of young children. We will confirm the session length that is right for your child during the free assessment.

Subjects & Curriculum

We align with the EYFS statutory framework but teach through our own MaThesis and EngLit programmes — designed to build deeper understanding and stretch your child beyond what nursery or reception covers alone. This applies whether your child attends a British, IB, or any other curriculum school.
Absolutely. Our programme deliberately bridges EYFS and KS1. Children who complete our EYFS programme enter Year 1 with stronger number sense, early literacy, and reasoning skills that set them apart from the very first week.

Getting Started

We spend relaxed, informal time with your child to understand where they are developmentally. It is observational — not a test. Afterwards we share what we found and recommend next steps.

NEXT STAGE

Years 3 to 6 — the bridge from primary to secondary

Key Stage 2 is where academic foundations become academic skills. In Lower KS2 (Years 3 and 4), children build the core knowledge and confidence they need — mastering times tables, developing as writers, and beginning to think like scientists. In Upper KS2 (Years 5 and 6), the focus shifts toward exam readiness — GL Assessments, 11+ preparation, and the deeper mathematical and analytical thinking that secondary school demands from day one. At Improve ME, every KS2 student is matched to the right level within their year group, not just their year group alone.

What is Key Stage 2?

Years 3 to 6 — the bridge from primary to secondary

Key Stage 2 is where academic foundations become academic skills. In Lower KS2 (Years 3 and 4), children build the core knowledge and confidence they need — mastering times tables, developing as writers, and beginning to think like scientists. In Upper KS2 (Years 5 and 6), the focus shifts toward exam readiness — GL Assessments, 11+ preparation, and the deeper mathematical and analytical thinking that secondary school demands from day one. At Improve ME, every KS2 student is matched to the right level within their year group, not just their year group alone.

Science

Living World · Materials & Matter · Forces & Energy

What Children Learn at Key Stage 2

Skills that prepare for GL Assessments, 11+, and secondary school

Mathematics

  • Times tables mastery (2s through 12s) — the foundation of all future maths
  • Place value to 10,000 — understanding big numbers with confidence
  • Addition and subtraction with regrouping — mental and written methods
  • Introduction to fractions — halves, quarters, thirds, and equivalent fractions
  • Multiplication and division — moving beyond tables to multi-step problems
  • Shape, perimeter, and area — practical geometry that connects to real life
  • Data handling — reading and creating charts, tables, and graphs
  • Problem-solving — word problems and reasoning that build mathematical thinking

English

  • Reading with understanding — moving from decoding to genuinely comprehending texts
  • Vocabulary development — expanding the words children use and understand
  • Sentence construction — writing varied, interesting sentences with correct punctuation
  • Paragraph writing — structuring ideas logically with a beginning, middle, and end
  • Creative writing — stories, descriptions, and imaginative tasks that build confidence
  • Grammar essentials — nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, conjunctions in context
  • Spelling rules and patterns — building towards secure independent spelling
  • GL Assessment awareness — familiarisation with assessment formats from Year 3

Science (Living World · Materials & Matter · Forces & Energy)

  • Living World: Plants and what they need to grow; animals and their habitats; food chains and what eats what; the human body — bones, muscles, and keeping healthy
  • Materials & Matter: Properties of everyday materials; solids, liquids, and gases; why we use different materials for different purposes; simple changes and investigations
  • Forces & Energy: Pushes, pulls, and magnets; light and shadows; sound and vibration; simple circuits and electricity basics

Our Teaching Approach for Primary

Structured, exam-aware, and focused on independence

01

Enough space for every child to be heard.

Familiarisation with question types, timing, and mark schemes.

03

Progress tracking

Clear targets and regular feedback so you know how your child is progressing.

04

Independence and resilience

We encourage problem-solving and self-checking so children are ready for secondary.

GL Assessment and 11+ preparation where needed

Small groups (average 2–3 students) for focused teaching

GL Assessments and readiness for secondary

At the end of Year 6, children in England (and many British curriculum schools in Dubai) sit GL Assessments in Reading, Mathematics, and Grammar, Punctuation and Spelling. Science is typically assessed by teacher assessment. These results help secondary schools understand each child's attainment and can inform setting and support in Year 7.

We support KS2 students with curriculum-aligned teaching and exam-style practice so they approach the tests with confidence. We also offer 11+ entrance exam preparation for families whose children are applying to selective schools.

KS2 covers Years 3 to 6, typically ages 7 to 11. It is divided into Lower KS2 (Years 3 and 4) and Upper KS2 (Years 5 and 6).

Yes. The core skills developed at KS2 — extended problem solving, analytical writing, scientific thinking — are required across all major curricula. Our programmes build these transferable skills regardless of your child's school framework.

Yes — this is one of the primary goals of our Upper KS2 programme. We focus on building the independent thinking, extended writing, and problem-solving skills that secondary school demands from day one.
KS2 sessions build the foundational skills that underpin entrance assessments. If your child is specifically preparing for 7+ or 11+ selective school entry, we also offer a dedicated Entrance Preparation programme — ask about this at your free assessment.
Yes. Science is available from KS2 onwards. Lower KS2 covers life processes, materials, and physical processes. Upper KS2 extends into more structured scientific thinking and experimental understanding. From GCSE onwards, Science splits into Physics, Chemistry, and Biology as separate subjects.

Progress & Results

Progress is tracked every session and parents receive regular written updates. At KS2 we also provide termly progress reviews so you always have a clear picture of where your child stands and what comes next.

Getting Started

SECONDARY LEARNING

Key Stage 3

Ages 11–14 (Years 7–9)

Key Stage 3 covers Years 7, 8, and 9 — also known as Grades 6, 7, and 8 in some Dubai schools. It's the bridge between the primary years and the exam years, sitting after Key Stage 2 and before GCSE, IGCSE, or MYP.

While our core framework follows the British Curriculum, the skills we build—inquiry, analysis, and structured writing—are universally essential for students transitioning to IB MYP or US curriculums.

Book Free Assessment →View Secondary Curriculum →

Trusted by families across Dubai since 2010 · KHDA-approved

PARENT GUIDE

What is Key Stage 3?

A parent's guide to your child's first three years of secondary school

Key Stage 3 covers Years 7, 8, and 9 — also known as Grades 6, 7, and 8 in some Dubai schools. It's the bridge between the primary years and the exam years, sitting after Key Stage 2 and before GCSE, IGCSE, or MYP.

While our core framework follows the British Curriculum, the skills we build—inquiry, analysis, and structured writing—are universally essential for students transitioning to IB MYP or US curriculums.

These are the years where your child builds the subject knowledge, study habits, and independent thinking that everything else depends on. What they learn now directly shapes how they perform at GCSE and the options available to them afterward.

For families in Dubai following the UK curriculum, KS3 is taught at schools like Dubai College, JESS, Kings', Repton, and Nord Anglia. Whether your child calls it Year 7 or Grade 6, the curriculum expectations are the same.

At Improve ME, we've supported KS3 students in Dubai since 2010. We know this stage inside out — the curriculum, the pressure points, and what it takes to arrive at GCSE ready to succeed.

Key Facts

Age: 11–12

Year 7 (Grade 6)

Age: 12–13

Year 8 (Grade 7)

Age: 13–14

Year 9 (Grade 8)

AT A GLANCE

KS3 at a Glance

Age: 11–12

First year of secondary — new school, new subjects, new expectations. Focus: Core foundations and settling in.

Age: 12–13

Content deepens — study skills become essential. Focus: Building depth and strong habits.

Age: 13–14

GCSE preparation begins — choosing options, bridging into exam-level content. Focus: Readiness and subject choices.

TRANSITION SUPPORT

The Year 7 Transition — What Parents Need to Know

Starting secondary school is one of the biggest changes your child will face

Your child may have just passed a competitive entrance exam, changed schools entirely, or simply moved from being the oldest in primary to the youngest in secondary. Either way, everything changes at once.

The workload increases. Subjects are taught by different specialist teachers. Homework expectations jump. Your child is expected to manage their own time, organise their materials, and adapt to a pace that's noticeably faster than primary school.

For many students — even strong ones — this can feel overwhelming. It's completely normal. And it's exactly why the right support during KS3 matters so much.

Academically,

students often arrive with gaps they don't realise they have. Primary school may have covered topics broadly, but secondary teachers expect mastery. Maths moves from arithmetic to algebra. English moves from comprehension to literary analysis. Science splits into three distinct disciplines.

Emotionally,

your child is adjusting to a new environment, new friendships, higher expectations, and the pressure of being assessed more formally and more frequently.

How We Support the Transition

Diagnostic Assessment

We start every student with a diagnostic assessment to understand exactly where they are and where the gaps lie.

Fill the Gaps

We address knowledge gaps from primary before they compound — so your child isn't playing catch-up all year.

Build Study Habits

We teach the organisational and revision skills that secondary school demands but rarely teaches.

Small, Safe Groups

With a maximum of six students and an average of 2–3, your child gets attention that's hard to find in a classroom of 25–30.

SUBJECTS

Subjects at Key Stage 3

Choose your subject to explore what we cover and how we prepare your child for GCSE and beyond.

Mathematics

Core skills and problem-solving foundations

View Course →

English

Reading, writing, and analysis skills

View Course →

Science

Biology, chemistry, and physics foundations

View Course →

YEAR BY YEAR

What Changes from Year 7 to Year 9

KS3 isn't three identical years — each one builds on the last

Foundation

Year 7: Finding Their Feet

Year 7: Finding Their Feet

Introduced to secondary-level content across Maths, English, and Science. Focus on core knowledge, basic analytical skills, and adjusting to the pace of secondary school.

Watch for:

Gaps from primary that cause problems as content builds. Students who were strong at KS2 can struggle if skills in fractions, sentence structure, or scientific vocabulary aren't secure.

Building Depth

Year 8: The Forgotten Year

Year 8: The Forgotten Year

Content gets significantly harder and study skills become essential. Often overlooked between the excitement of Year 7 and the urgency of Year 9.

Watch for:

A dip in motivation or results. Many students coast through Year 8 and arrive at Year 9 underprepared. This is the year to lock in strong habits.

Exam Readiness

Year 9: The Bridge to GCSE

Year 9: The Bridge to GCSE

Many Dubai schools begin covering GCSE-level content in Year 9. Students face one of their most important decisions: choosing GCSE or MYP options.

Watch for:

Subject choices that don't align with your child's strengths or plans. Homework volume and difficulty increase sharply.

GCSE GUIDANCE

Choosing GCSE and MYP Options — Getting It Right

One of the most important decisions your child makes during KS3

At most British curriculum schools in Dubai, students study a core of compulsory subjects at GCSE: Mathematics, English Language, English Literature, and the Sciences. Beyond that, they typically choose 3–4 additional subjects from options that may include Business Studies, Economics, Psychology, History, Geography, and others.

If your child follows the IB Middle Years Programme (MYP), the structure differs — but the same principles of informed subject selection apply.

Most schools ask students to select their options during Year 8 or early Year 9. The subjects your child chooses now directly affect their A-Level options, university applications, and career pathways.

What parents should consider

Strengths and interests.

GCSE subjects are demanding. Students perform better in subjects they enjoy and have a natural aptitude for.

Future pathways.

Some A-Level and university courses require specific GCSEs. A student considering A-Level Economics benefits from strong GCSE Maths. Medicine pathways typically require all three sciences.

Balance.

A mix of subjects — some analytical, some creative, some humanities-based — tends to keep options open and prevent burnout.

How we help with options decisions

We don't make the decision for you, but we're well placed to support it. We can assess your child's current performance across subjects, provide honest feedback on whether a subject is realistic at GCSE level, and support the transition once options are confirmed.

We offer GCSE and IGCSE tutoring across Mathematics, English, Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Business Studies, Economics, and Psychology.

View counselling Pathways & Future Skills
Explore GCSE CoursesExplore IGCSE CoursesExplore MYP Courses

METHODOLOGY

How We Prepare Your Child for What's Coming

KS3 doesn't have formal external exams — but exam preparation starts here

The schools your child attends are assessing them every term. The skills they need for GCSE begin developing now. Here's how we build exam readiness from Year 7 onward.

01

Diagnostic Assessments

We identify your child's specific gaps and strengths immediately, reassessing regularly to track progress rather than relying on guesswork.

02

Exam-Style Practice

Students learn to interpret command words and structure answers for maximum marks, moving to authentic past-paper questions by Year 9.

03

Revision Packs & Study Skills

We provide targeted revision materials and teach active recall techniques—essential study habits most students don't learn until it's too late.

04

Personalised Learning

Our small group model allows us to adapt to your child's needs, providing you with clear updates on exactly what we've covered.

EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

All exam boards covered: UK National, International Schools

UK National Curriculum

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

HOW WE TEACH

How we teach Key Stage 3

01

Small Groups

Maximum 6 students per class. Many groups average 2–3 for personalised attention.

02

Exam Technique

We teach mark schemes, command words, structure, timing, and common pitfalls to avoid.

03

Confidence & Consistency

Regular practice and feedback builds trust so your child enters each school term with confidence.

04

Progress Tracking

Clear targets and regular check-ins so students (and parents) know exactly what to work on next.

WHY IMPROVE ME

Why Improve ME for Key Stage 3?

KHDA-approved tutoring centre in Gold & Diamond Park

16+ years' experience teaching KS3 in Dubai

Small groups (a maximum of six students) for personalised support

Specialist subject tutors with proven track records

Structured, curriculum-aligned teaching across all exam boards

All exam boards covered: UK National, International Schools

Regular progress reports to track improvement

Flexible scheduling to fit your family's routine

Dedicated learning environment with easy access from Sheikh Zayed Road

MORE THAN ACADEMICS

More Than Academics — Supporting Your Child Through KS3

KS3 isn't just about what your child learns in the classroom. It's a period of real personal growth — and sometimes real pressure. Students are navigating new friendships, greater independence, higher expectations, and the growing awareness that their performance has long-term consequences.

A child who feels overwhelmed or unsupported won't perform at their best, no matter how good the teaching is. That's why our approach goes beyond content delivery.

A safe, small-group environment.

Your child isn't lost in a crowd. They can ask questions, make mistakes, and build confidence without the social pressure of a large classroom.

Tutors who know your child.

Because our groups are small and consistent, our tutors develop a genuine understanding of each student — their strengths, struggles, and what motivates them. This isn't drop-in tutoring. It's an ongoing relationship.

Building independence.

We don't do the work for your child. We teach them how to approach problems, manage their time, and take ownership of their learning — habits that carry them through GCSE, A-Level, and beyond.

Communication with parents.

If we notice a change in your child's attitude, effort, or performance, we'll let you know and work with you on a plan.

SUCCESS STORIES

"My son started Year 7 really struggling with the jump from primary. Within a term at Improve ME, his confidence came back and his grades followed."

Parent, Year 7 Student

Parent, Year 8 Student

KS3 is the foundation stage — Years 7 to 9. GCSE is the exam stage — Years 10 and 11. What your child learns at KS3 directly prepares them for GCSE-level content and exams.

We have 40+ tutors with specialist subject knowledge across Mathematics, English, and Science. All are experienced in teaching the UK curriculum at KS3 level and above.
Our sessions are structured around curriculum content, not homework help. However, because we align with your child's school scheme of work, what we cover often directly supports the homework they're set.
