EYFS Mathematics

Structured mathematics sessions for ages 3–5. We build early number confidence, shape recognition, and logical reasoning through our MaThesis programme — with one tutor per child, every session.

Book Free AssessmentView All Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

What Is EYFS Mathematics?

EYFS Mathematics develops secure early foundations in number, numerical patterns, shape, space, and measure. Children learn to count confidently, understand quantity, and apply simple calculations — while also recognising patterns and exploring spatial awareness.

The statutory framework places strong emphasis on building a secure grounding in number to support future mathematical success. At Improve ME, we align closely with the EYFS framework while extending learning through our structured MaThesis programme — ensuring children develop confidence, accuracy, and skills that go beyond classroom expectations.

Ages 3–5

Age range

Nursery / Foundation Stage 1 & 2

Stage

1 student per tutor — always

Group size

MaThesis programme

Programme

COURSE CONTENT

What Your Child Learns

Four areas of focus across every EYFS Mathematics session.

Building early number confidence

Number & Arithmetic

  • •Number sequencing up to 50 — recognising, ordering, and finding missing numbers
  • •Mental addition and subtraction using counting on and counting back
  • •Introductory multiplication through skip counting and doubling
  • •Early division through sharing and halving activities
  • •Simple word problems using addition or subtraction

Exploring the physical world mathematically

Shapes & Measures

  • •Recognising 2D shapes and understanding their basic properties
  • •Fine motor development through tracing numbers and shapes with precision
  • •Size and measurement concepts — big/small, long/short, heavy/light
  • •Reading and labelling a clock with simple time

Making sense of information

Data Handling & Comparison

  • •Sorting and classifying objects into clear categories
  • •Comparing numbers using greater than, less than, more, fewer, most, least
  • •Interpreting simple picture graphs, bar graphs, and data tables

Thinking carefully, not just calculating

Reasoning & Logic

  • •Identifying the odd one out and spotting differences
  • •Recognising and completing patterns and sequences
  • •Solving simple 4×4 Sudoku puzzles using shapes and numbers
  • •Non-verbal reasoning tasks that build analytical confidence

Logical thinking at EYFS is developed through mathematical games and structured activities. Children learn to observe closely, think systematically, and apply problem-solving strategies — skills that build the foundation for CAT4 preparation in later years.

ASSESSMENT

How We Track Progress

Before sessions
Baseline Assessment

Before sessions begin we assess your child to understand exactly where they are — what's forming, what's secure, and where to start. At EYFS this is observational and gentle, not a formal test.

Every session
Session Monitoring

Progress is tracked every session. We note what your child has grasped and what needs more time, so learning always moves forward at the right pace.

Regular updates
Parent Updates

Parents receive regular updates on what their child has learned, where confidence has grown, and what comes next. At this age, the tutor-parent relationship is especially important — and we take it seriously.

TEACHING APPROACH

How We Teach It

01

One-to-One Sessions

Every EYFS Mathematics session is one tutor, one child. At this age that ratio isn't a luxury — it's the only way to teach effectively. Your child gets complete attention, and the tutor can respond to every moment of confusion or curiosity immediately.

02

Written Practice from the Start

We emphasise written work and correct method from the very first session. Children learn not just to get the right answer, but to record their thinking accurately — a habit that pays dividends all the way through primary school.

03

Structured Progression

Our MaThesis programme sequences every topic deliberately. Sessions don't follow whatever happened to come up in school that week — they follow a structured path designed to build genuine understanding from the ground up.

04

Progress Tracking

We track what your child has covered and how confidently they've grasped it. Parents receive honest, specific updates — not vague reassurances.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME?

One tutor per child — complete focus, no gaps missed
MaThesis programme provides structured, sequenced progression
Written practice and correct method from day one
Reasoning and logic activities lay the groundwork for CAT4 preparation
Sessions bridge EYFS and KS1 so your child enters Year 1 genuinely ready
Regular parent updates — honest, specific, and timely
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Mathematics at Improve ME

About This Stage

Our EYFS Mathematics programme covers four areas: Number and Arithmetic, Shapes and Measures, Data Handling and Comparison, and Reasoning and Logic. All four are taught through our structured MaThesis programme.
Yes. The foundational maths skills we build at EYFS — number sense, pattern recognition, early reasoning — are universal and directly relevant regardless of which curriculum your child's school follows.

Our Sessions

Always one. One tutor, one child — no exceptions at EYFS level.
Sessions are structured and teacher-led with a strong emphasis on written practice and correct method. Activities are age-appropriate and engaging — children at this age learn best through a combination of structured tasks and guided exploration.

Subjects & Curriculum

We align with the EYFS statutory framework but teach through our MaThesis programme — which goes further and builds deeper understanding than classroom coverage alone. This applies whether your child attends a British, IB, American, or any other curriculum school in Dubai.
Yes. The reasoning and logic component of our EYFS programme is specifically designed to build the analytical thinking skills that underpin CAT4 performance in later years.

Progress & Results

Progress tracking at EYFS is observational and session-by-session. We note what concepts are forming, what is secure, and what needs more time. Parents receive regular updates in plain, honest language — not educational jargon.

Getting Started

We spend relaxed, informal time with your child — observing how they approach numbers, shapes, and simple problems. It is not a test. Afterwards we share what we found and explain exactly how we would structure sessions.
PROGRESSION

What Comes Next

The best thing you can do for your child's long-term academic success is get the foundations right. A child who leaves EYFS with strong number sense, early literacy, and a love of learning is set up for everything that follows — not just Year 1, but the whole of primary school and beyond.

Help your child build a love of reading and writing from the very start. Our EYFS English programme develops phonics, handwriting, comprehension, and early creative writing through personalised sessions designed for ages 3–5.

Book Free AssessmentView All English Courses
OVERVIEW

What is EYFS English?

In the EYFS framework, English falls under the Literacy area of learning. Literacy has two core dimensions: reading (word reading and comprehension) and writing (transcription and composition). Reading begins with phonics and builds towards blending sounds into words and understanding simple stories. Writing starts with letter formation and develops into spelling simple words and writing short sentences. At Improve ME, our EngLit programme aligns with the EYFS framework while extending your child's learning — introducing reading schemes, creative writing, and spelling skills that prepare them for Year 1.

EYFS
Qualification
Ages 3-5

Ages 3-5

Clear, age-appropriate learning with real progress you can see.

  • •Learning letter sounds and Jolly Phonics (Sets 1–7)
  • •Blending and segmenting, word families
  • •Spelling high-frequency words and CVC words
  • •Correct formation of uppercase A–Z and lowercase a–z

Reading & Comprehension

  • •Understanding stories through pictures
  • •Picture-based comprehension and retrieval questions
  • •Introduction to Oxford Reading Series and decodable stories
  • •Vocabulary building and true/false exercises

Writing & Creative

  • •Capital letters, full stops, and question marks
  • •Introduction to nouns, verbs, and adjectives
  • •Sentence formation and creative writing from prompts
  • •Story elements: characters and settings

How We Teach It

01

Each session adapts to your child's reading level and pace. Your child's tutor identifies which letter sounds they know, where they need support, and builds from there.

02

Phonics-Led Approach

We follow the Jolly Phonics programme, teaching letter sounds systematically so your child can blend sounds into words and begin reading independently.

03

Structured Reading

From picture comprehension at age 3 to decodable stories and the Oxford Reading Series at age 4–5, your child progresses through carefully graded reading materials.

04

Building Confidence

When your child reads their first word, writes their first sentence, or earns their first EngLit certificate, those moments build a lifelong love of literacy.

WHY CHOOSE US

Key Stage 1 Mathematics

Structured mathematics sessions for ages 5–7. We build core number confidence, early geometry, and logical reasoning through our MaThesis programme — with a maximum of two students per tutor.

Book Free AssessmentView All Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

What Is Key Stage 1 Mathematics?

Key Stage 1 Mathematics establishes the critical skills children need to progress through primary school and beyond. At this stage, children move from early number recognition to applying arithmetic confidently, exploring shape and space, and beginning to interpret data.

At Improve ME, we follow our structured MaThesis programme alongside the KS1 national curriculum — ensuring your child builds genuine understanding, not just procedural answers. Sessions run with a maximum of two students per tutor, so every misconception gets caught and corrected early.

Ages 5–7

Age range

Year 1 and Year 2

Stage

Max 2 students per tutor

Group size

MaThesis programme

Programme

COURSE CONTENT

What Your Child Learns

Three areas of focus across every KS1 Mathematics session.

Core calculation skills

Number & Arithmetic

  • •Number sequencing and place value up to 100
  • •Addition and subtraction using mental and written methods
  • •Multiplication through skip counting, doubling, and repeated addition
  • •Division through sharing, grouping, and halving
  • •One and two-step word problems

Understanding space and measurement

Shapes & Geometry

  • •Properties of 2D and 3D shapes
  • •Symmetry and basic spatial reasoning
  • •Measuring length, mass, capacity, and temperature in standard units
  • •Reading time to the hour and half hour on analogue and digital clocks
  • •Positional and directional language

Interpreting information and thinking logically

Data & Reasoning

  • •Sorting and classifying using one or more criteria
  • •Comparing and ordering numbers — greater than, less than, equal to
  • •Reading pictograms, tally charts, and block diagrams
  • •Identifying patterns, sequences, and the odd one out
  • •Introduction to 4×4 Sudoku and structured logic puzzles
ASSESSMENT

How We Track Progress

At the start
Baseline Assessment

We assess your child at the start to identify exactly where they are in relation to the KS1 curriculum — what's secure, what's emerging, and what needs immediate attention.

Every session
Session Monitoring

Progress is tracked session by session. We note which concepts have been grasped and which need revisiting so no gap goes unaddressed.

Regular updates
Parent Updates

Parents receive regular updates on what their child has covered, what's improved, and what the next focus areas are. Clear, honest progress reporting — no surprises.

TEACHING APPROACH

How We Teach It

01

Small Groups

Every KS1 session has a maximum of two students. This isn't just a selling point — it fundamentally changes what's possible. Your child can't hide behind others, and neither can gaps.

02

Written Practice from the Start

We teach children to work accurately and systematically from the very beginning. Written practice and correct method are emphasised at every session — because habits formed at KS1 shape how children approach maths for years.

03

Structured Progression

Our MaThesis programme sequences learning deliberately. Each session builds on the last, so your child is always moving forward — not repeating what they already know or jumping ahead before foundations are secure.

04

Progress Tracking

We track exactly what your child has covered and how confidently they've grasped it. Regular updates keep parents informed, and our tutors adjust the pace based on what each child needs — not what a class average suggests.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME?

Maximum two students per tutor — your child is always seen and heard
MaThesis programme ensures structured, sequenced progression through KS1
Tutors identify and close gaps before they carry into KS2
Written practice and correct method from day one — building habits that last
Regular parent updates so you always know where your child stands
Bridging content available to prepare children for KS2 and beyond
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Mathematics at Improve ME

About This Stage

Our KS1 Mathematics programme covers three areas: Number and Arithmetic, Shapes and Geometry, and Data and Reasoning. All three follow our structured MaThesis programme.
Year 2 does include statutory assessments in Maths and English in British curriculum schools. However, our focus is never narrowly on assessment preparation — we build genuine understanding that means your child is naturally ready when assessments come.

Our Sessions

A maximum of two students per tutor. Most sessions run with one or two children.
We focus on making the most of session time. We may occasionally suggest consolidation activities between sessions, but we do not set formal homework — the session itself is where the learning happens.

Subjects & Curriculum

We align with KS1 curriculum frameworks across British, IB, American, and CBSE schools but teach through our own MaThesis programme. Sessions support school learning while building deeper understanding that classroom time alone rarely has space to develop.
We stretch as well as support. If your child is ahead, sessions focus on deepening understanding, introducing higher-order reasoning, and building the habits — accuracy, method, systematic thinking — that matter as much as raw ability.

Progress & Results

Most parents notice a change in confidence within four to six weeks. We track progress every session and provide regular parent updates so you always know where your child stands.

Getting Started

As early as possible. KS1 is where arithmetic habits form. Gaps that appear small at Year 1 become significantly harder to address by Year 3 or 4.
PROGRESSION

What Comes Next

KS1 is where habits form. Children who leave Year 2 with secure arithmetic, confident reading, and a willingness to attempt new problems have a fundamentally different experience at KS2 than those who arrive with gaps. Getting this stage right is one of the most valuable investments a parent can make.

html:
1. Home

KS3 Mathematics — Maths Tutoring in Dubai for Years 7–9

KS3 is the bridge between primary arithmetic and the abstract thinking that GCSE requires. For students in Years 7–9 at Dubai's British and international schools, these three years determine whether GCSE Maths feels achievable — or like catch-up from day one. At Improve ME Institute, we treat KS3 Maths as the most important investment a family can make before the exam years begin.

Book Free AssessmentView All Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

The 'Year 7 Dip' — Why So Many Students Struggle

Many students who were confident in primary school hit a wall in Year 7. The shift from number-based problems to abstract algebra and ratio is sudden, and school classrooms cannot pause for every student who needs more time. The most common sticking points are fractions and percentages, algebraic manipulation, and ratio word problems. None of these are signs of low ability. They are signs of a gap that was never properly addressed.

Key Stage 3
Qualification
Ages 11-14
Age range
UK National Curriculum
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

What We Cover Across Years 7–9

Strong foundations across the key strands before GCSE.

Year 7

  • •Number (fractions, decimals, percentages, indices)
  • •Algebra (expressions, linear equations, sequences)
  • •Geometry (angles, area, volume)
  • •Plugging primary gaps, introducing formal algebraic notation

Year 8

  • •Proportional reasoning
  • •Complex 2D/3D geometry
  • •Compound measures, transformations
  • •Pythagoras' Theorem

Year 9

  • •GCSE-style thinking
  • •Multi-step problem solving
  • •Higher-level algebra (double brackets, simultaneous equations)
  • •Data and statistics, probability
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Assessment is built around topic mastery, regular testing, and exam-style practice where applicable.

School 1
Topic Tests
  • •Curriculum-aligned topics
  • •Structured revision plan
  • •Timed practice + feedback
School 2
Topic Tests
  • •Curriculum-aligned topics
  • •Structured revision plan
  • •Timed practice + feedback
School 3
Coursework / Practical
  • •Skills building
  • •Confidence and consistency
  • •Bridging to the next level
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

UK National Curriculum
School Schemes
Transition to GCSE

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our Key Stage 3 Mathematics Teaching Approach

01

The MaThesis Programme

MaThesis is Improve ME's in-house mathematics programme. At KS3, it includes diagnostic booklets that pinpoint exactly where a student's foundation gap is, topic trackers showing what has been mastered and what comes next, and scientific calculator workshops to build fluency before Year 10.

02

A Misconception Worth Addressing

Many parents treat Years 7 and 8 as low-stakes. In reality, roughly half the GCSE Maths syllabus is content first introduced at KS3. A student who arrives in Year 10 with shaky fractions or algebra spends two years playing catch-up instead of perfecting exam technique.

03

When to Start

Late October / Early November: First secondary school reports arrive. January: Post-mock window — fix foundations before Year 9 options. May / June: Pre-teach next year's algebra over summer.

Sessions are scheduled in blocks that match the Key Stage 3 academic year. We cover the full specification or curriculum in a logical order, with built-in review and past-paper practice where applicable. You receive regular progress updates so you can see how your child is improving.
Our tutors are subject specialists with experience teaching in Dubai and internationally. They use our in-house lesson plans and materials, so every student benefits from the same high standard of teaching regardless of which tutor they work with.

KS3 English — English Tutoring in Dubai for Years 7–9

English is not simply a school subject — it is a set of skills. At KS3, the skills your child builds in reading comprehension, analysis, and writing will determine how far they can go at GCSE and beyond. At Improve ME Institute, we teach KS3 English in small groups (maximum 6, average 2–3) using our EngLit programme, with structured resources, weekly written feedback, and a safe environment where students are encouraged to test ideas and develop their own analytical voice.

OVERVIEW

Why Students Struggle at KS3 English

The most common gap is analytical writing. Students can broadly understand a poem or extract but cannot interrogate it. GCSE exam boards reward original, out-of-the-box thinking at the highest grade bands — and that thinking must be developed well before Year 10. Many students are also held back by a fear of being wrong. In English, any interpretation supported by evidence is valid.

KS3 Science — Science Tutoring in Dubai for Years 7–9

The transition from primary science to KS3 is a significant step. Students who once learned general science as one subject now face three distinct disciplines — Biology, Chemistry, and Physics — each with its own vocabulary, methods, and ways of thinking. At Improve ME Institute, our KS3 Science programme builds the deep understanding and scientific literacy that makes GCSE feel like a natural next step.

Book Free AssessmentView All Science Courses
OVERVIEW

Topics Covered Across Years 7–9

Biology: cells and organisation, human systems, ecosystems and food webs, reproduction, evolution and inheritance, health and lifestyle. Chemistry: particles and states of matter, atoms/elements/compounds, reactions and energy, the periodic table, acids and bases, materials and properties. Physics: forces and motion, energy and energy transfer, electricity and magnetism, waves and light/sound, thermal physics, gravity and space concepts. Year 7: foundational scientific ideas. Year 8: more complex applications. Year 9: bridges directly to GCSE readiness.

Key Stage 3
Qualification
Ages 11-14
Age range
UK National Curriculum
Exam boards

Biology

  • •Reproduction and inheritance (intro)
  • •Ecology and environment
  • •Health and disease basics
  • •Data interpretation in biology

SECONDARY — AGES 14–16

GCSE

Ages 14–16 (Years 10–11)

GCSE stands for General Certificate of Secondary Education. These are the formal qualifications your child sits at the end of Year 11, typically across 8–10 subjects. In the UK grading system, GCSEs are scored from 9 (highest) to 1 (lowest), with a grade 4 considered a standard pass and a grade 7 equivalent to the old A grade.

For families in Dubai following the British curriculum, GCSEs are the qualifications taken at schools like Dubai College, JESS, Kings' Schools, Repton, and Nord Anglia. They are the UK national standard — recognised by universities and employers worldwide.

Book Free Assessment →View Secondary Curriculum →

Trusted by families across Dubai since 2010 · KHDA-approved

PARENT GUIDE

What is GCSE? A Parent's Guide

Everything you need to know about your child's most important exams so far

GCSE stands for General Certificate of Secondary Education. These are the formal qualifications your child sits at the end of Year 11, typically across 8–10 subjects. In the UK grading system, GCSEs are scored from 9 (highest) to 1 (lowest), with a grade 4 considered a standard pass and a grade 7 equivalent to the old A grade.

For families in Dubai following the British curriculum, GCSEs are the qualifications taken at schools like Dubai College, JESS, Kings' Schools, Repton, and Nord Anglia. They are the UK national standard — recognised by universities and employers worldwide.

The most significant thing parents need to understand about today's GCSEs is this: coursework has been almost entirely replaced by terminal exams. Everything your child has learnt over two years comes down to their performance in the exam hall at the end of Year 11. There is no safety net of ongoing assessment to fall back on.

That's why preparation isn't something that starts in the final term. It starts the moment your child begins Year 10 — and at Improve ME, we've been preparing Dubai students since 2010 for when that moment arrives.

Key Facts

Age: 14–15

Year 10 (Grade 9)

Age: 15–16

Year 11 (Grade 10)

Typically 15–25 individual exam papers across all subjects

Exam Season (May–June)

AT A GLANCE

GCSE at a Glance

Age: 14–15

GCSE courses begin — content is taught from day one. Internal assessments and early topic tests. Focus: Building subject knowledge and exam foundations.

Age: 15–16

Mock exams in autumn/spring term. Intensive revision period before final exams. Focus: Exam technique, revision, and peak performance.

Typically 15–25 individual exam papers across all subjects

Results released in August. Grades determine A-Level and post-16 pathways. Focus: Execution — everything comes together here.

WHY IT MATTERS

Why GCSE Results Matter More Than Ever

GCSE grades are no longer just a stepping stone. They are the grades that sixth forms, colleges, and universities look at first. A-Level courses typically require a minimum of grade 6 or 7 in relevant GCSEs. Competitive sixth forms set their own entry thresholds. And many university courses consider GCSE results alongside A-Level predictions during the application process.

For students in Dubai's British curriculum schools, GCSE performance also determines which subjects they can continue at A-Level — and which doors remain open for medicine, engineering, law, business, and other competitive pathways.

The good news: with the right preparation, structure, and guidance, strong results are entirely achievable. That's what we do.

SUBJECTS

GCSE Subjects at Improve ME

Specialist tutoring across every major GCSE subject — taught by tutors who know each syllabus inside out

Mathematics

Build fluency across number, algebra, geometry, ratio, and statistics. Covers both foundation and higher tier.

View Course →

View Course →

View Course →

From cell biology to ecology — structured coverage of every topic with exam-style practice from day one.

View Course →

Atomic structure, bonding, organic chemistry, and quantitative skills. We make the abstract concrete.

View Course →

Physics

View Course →

Business Studies

Entrepreneurship, marketing, finance, and operations. Real-world business concepts brought to life for the exam hall.

View Course →

Economics

Micro and macroeconomics — supply and demand, market structures, fiscal and monetary policy. Analytical skills that examiners reward.

View Course →

Psychology

Research methods, memory, social influence, and psychopathology. Evidence-based answers that hit every mark scheme point.

View Course →

METHODOLOGY

How We Get Your Child Exam-Ready

A structured, data-driven approach built on our GCSE results in Dubai since 2010

We don't leave exam results to chance. Every session, every resource, and every conversation with your child is designed to move them closer to the grades they need. Our lessons are structured around the grade bands that matter most — Grades 7, 8, and 9 at GCSE, and A and A* at IGCSE. Here's how.

01

Personalised Learning Plans

No two students arrive with the same gaps. Before your child attends a single session, we run a comprehensive diagnostic assessment that maps their knowledge against the full GCSE specification. We identify exactly which topics are secure, which need reinforcement, and which haven't been understood at all. From there, we build a learning plan that targets the areas where marks are being lost — not a generic programme that covers everything at the same pace.

As your child progresses, we adjust. If they master a topic, we move on. If something isn't clicking, we address it from a different angle. This is what personalised learning actually looks like — not a marketing claim, but a working plan that changes as your child does.

02

Syllabus Mastery & Exam-Board Expertise

Our tutors don't just teach the subject — they teach the exam. We work with the AQA, Edexcel, and OCR specifications daily, and we know the difference between them. We know which topics carry the most marks, which question types catch students out, and exactly how examiners allocate grades.

Your child's tutor can walk through a mark scheme with their eyes closed. They know the command words. They know the common mistakes. They know where students drop marks unnecessarily — and they train your child to stop making those errors.

We've also developed our own revision books and study materials, tailored to the specific exam boards and subjects we teach. These aren't off-the-shelf resources. They're built from years of exam analysis, student feedback, and the patterns we see repeated in every exam series.

03

Mock Exam Preparation

We know when your child's school mocks are happening. We know because we've been tracking the mock exam calendars of Dubai's British curriculum schools for over a decade. Our programme is built around these dates — not around an arbitrary schedule.

In the weeks leading up to mocks, we shift into exam-simulation mode. Your child sits full papers under timed conditions, receives detailed feedback against the mark scheme, and works through their weakest areas before the real mock arrives. The result: your child walks into their mock confident, prepared, and familiar with the pressure of exam conditions.

Mock results matter. They set predicted grades, determine set changes, and shape teacher expectations. We treat them with the same seriousness as the final exams.

04

Progress Reports & Data-Driven Tracking

You'll never have to guess how your child is performing. We issue regular progress reports that show exactly where your child stands across every topic in their specification. You'll see which areas have improved, which are still being worked on, and what the plan is for the sessions ahead.

Our tracking isn't just for parents — we use it to drive our teaching. When data shows a student consistently losing marks on a particular question type, we adjust. When a topic clicks and scores improve, we bank it and move on. This is what data-driven tutoring means: every decision backed by evidence, not guesswork.

EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

Whichever board your child's school uses, we've got it covered

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

MOCK EXAMS

Mock Exams and the Road to Results

We plan around the milestones that actually matter

GCSE preparation isn't a straight line — it's a series of critical checkpoints. We build our programme around each one, so your child arrives at every milestone ready.

1

September to December (Building Foundations)

The first term of each GCSE year is about building secure topic knowledge. We work alongside your child's school scheme of work, reinforcing what's being taught in class and filling any gaps that emerge. Regular topic tests track progress.

2

January to March (Mock Exam Preparation)

Mock exams are the first real test of exam readiness. We prepare your child specifically for their school's mock papers — timed practice, mark scheme analysis, and exam technique under pressure. Mock results set predicted grades and reveal exactly where to focus revision.

3

April to June (Final Revision & Exam Season)

The final stretch. We switch to intensive revision mode: targeted topic review, past paper practice, and examiner-focused technique work. Your child enters the exam hall having already practised under the exact conditions they'll face.

The final stretch. We switch to intensive revision mode: targeted topic review, past paper practice, and examiner-focused technique work. Your child enters the exam hall having already practised under the exact conditions they'll face.

HOW WE TEACH

How we teach GCSE

Small Groups

A maximum of six students per class, with most sessions averaging 2–3. Every student gets the attention they need — no one falls through the cracks.

02

Exam Technique

We teach how examiners think. Mark schemes, command words, timing, structure — your child learns to turn what they know into the marks they deserve.

03

Confidence & Consistency

Weekly sessions build momentum. Regular testing builds familiarity. By the time exams arrive, your child has done this hundreds of times before.

04

Progress Tracking

Data-driven reports show exactly where your child stands. No surprises — just clear information and a plan for what comes next.

WHY IMPROVE ME

Why Improve ME for GCSE?

KHDA-approved tutoring centre in Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai

16+ years preparing GCSE students for top grades in Dubai

Specialist subject tutors who know AQA, Edexcel, and OCR inside out

Small groups (a maximum of six students) for focused, personalised teaching

Our own revision books and study materials — built from real exam analysis

Data-driven progress reports so you always know where your child stands

SUCCESS STORIES

FAQ

GCSE — Frequently Asked Questions

GCSEs are typically sat in Year 11, when students are 15–16 years old. The two-year GCSE course runs from Year 10 to Year 11. In the UAE grade system, this corresponds to Grades 9 and 10.
GCSEs use a 9–1 grading scale, with 9 being the highest. A grade 4 is considered a standard pass, and a grade 7 is equivalent to the old A grade. The top grade of 9 is designed to differentiate the very highest-performing students.
GCSEs follow UK national exam boards (AQA, Edexcel, OCR) and are the standard qualification in British curriculum schools. IGCSEs are the international version, set by Cambridge International or Edexcel International, and are designed for schools outside the UK. Both are widely recognised, but the syllabus content and exam structure differ. We tutor for both.
Most students take 8–10 GCSE subjects. Core subjects (Maths, English, and Science) are compulsory, with 3–4 additional options chosen during Year 9. The exact number depends on your child's school.
Most Dubai schools schedule mock exams in the autumn or spring term of Year 11, typically between November and February. Some schools also run Year 10 mocks. We track mock dates across Dubai's major British schools and prepare your child accordingly.
In almost all subjects, coursework has been replaced by terminal exams at the end of Year 11. This means your child's entire grade depends on their exam performance. A small number of subjects retain a practical or spoken language component, but the vast majority is exam-assessed.
We cover AQA, Edexcel, and OCR — the three main UK exam boards used by British curriculum schools in Dubai. Our tutors know each board's specification, question styles, and mark schemes in detail.
Our groups have a maximum of six students, with most sessions averaging 2–3. Students are grouped by subject, exam board, and ability level to ensure every session is relevant and productive.
It's never too late to make a difference — but the sooner you start, the more we can achieve. We'll run a diagnostic assessment to identify the highest-impact areas and focus your child's time where it matters most. Even a few weeks of targeted preparation can shift mock results.
We are based at Suite 3016-3017, Building 3, Gold and Diamond Park, Dubai. Many of our GCSE students come from nearby schools including Dubai College, GEMS Wellington and Jumeirah College.
Yes — we serve GCSE students from across Dubai. Families from JLT, Arabian Ranches, Dubai Marina and JBR regularly attend our centre, including students from Repton, Horizon and NAS.

SECONDARY — AGES 14–16

IGCSE

Ages 14–16 (Years 10–11)

IGCSE stands for International General Certificate of Secondary Education. It's the international equivalent of the GCSE, designed specifically for schools outside the UK. In Dubai, the IGCSE is the dominant qualification across international schools that follow British-style curricula — from established institutions to the newest additions to the city's education landscape.

IGCSEs are set by two main exam boards: Cambridge International (also known as CAIE or CIE) and Edexcel International (part of Pearson). While both assess the same broad subject areas, they differ in syllabus content, exam structure, and how marks are awarded. Knowing which board your child's school uses — and understanding the specifics of their papers — is essential.

Trusted by families across Dubai since 2010 · KHDA-approved

PARENT GUIDE

What is IGCSE? A Parent's Guide

Understanding the qualification that most Dubai international schools use

IGCSE stands for International General Certificate of Secondary Education. It's the international equivalent of the GCSE, designed specifically for schools outside the UK. In Dubai, the IGCSE is the dominant qualification across international schools that follow British-style curricula — from established institutions to the newest additions to the city's education landscape.

IGCSEs are set by two main exam boards: Cambridge International (also known as CAIE or CIE) and Edexcel International (part of Pearson). While both assess the same broad subject areas, they differ in syllabus content, exam structure, and how marks are awarded. Knowing which board your child's school uses — and understanding the specifics of their papers — is essential.

Like GCSEs, IGCSEs are largely assessed through terminal exams at the end of the course. However, some IGCSE subjects offer a coursework or practical assessment component, depending on the exam board and subject. Your child's school will determine which assessment route they follow.

Key Facts

Cambridge & Edexcel

Two main exam boards. We teach to the exact specification your child is sitting.

Grades A*–G or 9–1

Cambridge uses A*–G; Edexcel International uses 9–1. Both recognised worldwide.

Core & Extended Tiers

Some subjects have Core (C–G) and Extended (A*–E). We help you choose the right tier.

AT A GLANCE

IGCSE at a Glance

Cambridge & Edexcel

Two main exam boards. We teach to the exact specification your child is sitting.

Grades A*–G or 9–1

Cambridge uses A*–G; Edexcel International uses 9–1. Both recognised worldwide.

Core & Extended Tiers

Some subjects have Core (C–G) and Extended (A*–E). We help you choose the right tier.

WHY IT MATTERS

Why IGCSE Results Matter in Dubai

IGCSE results carry real weight. They determine which A-Level or IB subjects your child can pursue, and competitive sixth form programmes set clear grade thresholds for entry. Universities increasingly consider IGCSE results as part of the admissions picture — particularly for early applicants and those targeting selective programmes.

In Dubai's competitive school environment, strong IGCSE performance also impacts set placement, scholarship eligibility, and the breadth of subject choices available at post-16 level. Students who perform well at IGCSE carry that momentum into A-Level or IB — and arrive at university applications with a track record that admissions teams can trust.

We've seen it since we opened in 2010: the students who take IGCSE preparation seriously are the ones who keep their options open. And keeping options open is exactly what the IGCSE years should achieve.

SUBJECTS

IGCSE Subjects at Improve ME

Every subject taught by tutors who know your child's exact exam board, specification, and paper structure

Mathematics

Number, algebra, geometry, and statistics across core and extended tiers. We know where Cambridge and Edexcel papers differ — and teach accordingly.

Learn More →

English Language

Reading comprehension, directed writing, and composition. We teach the specific skills each paper tests — from summary techniques to persuasive writing under exam conditions.

Learn More →

Biology

Cell biology to human physiology to ecology. Structured topic coverage with past paper practice built into every session.

Learn More →

Chemistry

From atomic structure to organic chemistry — taught with the precision and depth that top IGCSE grades demand.

Learn More →

Physics

Motion, energy, waves, electricity, and nuclear physics. We build conceptual understanding alongside the mathematical skills your child needs for the exam.

Learn More →

Business Studies

Learn More →

Economics

Learn More →

Psychology

Learn More →

METHODOLOGY

How We Deliver Top IGCSE Grades

Precision teaching for international exams — built on our results in Dubai since 2010

We know the IGCSE landscape in Dubai better than anyone. We know which schools use Cambridge, which use Edexcel International, and exactly how their exam structures differ. Our lessons are structured around the grade bands that matter most — Grades 7, 8, and 9 at GCSE, and A and A* at IGCSE. That depth of knowledge shapes everything we do.

01

Board-Specific Teaching from Day One

The first thing we establish is which exam board and specification your child is following. It sounds obvious, but it matters more than most parents realise. A Cambridge IGCSE Maths paper tests different content at different weightings to an Edexcel International paper. The question styles are different. The mark schemes reward different things.

Every session your child attends at Improve ME is aligned to their exact specification. We use past papers from their board, we reference their mark schemes, and we teach the specific exam techniques that their papers require. There is no generic IGCSE tutoring here.

02

Our Own Study Materials

We've invested years in developing our own revision books and topic resources — written specifically for IGCSE exam boards. These materials are built from detailed analysis of past papers, examiner reports, and the patterns we see across every exam series. They're structured to match the order topics are taught in Dubai schools and designed to be used alongside our sessions.

Your child won't be working from photocopied textbook pages. They'll have materials that are purpose-built, regularly updated, and aligned to the exact exam they're sitting.

03

Data-Driven Progress Tracking

We track your child's performance across every topic in their specification — not in general terms, but against the specific assessment objectives their exam board uses. When we see a pattern of lost marks in a particular area, we address it immediately. When progress is clear, we move on.

You'll receive regular progress reports that give you a transparent view of where your child stands. These reports don't just say "doing well" or "needs improvement." They tell you which topics are secure, which are being worked on, and what the plan is for the weeks ahead.

04

Mock Preparation & Exam Simulation

We prepare your child for their school's mock exams with the same intensity we bring to the final papers. That means full past paper practice under timed conditions, detailed mark scheme analysis, and targeted follow-up on areas where marks were lost.

We also build exam resilience. IGCSE students in Dubai typically sit 15–25 individual papers across their subjects in a compressed exam window. That's a significant physical and mental demand. We prepare your child not just for individual papers, but for the sustained performance that exam season requires.

EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

Deep expertise across both major international exam boards

Cambridge International (CAIE)Edexcel International (Pearson)

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

COMPARISON

How Does IGCSE Differ from GCSE?

This is the question we hear most from parents in Dubai. Here's the short answer: both are rigorous, internationally recognised qualifications — but they are not the same exam.

GCSE

  • •Set by UK boards: AQA, Edexcel (UK), OCR
  • •Designed for schools in the UK
  • •Grades 9–1
  • •UK-centric content and context in some subjects
  • •Almost entirely exam-assessed

IGCSE

  • •Set by Cambridge International or Edexcel International
  • •Designed for international schools worldwide
  • •Grades A*–G (Cambridge) or 9–1 (Edexcel International)
  • •Internationally contextualised content
  • •Mostly exam-assessed, with some coursework options

The key point for parents:

your child's tutor needs to know which qualification they're actually sitting. A tutor preparing a student for Cambridge IGCSE Biology is working with a different specification, different past papers, and different mark scheme expectations than one preparing for AQA GCSE Biology. At Improve ME, we don't blur this distinction. We teach to the exact specification your child is being examined on.

HOW WE TEACH

How we teach IGCSE

01

Small Groups

Maximum 6 students per class, with most averaging 2–3. Grouped by subject, exam board, and tier — so every minute of every session is relevant to your child's exam.

02

Specification-Led Teaching

Every session is mapped to your child's exact exam board specification. We don't teach generic content — we teach what's on the paper.

03

Exam Technique & Past Papers

Regular practice with real past papers from your child's board. We teach mark allocation, timing, and the specific answering strategies each paper rewards.

04

Progress Reports

Data-driven reports that show exactly which topics are secure and which need work. You'll always know where your child stands and what we're doing about it.

WHY IMPROVE ME

Why Improve ME for IGCSE?

KHDA-approved tutoring centre in Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai

16+ years supporting IGCSE students across Dubai's international schools

Deep expertise in both Cambridge International and Edexcel International specifications

Small groups (a maximum of six students) for focused, personalised attention

Our own revision books and study materials — built from real IGCSE exam analysis

Data-driven progress reports aligned to exam board assessment objectives

Mock exam preparation matched to your child's school calendar

Honest guidance on Core vs Extended tier placement

Trusted by 1,000+ students

SUCCESS STORIES

FAQ

IGCSE — Frequently Asked Questions

GCSEs are set by UK exam boards (AQA, Edexcel UK, OCR) for schools in the UK. IGCSEs are set by Cambridge International or Edexcel International for schools outside the UK. Both are rigorous and internationally recognised, but the syllabus content, exam structure, and grading scales differ. We tutor for both — and we teach to the exact specification your child is sitting.
Most Dubai international schools use either Cambridge International (CAIE) or Edexcel International. Your child's school will confirm which board they're registered with. If you're unsure, ask us — we're familiar with the exam boards used by most British-style schools in Dubai.
Some Cambridge IGCSE subjects offer two tiers. The Core curriculum covers grades C to G, while the Extended curriculum covers grades A* to E. If your child is targeting top grades, they need to be on the Extended paper. We can assess your child against both tiers and advise honestly on which pathway is right.
Yes. IGCSEs are recognised by universities worldwide, including in the UK, US, and across Europe and Asia. They carry the same weight as GCSEs in university admissions processes.
Most students take 7–10 IGCSE subjects. The exact number depends on your child's school. Core subjects typically include Maths, English, and Science, with additional options in humanities, business, and languages.
The main IGCSE exam session runs from May to June. Cambridge International also offers a November exam session, which some Dubai schools use. Results are typically released in August (May/June session) or January (November session).
Yes. Our tutors are experienced with both boards and teach to each specification separately. We never mix exam board content — every session is specific to the papers your child will sit.
It's never too late to improve, but earlier is always better. Even in the final months before exams, targeted preparation focused on high-impact areas can make a meaningful difference to grades. We'll assess your child's position and focus our time where it will count most.

IB Middle Years Programme

The IB Middle Years Programme (MYP) is a challenging 5-year framework for students aged 11–16, designed by the International Baccalaureate. It bridges the IB Primary Years Programme (PYP) and the IB Diploma Programme (DP), developing internationally minded, critical thinkers.

MYP goes beyond memorisation — it focuses on inquiry-based learning, interdisciplinary thinking, and real-world problem solving. Students are assessed through internal tasks, a Personal Project, and eAssessments.

PARENT GUIDE

What is MYP? A Parent's Guide

Understanding the IB Middle Years Programme and how we support it

The IB Middle Years Programme (MYP) is a challenging 5-year framework for students aged 11–16, designed by the International Baccalaureate. It bridges the IB Primary Years Programme (PYP) and the IB Diploma Programme (DP), developing internationally minded, critical thinkers.

MYP goes beyond memorisation — it focuses on inquiry-based learning, interdisciplinary thinking, and real-world problem solving. Students are assessed through internal tasks, a Personal Project, and eAssessments.

At Improve ME, our tutors understand the MYP framework deeply — from subject-specific criteria to the ATL (Approaches to Learning) skills that underpin MYP success. We target criterion scores of 6 and 7 across all assessed criteria.

MYP Mathematics

Build confidence and achieve strong results in MYP Mathematics with expert support.

Book Free AssessmentView All Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is MYP Mathematics?

MYP Mathematics is assessed against four criteria — Knowing & Understanding, Investigating Patterns, Communicating, and Applying Mathematics in Real-Life Contexts — each marked out of 8. Unlike traditional exam-based curricula, MYP rewards mathematical reasoning, investigation skills, and the ability to connect maths to real-world situations. Students in MYP Years 4 and 5 may also sit the MYP eAssessment, which is an externally moderated on-screen examination. At Improve ME, our MYP Maths tutors are trained in the IB framework and understand exactly how criterion-based marking works — preparing students to score 7s and 8s across all four criteria, not just get the right answer.

MYP
Qualification
Ages 11-16
Age range
IB Middle Years Programme
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

MYP Mathematics Course Content

Inquiry-led coverage with criteria-based success strategies and progress to DP.

Number, Algebra & Functions

  • •Natural numbers, integers, rational and irrational numbers, real number system
  • •Prime factorisation, HCF, LCM, powers and roots
  • •Algebraic manipulation — expanding, factorising, simplifying expressions
  • •Linear equations and inequalities — solving and graphing
  • •Quadratic equations — factorising, completing the square, quadratic formula
  • •Linear and quadratic functions — domain, range, intercepts, graphing
  • •Simultaneous equations — substitution and elimination methods
  • •Sequences — arithmetic and geometric, nth term, sum formulas
  • •Exponential functions and their real-world applications
  • •Logarithms — laws, solving logarithmic equations (MYP Year 5)

Geometry, Statistics & Probability

  • •Coordinate geometry — distance, midpoint, gradient, equation of a line
  • •Trigonometry — right-angled triangles, sine and cosine rules, angles of elevation/depression
  • •Circle theorems and arc/sector calculations
  • •Mensuration — area and volume of 2D and 3D shapes, composite shapes
  • •Transformation geometry — reflection, rotation, translation, enlargement
  • •Descriptive statistics — mean, median, mode, range, standard deviation
  • •Data representation — histograms, box plots, cumulative frequency curves
  • •Probability — single events, combined events, tree diagrams, Venn diagrams
  • •Bivariate statistics — scatter graphs, lines of best fit, correlation (MYP Year 5)
  • •Statistical investigation cycle — formulating questions, collecting data, analysing, concluding

MYP Assessment Criteria in Detail

  • •Criterion A: Knowing & Understanding — applying mathematical knowledge to solve problems with and without a calculator; selecting appropriate methods; showing complete and correct working
  • •Criterion B: Investigating Patterns — selecting and applying strategies to identify mathematical patterns; generalising rules using mathematical language; justifying and proving generalisations
  • •Criterion C: Communicating — using mathematical language, notation, diagrams, and representations correctly; organising information logically; presenting clear and coherent mathematical arguments
  • •Criterion D: Applying in Real-Life Contexts — identifying the relevant mathematics in a real-world problem; applying mathematics to find a valid solution; justifying the degree of accuracy; reflecting on the reasonableness of results
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

We support criteria-based success with clear expectations, task planning, and consistent feedback aligned to MYP objectives.

Internal
Criteria A–D (0–8 each)
  • •Each criterion marked 0–8 by the classroom teacher
  • •Internal assessments include investigations, problem-solving tasks, and real-life application tasks
  • •Schools submit samples to IB for external moderation to ensure consistency
eAssessment
Year 5 On-Screen Examination
  • •2 on-screen papers — Paper 1 (no GDC), Paper 2 (GDC allowed) — externally moderated by IB
  • •eAssessment counts as 80% of the final grade for Year 5 students who sit it
  • •IA portfolio counts for remaining 20%
Criteria
What Each Criterion Tests
  • •A: Knowing & Understanding — applying knowledge, selecting methods, showing complete working
  • •B: Investigating Patterns — identifying patterns, generalising rules, justifying conclusions
  • •C: Communicating — correct notation, logical organisation, clear mathematical arguments
  • •D: Applying in Real-Life Contexts — identifying relevant maths, finding valid solutions, reflecting on reasonableness
EXAM BOARDS

Assessment Framework

MYP Criteria
Inquiry Cycles

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our MYP Mathematics Teaching Approach

01

Criterion-Targeted Practice

Every session is mapped to one or more MYP criteria. Students practice the exact task types that appear in school assessments — investigations for Criterion B, real-life problems for Criterion D — so marks are never lost to unfamiliarity with the format.

02

Mathematical Investigation Coaching

We train students to select strategies, identify patterns, form generalisations, and justify conclusions — the specific skills Criterion B rewards that most traditional tutoring ignores.

03

eAssessment Preparation

For MYP Year 4 and 5 students, we run timed eAssessment-style mock papers under exam conditions with detailed feedback aligned to IB grade boundaries.

04

Communication & Notation

We require students to use correct mathematical notation and present working clearly in every session — directly building the Criterion C marks that students consistently underperform in.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for MYP Mathematics?

MYP Mathematics specialists who understand criterion-based marking, not just content delivery
Covers all MYP Year 1–5 content across Number, Algebra, Geometry, Statistics, and Probability
Dedicated eAssessment preparation for MYP Year 4 and 5 students
Criterion B investigation coaching — the skill most students lose marks on
Aligned with all IB World School Maths syllabuses used in Dubai
Regular criterion-by-criterion progress reports shared with parents
Smooth bridge to IB Diploma Mathematics AA and AI
Personal Project quantitative support available
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Mathematics at Improve ME

The MYP eAssessment is an optional externally moderated on-screen examination available to Year 5 students. It consists of two papers — Paper 1 (no GDC) and Paper 2 (GDC allowed) — and covers the full MYP Mathematics curriculum. Schools can choose whether to enter students for the eAssessment. Where taken, it counts for 80% of the final grade with the school's internal portfolio making up the remaining 20%.
MYP Maths is criterion-based rather than exam-based. Instead of a single final exam, students are assessed continuously through tasks aligned to four criteria — Knowing & Understanding, Investigating Patterns, Communicating, and Applying in Real-Life Contexts. This means students must develop a broader range of mathematical skills, not just exam technique.
Yes — Criterion B is one of the most commonly underperforming criteria because it requires a different skill set to standard problem-solving. We specifically train students to select strategies, identify and extend patterns, form general rules using mathematical language, and justify their conclusions. This is a learnable skill and our students consistently improve their Criterion B marks.
We support students in MYP Years 1 through 5, covering the full age range from approximately 11 to 16. Our sessions are tailored to the specific content and assessment expectations of each year group, with additional focus on eAssessment preparation for Year 4 and 5 students.
Strong MYP Maths performance is the best preparation for IB Diploma Mathematics. We ensure students not only achieve strong MYP grades but also build the foundational skills — algebraic fluency, logical reasoning, and mathematical communication — that IB Diploma AA and AI both demand. We also advise families on the most appropriate Diploma Maths pathway for their child.

MYP English

Build confidence and achieve strong results in MYP English with expert support.

Book Free AssessmentView All English Courses
OVERVIEW

What is MYP English?

MYP Language & Literature develops students' ability to analyse texts critically, construct well-structured arguments, and communicate ideas with clarity and precision across a range of text types. It is assessed against four criteria — Analysing, Organising, Producing Text, and Using Language — each marked out of 8. The course covers literary texts (prose, poetry, drama) and non-literary texts (media, speeches, opinion pieces), requiring students to engage with both analytical and creative writing. In Dubai's IB World Schools, MYP Language & Literature is one of the most assessment-intensive subjects, with multiple written and oral tasks per year. At Improve ME, our tutors help students master the IB-specific analytical frameworks and essay structures that consistently earn top criterion scores.

MYP
Qualification
Ages 11-16
Age range
IB Middle Years Programme
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

MYP English Course Content

Inquiry-led coverage with criteria-based success strategies and progress to DP.

Text Analysis & Literary Study

  • •Prose fiction — analysing narrative voice, characterisation, setting, theme, and structure
  • •Poetry — identifying and analysing poetic devices, tone, imagery, rhythm, and form
  • •Drama — understanding stage directions, dialogue, conflict, character development, and dramatic techniques
  • •Non-fiction and media texts — speeches, opinion articles, advertisements, news reports, visual texts
  • •Author's choices — how writers use language, structure, and form to create meaning and effect
  • •Context and purpose — understanding how social, cultural, and historical context shapes a text
  • •Comparative analysis — identifying similarities and differences between two or more texts
  • •Intertextuality — recognising references and connections between texts
  • •Close reading techniques — annotating, identifying evidence, building analytical arguments from the text

Writing, Production & Creative Tasks

  • •Formal analytical essay writing — introduction, body paragraphs with topic sentences, evidence, analysis, conclusion
  • •Persuasive writing — speeches, opinion pieces, letters to the editor — using rhetorical devices effectively
  • •Creative writing — short stories, descriptive writing, monologues — with stylistic awareness and craft
  • •Comparative essays — structuring and sustaining a comparison across two texts
  • •Reflective writing — personal responses, reader journals, literary commentary
  • •Adapting register and tone for different audiences and purposes
  • •Writing with precision — vocabulary choice, sentence structure variation, punctuation for effect
  • •Drafting, editing, and improving written work — the process of refinement

MYP Assessment Criteria in Detail

  • •Criterion A: Analysing — identifying and explaining how language, structure, technique, and style create meaning; supporting analysis with well-chosen textual evidence; evaluating the effects of the writer's choices
  • •Criterion B: Organising — structuring responses logically and coherently; using organisational features appropriate to the text type; maintaining a consistent and appropriate register throughout
  • •Criterion C: Producing Text — selecting an appropriate text type and style; using language and stylistic features effectively for the intended audience and purpose; demonstrating creativity and authenticity
  • •Criterion D: Using Language — using correct grammar, syntax, punctuation, and spelling; using varied and precise vocabulary; demonstrating control and accuracy of written or spoken language
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

We support criteria-based success with clear expectations, task planning, and consistent feedback aligned to MYP objectives.

Written
Analytical & Creative Tasks
  • •Written analytical essays, comparative essays, and creative writing tasks assessed against four criteria
  • •Criterion A (Analysing), B (Organising), C (Producing Text), D (Using Language) — each marked 0–8
  • •Exact task types vary by school and year group; all map to the same MYP criteria
Oral
Individual Oral Assessments
  • •Formal individual oral presentations form part of MYP Language & Literature
  • •Assessed on argument, structure, and language precision
  • •We prepare students from topic selection and argument planning through to delivery
Criteria
What Each Criterion Tests
  • •A: Analysing — how language, structure, technique, and style create meaning; textual evidence; evaluating effects
  • •B: Organising — logical structure, appropriate text type, consistent register
  • •C: Producing Text — appropriate style, effective language, creativity and authenticity
  • •D: Using Language — grammar, punctuation, spelling; varied vocabulary; control and accuracy
EXAM BOARDS

Assessment Framework

MYP Criteria
Inquiry Cycles

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our MYP English Teaching Approach

01

Analytical Framework Training

We teach students a structured approach to text analysis — identifying the technique, explaining the effect, and linking to theme or purpose — that applies across every text type and directly maps to Criterion A marks.

02

Essay Architecture

Every student learns to structure analytical and comparative essays with clear topic sentences, embedded evidence, and developed analysis. We review and mark drafts exactly as an IB teacher would, with criterion-specific feedback.

03

Oral Task Preparation

MYP Language & Literature includes formal oral assessments. We prepare students for individual oral tasks — from topic selection and argument planning to delivery and language precision — building confidence alongside marks.

04

Creative Writing Coaching

For Criterion C tasks, we develop students' ability to make deliberate stylistic choices — voice, tone, structure, vocabulary — rather than writing instinctively, significantly improving their production marks.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for MYP English?

MYP Language & Literature specialists covering all four assessment criteria
Written and oral assessment preparation included
Literary and non-literary text analysis coaching
Essay structure and drafting support with criterion-specific feedback
Creative writing coaching for Criterion C production tasks
Covers all text types tested in Dubai IB World School assessments
Regular criterion-by-criterion progress tracking
Preparation for the step up to IB Diploma Language & Literature or Language & Language Acquisition
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about English at Improve ME

MYP Language & Literature assessments typically include written analytical essays, comparative essays, creative writing tasks, and individual oral presentations. The exact task types vary by school and year group, but all are assessed against the four MYP criteria — Analysing, Organising, Producing Text, and Using Language.
This is very common in MYP Language & Literature. Strong general writing ability does not automatically translate into high MYP criterion marks because the IB rewards specific skills — textual analysis with embedded evidence, criterion-specific essay structures, and deliberate language choices. Our tutors teach students exactly what each criterion requires and how to demonstrate it consistently.
Yes — Criterion C (Producing Text) covers creative writing tasks including short stories, monologues, speeches, and descriptive pieces. We help students make deliberate stylistic choices that demonstrate creative skill and awareness of audience and purpose, which is what the IB is specifically looking for.
Absolutely. Oral assessments are a significant part of MYP Language & Literature and many students find them daunting. We prepare students for individual oral tasks from start to finish — topic selection, argument planning, scripting or note preparation, and delivery practice — until they feel genuinely confident.
Strong MYP Language & Literature performance builds the analytical, writing, and oral communication skills that IB Diploma Language & Literature (Group 1) directly builds on. Students who master criterion-based analytical writing in MYP transition into the Diploma Programme with significantly stronger foundations for Paper 1, Paper 2, and the Individual Oral.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /MYP
  4. /Sciences

MYP Sciences

Build confidence and achieve strong results in MYP Sciences with expert support.

Book Free AssessmentView All Sciences Courses
OVERVIEW

What is MYP Sciences?

MYP Sciences covers Biology, Chemistry, and Physics within an integrated, inquiry-based framework.

Students build scientific knowledge while also learning to design investigations, process data, and reflect on the role science plays in society — skills that go far beyond memorising facts.

The subject is assessed across four criteria: Knowing & Understanding, Inquiring & Designing, Processing & Evaluating, and Reflecting on the Impact of Science. Each is marked out of 8.

In MYP Years 1–3, sciences are typically taught as combined science. From Years 4–5, many Dubai IB schools separate Biology, Chemistry, and Physics. Year 5 students can also sit the Sciences eAssessment, which is externally moderated by the IB.

At Improve ME, our tutors cover all three disciplines and are trained in the full MYP assessment framework — preparing students for both school-based tasks and the eAssessment.

MYP
Qualification
Ages 11-16
Age range
IB Middle Years Programme
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

MYP Sciences Course Content

Our sessions cover the full MYP Sciences syllabus across Biology, Chemistry, and Physics, structured around all four assessment criteria and designed to prepare students for school assessments and the Year 5 eAssessment.

Biology Topics

  • •Cell biology — prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, cell structure and function, cell division (mitosis and meiosis)
  • •Biological molecules — carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, enzymes — structure and function
  • •Human body systems — digestion, respiration, circulation, excretion, nervous system, hormonal system
  • •Genetics — DNA structure, protein synthesis, inheritance, Punnett squares, genetic diseases
  • •Ecosystems and ecology — food webs, nutrient cycles, biodiversity, human impact on ecosystems
  • •Evolution and natural selection — evidence for evolution, adaptations, speciation
  • •Plant biology — photosynthesis, transpiration, plant reproduction, plant hormones
  • •Microorganisms and disease — bacteria, viruses, immune response, antibiotics

Chemistry Topics

  • •Atomic structure — protons, neutrons, electrons, atomic number, mass number, isotopes
  • •The Periodic Table — periods, groups, trends in properties, alkali metals, halogens, noble gases
  • •Chemical bonding — ionic, covalent, and metallic bonding; properties of compounds
  • •Chemical reactions — types of reactions, balancing equations, rates of reaction, catalysts
  • •Acids, bases, and salts — pH scale, neutralisation, preparation of salts
  • •The mole and stoichiometry — molar mass, mole calculations, limiting reagents
  • •Organic chemistry introduction — hydrocarbons, functional groups, polymers
  • •Electrochemistry — electrolysis, half equations, industrial applications
  • •Energetics — exothermic and endothermic reactions, enthalpy, Hess's Law (Year 5)

Physics Topics & MYP Assessment Criteria

  • •Forces and motion — speed, velocity, acceleration, Newton's Laws, momentum, work and energy
  • •Energy — forms of energy, conservation of energy, power, efficiency, renewable and non-renewable sources
  • •Waves — properties of waves, sound, light, reflection, refraction, electromagnetic spectrum
  • •Electricity and magnetism — circuits, Ohm's Law, series and parallel circuits, electromagnetic induction
  • •Space physics — Solar System, stars, galaxies, Big Bang theory
  • •MYP Criterion A: Knowing & Understanding — recalling and applying scientific knowledge to solve problems
  • •MYP Criterion B: Inquiring & Designing — formulating research questions, hypotheses, and safe, ethical investigation designs with controlled variables
  • •MYP Criterion C: Processing & Evaluating — recording and processing data, identifying uncertainties, evaluating results and the validity of the investigation
  • •MYP Criterion D: Reflecting on the Impact of Science — discussing how science and its applications interact with society, the environment, and ethics
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

We support criteria-based success with clear expectations, task planning, and consistent feedback aligned to MYP objectives.

Internal
Criteria A–D (0–8 each)
  • •Criterion A: Knowing & Understanding — recalling and applying scientific knowledge
  • •Criterion B: Inquiring & Designing — research questions, hypotheses, variables, safe and ethical method
  • •Criterion C: Processing & Evaluating — recording data, processing, uncertainties, evaluating results and validity
  • •Criterion D: Reflecting on the Impact of Science — science and society, environment, ethics
eAssessment
Year 5 On-Screen Examination
  • •Optional MYP Sciences eAssessment for Year 5 — externally moderated by IB
  • •Assesses all four criteria through knowledge questions, data response tasks, and investigation analysis
  • •Where taken, forms 80% of final grade; school internal portfolio 20%
School
Investigations & Portfolio
  • •Internal assessments include practical investigations and data-response tasks
  • •Schools submit samples for external moderation
  • •We focus on Criterion B design and Criterion C processing — where most marks are lost
EXAM BOARDS

Assessment Framework

MYP Criteria
Inquiry Cycles

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our MYP Sciences Teaching Approach

01

Criterion B Investigation Design

Most students lose marks on Criterion B because designing a valid scientific investigation is a specific skill that schools rarely teach explicitly.

We train students to write precise research questions, form testable hypotheses, identify all three variable types, and plan investigations that meet IB safety and ethics requirements.

02

Data Processing Coaching

Criterion C requires students to process data correctly, identify uncertainties, present results graphically, and evaluate both their results and their methodology.

We work through this systematically — so students know exactly what to write at each stage and can tackle any data set with confidence.

03

Conceptual Science Teaching

Rather than drilling facts for recall, we teach the underlying scientific concepts so students can apply their knowledge to unfamiliar situations.

This is exactly what Criterion A exam questions demand — and it builds the understanding students need for IB Diploma Sciences.

04

eAssessment Preparation

For MYP Year 4 and 5 students, we prepare specifically for the Sciences eAssessment.

Sessions include timed practice papers, data response questions, and criterion-specific feedback so students know where their marks are going.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for MYP Sciences?

Covers all three MYP sciences — Biology, Chemistry, and Physics — in one centre
Specialist tutors for each science discipline available for Years 4–5 separate sciences
All four MYP assessment criteria covered in every session
Dedicated Criterion B investigation design coaching
Criterion C data processing and evaluation training
MYP Year 5 eAssessment preparation
Aligned with all Dubai IB World School science syllabuses
Strong preparation for IB Diploma Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, Physics HL and SL)
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Sciences at Improve ME

Yes — Improve ME offers MYP science tutoring across all three disciplines. For MYP Years 1–3 where science is taught as combined science, we cover all three areas. For Years 4–5 where schools typically separate the sciences, we have specialist tutors for each discipline.
The MYP Sciences eAssessment is an optional on-screen examination available to Year 5 students, externally moderated by the IB. It assesses students on all four criteria through a combination of knowledge questions, data response tasks, and investigation analysis. Where taken, it forms 80% of the final grade with the school's internal assessment portfolio making up 20%.
Criterion B (Inquiring & Designing) assesses students' ability to design a valid scientific investigation. This includes writing a focused research question, forming a testable hypothesis, identifying all relevant variables (independent, dependent, and controlled), and designing a safe and ethical method. It is a specific skill set that requires explicit teaching — and it is one of our areas of specialist focus at Improve ME.
Criterion C requires students to accurately record raw data, process it using appropriate methods (averages, graphs, statistical analysis), identify uncertainties and errors, and evaluate both the results and the reliability of the investigation. We teach each of these components systematically, using past assessment tasks to build confidence with real data.
Yes — strong MYP Science performance is the most important foundation for IB Diploma Biology, Chemistry, and Physics. We ensure students not only achieve top MYP grades but also develop the conceptual understanding and investigation skills that HL and SL Diploma sciences build on directly.

What is A-Level? A Parent's Guide

Everything you need to know about the gold standard qualification for university entry

A-Levels (Advanced Levels) are the gold standard qualification for university entry in the UK and are widely recognised by top universities worldwide including those in the UAE, USA, Canada, and Australia.

Students typically study 3–4 A-Level subjects over two years (Year 12 and Year 13), sitting AS-Level exams at the end of Year 12 and full A-Level exams at the end of Year 13. The subjects chosen directly shape university options — making the right support critical.

At Improve ME, our A-Level tutors are subject specialists who know the exact mark schemes, essay structures, and exam techniques needed to achieve A and A* grades. We structure every lesson around what an A and A* answer actually looks like — not just content coverage.

IB DIPLOMA PROGRAMME

IB Diploma

Ages 16–19 (Years 12–13)

The International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP) is a rigorous two-year qualification for students aged 16–18, recognised by top universities worldwide as one of the most demanding and respected pre-university programmes available.

Students study 6 subjects across different subject groups — 3 at Higher Level (HL) and 3 at Standard Level (SL) — alongside three core components: Theory of Knowledge (TOK), the Extended Essay (EE), and Creativity, Activity, Service (CAS).

Book Free Assessment →View Secondary Curriculum →

Trusted by families across Dubai since 2010 · KHDA-approved

PARENT GUIDE

What is the IB Diploma? A Parent's Guide

Everything you need to know about the most demanding pre-university programme

The International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP) is a rigorous two-year qualification for students aged 16–18, recognised by top universities worldwide as one of the most demanding and respected pre-university programmes available.

Students study 6 subjects across different subject groups — 3 at Higher Level (HL) and 3 at Standard Level (SL) — alongside three core components: Theory of Knowledge (TOK), the Extended Essay (EE), and Creativity, Activity, Service (CAS).

The maximum score is 45 points. Most competitive universities require 38+ for selective courses. Our IB teaching targets scores of 6 and 7 — the bands that open doors to competitive university programmes worldwide. At Improve ME, our IB tutors understand not just the content but the specific Internal Assessment requirements, mark schemes, and examiner expectations that make the difference between a 5 and a 7.

Key Facts

IB Year 1 & Year 2 (Years 12–13)

Ages 16–18

6 subjects × 7 + up to 3 core points

Max 45 Points

Accepted in 75+ countries

Globally Recognised

AT A GLANCE

IB Diploma at a Glance

IB Year 1 & Year 2 (Years 12–13)

The Diploma Programme runs over two years, typically aligned with the final two years of secondary school.

6 subjects × 7 + up to 3 core points

Each subject is graded 1–7; TOK and the Extended Essay can add up to 3 bonus points. 38+ opens doors to top universities.

Accepted in 75+ countries

Over 2,000 universities recognise the IB Diploma, including Russell Group, Ivy League, and top UAE institutions.

IB CORE

The Three Core Components

Theory of Knowledge (TOK)

A course that explores the nature of knowledge and how we know what we know. Assessed through an essay and a presentation, with up to 3 bonus points contributing to the total Diploma score.

Extended Essay (EE)

An independent, self-directed piece of research culminating in a 4,000-word essay. Students choose a topic from one of their subjects (or an approved area). Success can add up to 3 points to the Diploma total.

Creativity, Activity, Service (CAS)

A programme of experiential learning outside the classroom. Students complete projects and reflections across creativity, physical activity, and service. CAS is not graded but must be completed to receive the Diploma.

WHY IT MATTERS

Why the IB Diploma Matters More Than Ever

The IB Diploma is accepted and respected by over 2,000 universities in 75+ countries. In Dubai, leading IB World Schools including GEMS World Academy, Jumeirah College, and Dubai International Academy produce highly competitive cohorts — meaning every point counts.

A student scoring 38+ points with strong HL grades opens doors to Russell Group universities, Ivy League schools, and top UAE institutions. The difference between a 5 and a 7 in a single HL subject can determine whether a conditional offer is met. Expert tuition at this level is not optional — it is the deciding factor.

SUBJECTS

Specialist tutoring across every major IB subject — HL & SL, with IA support and exam technique

Psychology
