METHODOLOGY

How We Get Your Child IB Ready

A structured approach built on IB mark schemes, examiner expectations, and years of Dubai IB results

We don't leave IB results to chance. Every session targets the criteria that earn marks — from IAs and the Extended Essay to TOK and final exams. Here's how we do it.

01

Internal Assessment Support

We guide students through every stage of their IAs — from research question to final draft — ensuring they meet all IB criteria and word count requirements.

Whether it's a Biology IA, an Economics commentary, or a Maths exploration, our tutors know exactly what examiners look for and how to structure work so it earns the marks it deserves.

02

Extended Essay Mentoring

Our tutors support students in selecting a focused EE topic, structuring their argument, and meeting the IB's academic writing standards.

From outline review to draft feedback across multiple subject areas, we help students avoid common pitfalls and maximise their chance of an A or B grade.

03

Past Paper & Mark Scheme Mastery

We drill students on past papers using real IB mark schemes so they know exactly what earns marks at HL and SL.

Command terms, marking points, and common errors — we teach your child to think like an examiner and write answers that hit every criterion.

04

TOK Essay & Presentation Coaching

We help students understand and apply the TOK framework to produce coherent, well-structured TOK essays and presentations.

Knowledge questions, arguments, and real-world examples are built into every session so TOK becomes a strength, not a last-minute worry.

EXAM BOARDS

Full alignment with the IB Diploma Programme and its assessment structure

IB preparation isn't a straight line — it's mock exams, IA deadlines, EE drafts, and final papers. We build our programme around each milestone so your child arrives ready.

IB mark schemes reward specific command terms. We train students to "analyse", "evaluate", and "discuss" with precision.

Our tutors approach every piece of work through the lens of what an IB examiner is looking for — and teach students to do the same.

html:
1. Home

Biology

Biology at GCSE and beyond is not a memory test — though many students treat it as one. The questions that separate top grades involve reasoning, data interpretation, and applying knowledge to scenarios students haven't encountered before. Parents often don't realise this until their child, who can recall every process in photosynthesis, drops two grades on their paper because they couldn't apply it to an unfamiliar experimental context. At Improve ME, Biology is taught from GCSE and IGCSE through to A-Level and IB Diploma, with board-specific preparation for AQA, Edexcel, Cambridge CAIE, and the IBO.

Comprehensive Biology Coverage

Cell Biology & Genetics

  • Cell structure, function, and division (mitosis and meiosis)
  • DNA structure, replication, and protein synthesis
  • Genetic inheritance, variation, and evolution by natural selection
  • Gene expression and mutations
  • Genetic engineering and biotechnology applications

Human Biology & Physiology

  • Organ systems (digestive, respiratory, circulatory, nervous, endocrine)
  • Gas exchange and respiration (aerobic and anaerobic)
  • Transport in animals (blood, circulation, heart structure and function)
  • Coordination and homeostasis (nervous system, hormones, temperature regulation)
  • Health, disease, immunity, and vaccines

Ecology & Environmental Biology

  • Ecosystems, food webs, and energy transfer
  • Photosynthesis and its role in life on Earth
  • Biodiversity, classification, and adaptation
  • Nutrient cycles (carbon, nitrogen, water)
  • Human impact on ecosystems and conservation strategies

Methodology

How we teach Biology

Each biology topic follows a proven five-step teaching sequence:

  1. Core concept introduction – Clear explanations of biological principles
  2. Visual learning – Diagrams, models, and interactive resources
  3. Practical application – Working through specimen questions and past papers
  4. Checkpoint assessment – Quick-fire questions to confirm understanding
  5. Exam technique – Command words, mark schemes, how to structure answers

Small Groups

Biology thrives on discussion and questioning. In small groups (average 3–4 students, maximum 6), your child can:

  • Ask questions immediately when concepts are unclear
  • Discuss ethical issues (genetic engineering, cloning, conservation)
  • Work through complex diagrams together (cell structure, food webs, body systems)
  • Practice explaining biological processes aloud (a key skill for exams)
01

Qualified Specialists

Subject experts with proven results across all exam boards.

02

Exam-Focused

We know what examiners expect.

What Students Achieve

Our Biology lessons are structured around Grades 7, 8, and 9 at GCSE and A and A* at IGCSE — the bands that reflect genuine subject mastery and open the strongest university pathways. We structure every A-Level Biology lesson around what an A and A* answer looks like in practice. For IB Biology, we target scores of 6 and 7 across all assessment components. The range of students we support reflects two different kinds of success. A student who joins at the start of Year 10 with a predicted Grade 4 can realistically target Grade 7–8 within two terms — with consistent attendance, regular past paper work, and targeted teaching on their specific weak areas. Students from Dubai's leading British schools, including Dubai College, have achieved Grade 9s at GCSE and A* grades at A-Level. At IB, students who joined with a score of 3 at HL have reached 7 by the end of the Diploma programme. Biology students from Improve ME have gone on to study at Oxford, UCL, Imperial College London, King's College London, the University of Manchester, the University of Edinburgh, and other leading universities — predominantly in Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Veterinary Science, Biochemistry, and Biomedical Science.

Resources and Support

Topic-by-topic diagnostic booklets to identify exact foundation gaps before teaching new content. Worked-example booklets showing precisely how top-grade answers are constructed. Interactive diagrams for key processes — cell division, photosynthesis, genetic inheritance. Past paper question banks organised by topic and difficulty level, updated per board. Yearly past paper booklets for GCSE, IGCSE, A-Level, and IB. Crash courses available during key revision windows. Winter revision and booster programmes. Progress reports issued regularly, with grade tracking and WhatsApp updates to parents.

What a Lesson Looks Like

A typical GCSE or IGCSE Biology lesson at Improve ME: 5–10 minutes revisiting the previous topic — not a test, but a structured recall exercise. 20–25 minutes of guided teaching on the new concept, with real-world examples used to build genuine understanding rather than surface familiarity. 15–20 minutes of exam-style questions under realistic conditions. Mark scheme review — students see exactly how marks are awarded, and where their answer fell short. Homework set from a targeted question bank organised by topic and difficulty. A-Level and IB lessons follow the same structure but with longer guided discussion phases and analytical questions that mirror the extended-response demands of those papers.

Past Papers — When and How

Past papers are introduced as soon as the content for a given year group is complete. In the second term of Year 11, lessons shift predominantly to past paper work, with short targeted revision sessions addressing weaknesses identified during papers. Term 3 is past-paper intensive with mock exams run under timed conditions. At A-Level and IB, past paper work begins earlier and runs alongside new content throughout the course. Students use board-specific topic question banks from Term 1 so they understand mark scheme language from the beginning, not just before the exam.

Exam boards

All Major Exam Boards

AQAEdexcelOCRCambridgePearsonIBWJEC

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

How Exam Boards Differ — and Why It Matters

AQA GCSE: Strong emphasis on human biology contexts. Mark scheme language is precise — students who use vague terminology lose marks they could have kept. Edexcel GCSE: More calculation-heavy than AQA. Data response questions are weighted throughout all papers. Cambridge IGCSE: Explicitly assesses practical and data interpretation skills, often through Alternative to Practical papers. Many students are underprepared for these. IB Biology HL: Significantly greater analytical depth than SL. Data analysis questions appear across Papers 1, 2, and 3. Internal Assessment is assessed and requires structured support. IB MYP Sciences: Criterion-referenced and inquiry-led — the emphasis is on extended written responses and real-world application rather than exam recall. At Improve ME, every student's lessons are built around their specific board from day one.

1. Home

Business Studies Tutoring in Dubai

Business Studies is one of those subjects where students often feel comfortable until the exam, and then discover that comfort and marks are not the same thing. Case study questions, evaluation tasks, and data response questions all require a specific kind of thinking — and specific technique — that is rarely given enough focus in school. At Improve ME, Business Studies is taught from GCSE and IGCSE through to A-Level and IB Diploma, with a focus on the question types that actually appear in papers.

Book Free AssessmentExplore Qualifications
GCSEIGCSEA-LevelIB

Business Studies Is Not Common Sense

The most damaging misconception about Business Studies is that it requires no real preparation — that it is 'common sense' compared to the sciences or mathematics. Students who arrive at GCSE or A-Level with this assumption consistently underperform. Business Studies rewards students who know exactly how to structure an answer for their specific exam board, how to apply theory to case study material, and how to write evaluatively. These are learned skills. They do not come from reading the textbook.

How Top Students Answer Business Studies Questions

At GCSE, IGCSE, and A-Level, our students use the PEEL method for extended answers: Point, Explain, Example, Link back to context. For evaluation questions, we use the AJIM structure: Answer, Justify, It Depends On, Most Importantly. These frameworks ensure students hit every assessment objective the mark scheme rewards — consistently. Each exam board has a different paper structure and question style. AQA, Edexcel, Cambridge International, and OCR all require board-specific practice materials. Using the wrong past papers — or mixed papers — is one of the most common preparation mistakes we see.

What We Cover — GCSE and IGCSE Business Studies

Business objectives and types of organisations, classification of businesses, location decisions, international business, internal and external communication, recruitment and training, employee motivation, organisational structures, sources of finance, cash flow forecasting, cost and break-even analysis, financial documents and accounts analysis, market research, the marketing mix, economies and diseconomies of scale, methods of production, quality management, and the sales process.

What We Cover — A-Level and IAL Business

Nature and purpose of business, marketing mix and strategy, managing people, entrepreneurship and leadership, financial planning and accounts analysis, resource management, external influences, decision-making techniques, managing change, global markets and business expansion, and global marketing.

Separate Sessions for Every Specification

Business Studies and Economics are subjects where exam board matters enormously — AQA, Edexcel, Cambridge International, and OCR all have different paper structures, question styles, and marking priorities. At Improve ME, we run separate sessions for each specification and year group throughout the day. Your child is never in a mixed-spec group where preparation for the wrong board becomes someone else's problem.

Is Your Child Ready for GCSE Business Studies?

Students should be comfortable with percentages, ratios, graphs, averages, and basic algebra before beginning GCSE or IGCSE Business Studies. These skills appear frequently in calculations — elasticity, break-even analysis, national income, and inflation all require numerical fluency. Students who are not confident with these areas will benefit from additional maths support alongside their Business Studies preparation.

What Students Achieve

We target Grades 7, 8, and 9 at GCSE and A and A* at IGCSE — achievable with the right exam technique, not just content knowledge. At A-Level we target grades A and A*. At IB we target scores of 6 and 7 — the bands competitive universities expect to see. A student from Jumeirah College joined Improve ME for Economics and improved from a GCSE Grade 2 to a Grade 8/9. Students from Dubai College and JESS have gone on to gain admission to UCL and Oxford after completing their A-Level and IB Business and Economics programmes at Improve ME.

Available across

Tutoring Built Around Your Child's Exam Board

Business studies isn't a single subject — it's taught differently depending on whether your child follows AQA, Edexcel, or Cambridge. At Improve ME, every session is built around your child's exact specification.

html:
1. Home

Economics Tutoring in Dubai

Economics students at GCSE and A-Level often find themselves caught between two demands: the ability to explain concepts clearly, and the ability to evaluate them with the kind of nuanced argument that earns the top mark bands. Diagrams, definitions, and essay structure all have to work together. At Improve ME, Economics is taught from GCSE and IGCSE through to A-Level and IB Diploma, with structured essay and diagram technique built into every session.

Book Free AssessmentExplore Qualifications
GCSEIGCSEA-LevelIB

Economics Is Not All Theory

'Economics is just theory' is one of the most persistent and costly misconceptions students bring into the exam room. In reality, Economics at GCSE, A-Level, and IB rewards a very specific combination of skills: the ability to define terms precisely, apply them to real-world scenarios, construct reasoned arguments, and evaluate those arguments from multiple perspectives — all under timed conditions. Students who treat Economics as a content-recall exercise consistently miss the evaluation marks that separate Grade 6 from Grade 9.

How We Build Exam Technique in Economics

At GCSE, IGCSE, and A-Level, we use the KAAE method: Knowledge (AO1), Application (AO2), Analysis (AO3), and Evaluation (AO4). Every extended answer is structured to hit all four assessment objectives — because mark schemes reward all four, in sequence. For IB Economics (SL and HL), we use the DEED+E method: Define, Explain, Example, Diagram, and Evaluation. IB papers require students to interpret real economic data, construct diagrams accurately, and write extended evaluative responses. These are skills we build deliberately from the first session, not the revision period.

What We Cover — GCSE and IGCSE Economics

Microeconomics: The basic economic problem, economic assumptions, demand and supply, elasticities, market equilibrium, mixed economy, externalities and market failure, the production process, division of labour, economies and diseconomies of scale, competition, monopoly, oligopoly, the labour market, and government interventions including taxes, subsidies, and pollution permits. Macroeconomics: Macroeconomic objectives of government, balance of payments, government policies, globalisation, international trade and protectionism, tariffs, quotas and embargoes, currency exchange rates, and the World Trade Organization.

What We Cover — A-Level and IAL Economics

Microeconomics: Economic methodology, individual economic decision-making, price determination, production, costs and revenue, monopoly and competition, business objectives, the labour market, market failure and government intervention. Macroeconomics: Measuring macroeconomic performance, financial markets, fiscal and monetary policy, supply-side policy, and the international economy.

What We Cover — IB Economics (SL and HL)

Introduction to economics, competitive market equilibrium, the role of government in microeconomics, market failure, inequality and poverty, variations in economic activity, measuring economic performance, demand management policies, supply-side policies, international trade and protectionism, globalisation, exchange rates, balance of payments, sustainable development, and economic growth and development strategies.

Why Economics Is a Life Skill, Not Just a Qualification

By the end of an Economics course at A-Level or IB, students can critically interpret economic data, construct evidence-based arguments, and make reasoned judgements under pressure. These are skills that most academic subjects never develop explicitly — part analytical, part creative, part real-world problem-solving. Parents who understand this push their children to take Economics seriously long before the exam window. Parents who don't often wish they had.

Separate Sessions for Every Specification

Business Studies and Economics are subjects where exam board matters enormously — AQA, Edexcel, Cambridge International, and OCR all have different paper structures, question styles, and marking priorities. At Improve ME, we run separate sessions for each specification and year group throughout the day. Your child is never in a mixed-spec group where preparation for the wrong board becomes someone else's problem.

What Students Achieve

We target Grades 7, 8, and 9 at GCSE and A and A* at IGCSE — achievable with the right exam technique, not just content knowledge. At A-Level we target grades A and A*. At IB we target scores of 6 and 7 — the bands competitive universities expect to see.

Available across

Economics Across All Qualifications

Economics consistently catches students off guard. Unlike subjects where memorising content is enough to pass, economics exams reward the ability to apply theory to unfamiliar scenarios, draw and interpret diagrams accurately, and construct arguments that evaluate both sides of a policy question.

Quantitative Reasoning

Structure

Programme Structure

Step-by-step preparation process

01

Hour 1 — Introduction to CAT4

What the test measures and how it is scored. Overview of the four batteries: Verbal Reasoning, Non-Verbal Reasoning, Quantitative Reasoning, Spatial Ability.

02

Hour 2 — Verbal Reasoning

Word analogies, odd one out, sentence completion. Timed practice with exam-condition technique.

03

Hour 3 — Non-Verbal Reasoning

Series completion, matrices, pattern recognition. Visual strategies and elimination technique.

04

Hour 4 — Quantitative Reasoning

Number sequences, data interpretation, word problems. Mental maths strategies under timed conditions.

05

Hour 5 — Spatial Ability

2D/3D shape rotation, nets, spatial sequences. Common error patterns and how to avoid them.

06

Hour 6 — Full Practice Test + Review

Simulated CAT4 test under timed conditions. Full debrief, score analysis, and targeted revision plan for each student.

WHAT WE DO

What is Educational Counselling?

Educational counselling provides expert guidance to students and families making important academic decisions. From choosing the right school and entrance exam preparation to selecting GCSE/IGCSE options and planning university applications, our counsellors help navigate the complex educational landscape in Dubai and internationally.

School Selection & Admissions

title: Home Education Tutoring Dubai | Flexible Learning Support
description: Expert home education support in Dubai. Flexible, structured tutoring for home-educated students across core subjects. Small groups, specialist teachers. Book a free assessment today.
html:
1. Home
  2. /Courses
  3. /Home Education Support

Supporting Home-Educated Students in Dubai — Every Subject, Every Age

Home education in Dubai is more common than most people realise — and more demanding than most people anticipate. Whether your child is educated at home full-time, following a period between schools, or pursuing a qualification pathway as a private candidate, the academic foundation they build now will shape every opportunity available to them later.

Improve ME Institute has been supporting students in Dubai since 2010. We work with home-educated students across all ages and all curricula — not as a replacement for a parent's teaching, but as the structured academic complement to it.

Book a Free Assessment

What We Offer Home-Educated Students

We cover all core subjects taught at Improve ME — Mathematics, English, Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, Physics), Business Studies, and Economics— for students from EYFS through to A-Level and IB Diploma. Home-educated students attend the same small-group sessions as all our students, sitting alongside peers and working through structured programmes that match their level and qualification pathway.

For families following a specific curriculum — British, IB, American, CBSE, or another framework — we align our teaching directly to that curriculum. If your child is working towards IGCSEs or A-Levels as a private candidate, we build their programme around the specific boards and examination schedule you have registered for.

Flexible Scheduling Around Your Timetable

One of the significant advantages of home education is control over time. Our sessions run throughout the day — mornings, afternoons, and evenings — so home-educated students can attend during hours that would not be available to school-based students. We work with families to build a weekly schedule that fits naturally around the rest of your child's education, rather than fragmenting it.

Qualification Pathways for Home-Educated Students

Home-educated students in Dubai can still sit internationally recognised qualifications through private candidate registration. The most common routes our students take:

  • IGCSE: Available through Cambridge CAIE and Edexcel International. Recognised globally and widely accepted for university entry. No school registration required — students register as private candidates through an approved examination centre.
  • A-Level and International A-Level (IAL): Available through AQA, Edexcel, and Cambridge. Modular IAL options offer particular flexibility for students managing their own timetable.
  • IB Diploma: Requires enrolment through an IB World School — this is not a private candidate qualification. We can advise on which Dubai schools accept external students for IB enrolment.

We do not register students for examinations — that is a family responsibility. We prepare students to excel in whichever pathway they have chosen. A verified online school must be attended in order to receive yearly grade and year advancement.

The Group Learning Advantage

One of the most consistent pieces of feedback we receive from home-educated families is the value of small-group learning. Their child understands the academic content — what they gain from Improve ME is the experience of working through problems alongside other students: hearing different approaches, being challenged by peers, and developing the kind of collaborative thinking that examinations and universities both reward.

Our groups run to a maximum of six students, with an average of two to three. Home-educated students are encouraged to be placed in morning sessions where they are in a smaller class setting. This can cater to the needs of students who may need extra support, especially in cases where students are moving into a new curriculum in Dubai.

Progress Reporting for Parent-Educators

We recognise that parents who home-educate carry a layer of responsibility that school parents do not — they need to know not just that their child is improving, but specifically where, by how much, and what comes next.

Every home-educated student at Improve ME receives structured progress reports covering performance by topic, areas for development, and recommendations for the following term. Our tutors are available for direct communication throughout the term. You will never have to chase us for an update.

Where to Start

The first step is a free assessment. We will identify exactly where your child is working, which subjects need structured support, and how to build a programme that fits your educational approach.

Home-educated students are welcome at every level — from a three-year-old beginning phonics to an eighteen-year-old preparing for their final examinations.

Book a Free AssessmentEducational Counselling →Explore our curriculum section →

For sciences: Physics, Chemistry, and Biology. For curriculum pathways, explore our curriculum section.

GCSE Mathematics

Master GCSE Mathematics from Foundation to Higher with structured support for number, algebra, geometry, trigonometry, and statistics. We build exam technique and past-paper confidence for Grades 7–9.

Book Free AssessmentView All Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is GCSE Mathematics?

GCSE Mathematics is a core qualification that builds from number and algebra through to geometry, trigonometry, and statistics. Students are assessed across three written papers (one non-calculator, two calculator), with Foundation tier covering Grades 1–5 and Higher tier Grades 4–9. At Improve ME, our programme maps every topic to the AQA, Edexcel, and OCR specifications, with structured past-paper practice, method marks focus, and exam technique that targets Grades 7–9.

Foundation Tier

Grades 1–5. Focus on essential mathematical skills, number work, basic algebra, geometry, and statistics.

  • •Core arithmetic and fluency
  • •Introduction to algebra
  • •Geometry and measurement basics

Higher Tier

Grades 4–9. Advanced topics including challenging algebra, trigonometry, and multi-step problem-solving.

  • •Algebraic reasoning and equations
  • •Trigonometry and circle theorems
  • •Higher-grade exam technique

Consider GCSE Further Mathematics

High-achieving students (grade 8–9 target) should consider GCSE Further Mathematics alongside regular GCSE Maths.

GCSE Further Mathematics
COURSE CONTENT

GCSE Mathematics Course Content

Comprehensive coverage of exam-board specifications with targeted grade improvements and exam technique for Grades 7–9.

Number, Algebra & Ratio

  • •Number: place value, four operations, fractions, decimals, percentages, bounds
  • •Expanding, factorising, and solving linear and quadratic equations
  • •Sequences, nth term, and quadratic sequences
  • •Ratio, proportion, and direct/inverse proportion
  • •Surds and indices; problem-solving for Grades 7–9

Geometry & Trigonometry

  • •Circle theorems, proof, and geometric reasoning
  • •Pythagoras and trigonometry (SOH CAH TOA)
  • •Area, volume, similarity, and congruence
  • •Vectors and transformations
  • •Multi-step geometric and applied problems

Statistics & Probability

  • •Probability trees, Venn diagrams, and combined events
  • •Averages, spread (range, IQR), and sampling
  • •Representing and interpreting data; time series
  • •Foundation vs Higher tier; exam technique and command words
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Students sit three written papers (one non-calculator, two calculator). We build technique, timing, and confidence with structured past-paper practice for Grades 7–9.

Paper 1
Non-Calculator
  • •1 hour 30 minutes, 80 marks
  • •Number, algebra, ratio, geometry, probability
  • •Method and accuracy; no calculator allowed
Paper 2
Calculator
  • •1 hour 30 minutes, 80 marks
  • •Full specification coverage
  • •Problem-solving and multi-step questions
Paper 3
Calculator
  • •1 hour 30 minutes, 80 marks
  • •Mixed topic coverage
  • •Structured past-paper practice builds confidence

Grade boundaries vary by year and exam board. We focus on maximising marks through method, accuracy, and smart exam strategy.

OCR

04

Progress Tracking

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for GCSE Mathematics?

KHDA-approved centre in Gold and Diamond Park — 16+ years teaching Mathematics in Dubai
Small groups (a maximum of six students) for personalised attention
Structured curriculum aligned to AQA, Edexcel, and OCR — Foundation and Higher tier
Three written papers: non-calculator and calculator practice with past papers
Exam technique and method marks focus for Grades 7–9
Consider GCSE Further Mathematics for high achievers (grade 8–9 target)
Regular progress reports and flexible scheduling
Dedicated learning environment in our 7,000 sq ft facility
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Mathematics at Improve ME

Foundation tier covers Grades 1–5 and Higher tier Grades 4–9. The same topics appear at different depths; Higher includes more algebra, circle theorems, and trigonometry. We place students according to their assessment and school pathway, and support both tiers with specification-aligned practice.
Students sit three written papers: Paper 1 (non-calculator, 1 hr 30 min, 80 marks) and Papers 2 and 3 (calculator, 1 hr 30 min each, 80 marks). We build technique and timing with structured past-paper practice across all three.
GCSE Further Mathematics is an additional qualification for high-achieving students targeting grade 8–9 in GCSE Maths. It is taken alongside GCSE Maths, not instead of it, and builds a strong foundation for A-Level. We can advise based on your child's current performance and plans.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /GCSE
  4. /Biology

GCSE Biology

Strengthen cell biology, bioenergetics, ecology, and genetics with exam-focused teaching and structured practice in small groups (average 3–4 students, maximum 6).

Book Free AssessmentView All Biology Courses
OVERVIEW

What is GCSE Biology?

GCSE Biology is one of the most content-heavy qualifications your child will sit. It spans seven major topic areas — from cell structure and transport through to ecology and ecosystems — and is assessed across two written papers, each worth 50% of the final grade. Students must demonstrate precise recall of processes and terminology alongside the ability to analyse data, evaluate experiments, and apply knowledge to unfamiliar contexts. At Improve ME, our GCSE Biology programme follows a structured 26-to-36-week plan (depending on exam board and pathway) that maps every sub-topic to a specific lesson. Each concept is taught in focused 10-20 minute segments, followed immediately by worksheet practice at three levels of difficulty — so your child builds understanding and confidence week by week, with nothing left to last-minute revision.

GCSE
Qualification
Ages 14-16
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

GCSE Biology Course Content

Comprehensive coverage of exam-board specifications with targeted grade improvements and exam technique for Grades 7–9.

Cell Biology and Infection

  • •Cell structure: animal, plant, bacterial, prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells
  • •Microscopy and calculating magnification
  • •Transport in cells: diffusion, osmosis, and active transport
  • •Cell division: the cell cycle, stages of mitosis, and cancer
  • •Stem cells: types, uses, and ethical considerations
  • •Communicable diseases: viral, bacterial, fungal, and protist pathogens
  • •Immune system: vaccination, antibiotics, and monoclonal antibodies (Triple)
  • •Non-communicable diseases: cancer, CVD, and lifestyle risk factors
  • •Drug discovery and development, including clinical trials

Bioenergetics and Homeostasis

  • •Photosynthesis: rate factors, uses of glucose, and leaf structure
  • •Aerobic and anaerobic respiration, and response to exercise
  • •The nervous system: neurons, synapses, and the reflex arc
  • •The brain and the eye, including defects and treatments (Triple)
  • •Hormonal control: blood glucose regulation and negative feedback
  • •The menstrual cycle, contraception, and reproductive technologies
  • •Thermoregulation and the role of ADH in water balance
  • •Kidney function: dialysis and kidney failure
  • •Plant hormones: auxins, phototropism, and gravitropism (Triple)

Ecology and Genetics

  • •Ecosystems: communities, populations, and interdependence
  • •Abiotic and biotic factors affecting distribution
  • •Nutrient cycles: carbon, water, and nitrogen
  • •Biodiversity, human impact, and conservation strategies
  • •DNA structure, transcription, and translation
  • •Genetic inheritance: Punnett squares, pedigree analysis, and alleles
  • •Variation, natural selection, and evidence for evolution
  • •Classification and speciation
  • •Genetic engineering, selective breeding, and cloning

Topics Covered Edexcel GCSE Biology topic structure: Topic 1: Key concepts in Biology Topic 2: Cells and control Topic 3: Genetics Topic 4: Natural selection and genetic modification Topic 5: Health, disease and the development of medicines Topic 6: Plant structures and their functions Topic 7: Animal coordination, control and homeostasis Topic 8: Exchange and transport in animals Topic 9: Ecosystems and material cycles AQA and OCR follow different topic structures but cover equivalent content areas. All students use board-specific materials from Term 1.

ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Two written papers cover the full specification; required practicals are assessed within the papers. We build exam technique and extended response skills.

Paper 1
Cell Biology, Organisation, Infection, Bioenergetics
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Tests cell structure, organisation, communicable and non-communicable diseases, and bioenergetics
  • •Mix of multiple choice, structured, closed short-answer, and open-response questions
  • •Required practicals on microscopy, osmosis, enzymes, and food tests
Paper 2
Homeostasis, Inheritance, Ecology
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Tests homeostasis, genetics, evolution, and ecology
  • •Same question format as Paper 1
  • •Exam-technique focus on 6-mark extended-response questions
  • •Required practicals on decay, reaction times, and field investigations
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

01

Small Groups

Small groups (a maximum of six students) for personalised attention.

02

Exam Technique

We teach what examiners reward: method marks, structure, timing, and common pitfalls to avoid.

03

Past Papers

Regular practice with authentic questions under timed conditions—reviewed with clear feedback.

04

Progress Tracking

Clear targets and regular check-ins so students know exactly what to improve next.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for GCSE Biology?

KHDA-approved centre in Gold and Diamond Park — tutoring Biology in Dubai since 2010
Small groups (a maximum of six students) for near-private attention
Dedicated subject tutors with proven exam-board knowledge and access to our proprietary lesson plans
Structured curriculum aligned to AQA, Edexcel, and OCR — every topic mapped week by week across 26–36 sessions
Separate Triple and Combined groups — your child learns at the right depth for their qualification pathway
Three-tier worksheet system — each topic includes worksheets at three difficulty levels for targeted consolidation
Exam boards matched to your school — students at Dubai College, JESS, Kings Al Barsha, and other leading schools already study with us
Dedicated learning environment across our 7,000 sq ft facility with 40+ specialist tutors
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Biology at Improve ME

Most students find homeostasis and response the most challenging unit — it brings together the nervous system, hormonal control, blood glucose regulation, and kidney function. Genetics and inheritance is another common sticking point. At Improve ME, our lesson plans dedicate significant time to these demanding topics, with focused 10–15 minute segments and worksheet practice at three difficulty levels.
We help students move beyond passive re-reading with active recall, spaced repetition, and specification-led revision. Our three-tier worksheets are designed for active recall, and our week-by-week programme revisits earlier topics at strategic intervals.
Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your child's strengths and future plans. Triple Science awards three separate GCSEs and is stronger for A-Level sciences or careers in medicine or research. Combined Science covers core content in 26–29 weeks. We run separate groups for each pathway.
Start with the specification as a checklist, practise past papers early (we build this into lessons), focus on diagrams and processes, and follow a structured programme so every topic is covered with built-in review points.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /GCSE
  4. /Chemistry

GCSE Chemistry

Tackle moles, bonding, and organic chemistry with clear step-by-step support and exam technique for both papers.

Book Free AssessmentView All Chemistry Courses
OVERVIEW

What is GCSE Chemistry?

GCSE Chemistry builds from atomic structure and bonding through to organic chemistry and atmospheric science. It is a subject that rewards methodical thinking — students who can set up calculations confidently, balance equations accurately, and explain reaction mechanisms clearly tend to perform strongly across both papers. At Improve ME, our GCSE Chemistry programme follows a structured 21–28 week plan (depending on exam board and pathway) that maps every sub-topic to a specific lesson. Each concept is taught in focused segments of 5–15 minutes, followed immediately by worksheet practice at three difficulty levels — so your child masters each step before moving to the next.

GCSE
Qualification
Ages 14-16
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

GCSE Chemistry Course Content

Comprehensive coverage of exam-board specifications with targeted grade improvements and exam technique for Grades 7–9.

Atomic Structure and Bonding

  • •Atomic structure: protons, neutrons, electrons, and isotopes
  • •Electronic configuration and the modern periodic table
  • •Ionic bonding: forming ions, dot-and-cross diagrams, and lattice properties
  • •Covalent bonding: simple molecules and their properties
  • •Giant covalent structures: diamond, graphite, graphene, and fullerenes
  • •Metallic bonding and properties of metals
  • •States of matter and changes of state
  • •Chemical calculations: relative formula mass, moles, and empirical formulae

Reactions and Energy

  • •Acids, alkalis, and the pH scale
  • •Reactions of acids: neutralisation, metal carbonates, and metal oxides
  • •Making salts: titration method, solubility rules, and insoluble salts
  • •Electrolysis: molten compounds, aqueous solutions, and half equations
  • •Reactivity series, displacement reactions, and metal extraction
  • •Rates of reaction: concentration, temperature, surface area, and catalysts
  • •Energy changes: exothermic and endothermic reactions, reaction profiles, and bond energy calculations
  • •Reversible reactions and dynamic equilibrium

Organic Chemistry and Analysis

  • •Crude oil, fractional distillation, and properties of hydrocarbons
  • •Alkanes and alkenes as homologous series
  • •Cracking and its industrial importance
  • •Reactions of alkenes and alcohols, and production of ethanol (Triple)
  • •Polymers and nanoparticles (Triple)
  • •Earth's atmosphere: formation, greenhouse effect, and climate change
  • •Atmospheric pollutants and water treatment
  • •Chemical analysis: flame tests, tests for cations, anions, and common gases
  • •Using resources: life-cycle assessments, ceramics, and composites (Triple)
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Two written papers cover the full specification; required practicals are assessed within the papers. We build exam technique and extended response skills.

Paper 1
Atomic Structure, Bonding, Quantitative, Chemical Changes
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Atomic structure, bonding, quantitative chemistry, chemical changes, and energy changes
  • •Mix of multiple choice, short answer, and extended response questions
  • •Heavy on calculation-based questions: moles, concentrations, and reacting masses
  • •Required practicals on titrations, electrolysis, and temperature changes
Paper 2
Rates, Organic, Analysis, Chemistry of Atmosphere
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Rates of reaction, organic chemistry, chemical analysis, atmospheric chemistry, and using resources
  • •Data interpretation and graph-reading questions are common
  • •Extended responses on industrial chemistry and environmental topics
  • •Required practicals on rates of reaction, chromatography, and identifying ions
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our GCSE Chemistry Teaching Approach

01

Small Groups

Small groups (a maximum of six students) for personalised attention.

02

Exam Technique

We teach what examiners reward: method marks, structure, timing, and common pitfalls to avoid.

03

Past Papers

Regular practice with authentic questions under timed conditions—reviewed with clear feedback.

04

Progress Tracking

Clear targets and regular check-ins so students know exactly what to improve next.

05

What Top Grades in Chemistry Actually Require

'My child understands it in class — why aren't they getting an A?' This is the most common question we hear from parents. Understanding content and performing under exam conditions are two different skills. Chemistry rewards students who show full working in calculations, use precise mark scheme language, apply knowledge to unfamiliar scenarios, and manage time well across all three papers. Comprehension without exam practice is not sufficient for top grades.

06

Grade Improvement — What to Expect

A GCSE student who joined Improve ME predicted Grade 5 achieved Grade 8 after two terms. An A-Level student who received a C in Year 12 mocks achieved an A in the final exam. These outcomes follow a consistent pattern: structured teaching, mole calculation practice, board-specific past papers, and detailed feedback on every session.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for GCSE Chemistry?

KHDA-approved centre in Gold and Diamond Park — teaching Chemistry in Dubai since 2010
Small groups (a maximum of six students) for personalised attention
Specialist subject tutors with deep exam-board knowledge and access to our proprietary lesson plans
Structured curriculum aligned to AQA, Edexcel, and OCR — every topic mapped week by week across 21–28 sessions
Separate Triple and Combined groups — your child learns at the right depth for their pathway
Three-tier worksheet system — each topic includes worksheets at three difficulty levels for targeted consolidation
All exam boards covered: AQA, Edexcel, OCR — matched to your child's school requirements
Flexible scheduling to fit your family's routine — with regular progress reports so you always know where things stand
Dedicated learning environment in our 7,000 sq ft facility with 40+ specialist tutors
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Chemistry at Improve ME

Work systematically: count atoms of each element on both sides, then adjust coefficients one element at a time, starting with the most complex molecule. At Improve ME, we teach equation balancing from the first week and reinforce it through every subsequent topic.
The topics students struggle with most are moles and chemical calculations, electrolysis (predicting products and half equations), and organic chemistry. Our programme dedicates two full weeks to chemical calculations alone and two weeks to electrolysis, with focused worksheet sets for each.
Master the calculations first; learn equations in context within the reactions they belong to; practise past papers under timed conditions (we build this in from early on); and use the specification as a checklist.
All boards require recall of key equations: word and symbol equations for acids and displacement, combustion of hydrocarbons; formulae for relative formula mass, moles = mass / Mr, concentration; and (Triple) atom economy, percentage yield, bond energy. We introduce each equation when the topic is taught and reinforce through worksheets and past papers.

html:
1. Home

GCSE Physics

Master forces, energy, electricity, and atomic structure with required practicals and exam technique for Foundation and Higher tier.

Book Free AssessmentView All Physics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is GCSE Physics?

GCSE Physics covers forces, energy, electricity, waves, and atomic structure. It is the most mathematical of the three sciences — students need to confidently rearrange formulae, substitute values, and interpret graphs across both papers. Building this fluency early is what separates students who scrape through from those who achieve top grades. At Improve ME, our GCSE Physics programme follows a structured 23–32 week plan (depending on exam board and pathway) that maps every topic to a specific lesson. Each concept is taught in focused segments, paired immediately with worksheet practice at three difficulty levels — building both conceptual understanding and calculation confidence week by week.

GCSE
Qualification
Ages 14-16
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

GCSE Physics Course Content

Comprehensive coverage of exam-board specifications with targeted grade improvements and exam technique for Grades 7–9.

Forces and Motion

  • •Scalar and vector quantities, distance, displacement, speed, velocity, and acceleration
  • •Distance-time and velocity-time graphs: interpretation and calculations
  • •Newton's three laws of motion, inertial mass, and weight
  • •Resultant forces, free-body diagrams, and vector resolution
  • •Terminal velocity and air resistance
  • •Momentum: conservation, change in momentum, and collisions
  • •Stopping distances: thinking distance, braking distance, and factors affecting both
  • •Moments, gears, and levers (Triple)
  • •Hooke's law, spring constant, and limit of proportionality
  • •Pressure in fluids and upthrust (Triple)

Energy, Electricity and Waves

  • •Energy stores and transfers, kinetic energy, and gravitational potential energy
  • •Conservation of energy, dissipation, and efficiency calculations
  • •Renewable and non-renewable energy sources and global demand
  • •Work done, power, and energy transfer calculations
  • •Transverse and longitudinal waves, wave equation, and properties
  • •Sound, ultrasound, infrasound, and seismic waves (Triple)
  • •Electromagnetic spectrum: types, uses, dangers, and ionising radiation
  • •Lenses, ray diagrams, and total internal reflection (Triple)
  • •Series and parallel circuits, Ohm's law, V=IR, and I-V characteristics
  • •Mains electricity, fuses, circuit breakers, and electrical safety
  • •Static electricity, electric fields, and earthing (Triple)

Magnetism and Atomic Structure

  • •Permanent and induced magnets, magnetic fields, and field lines
  • •Electromagnets: wire, flat coil, and solenoid
  • •Motor effect, Fleming's left-hand rule, and the electric motor
  • •Electromagnetic induction, transformers, and the National Grid
  • •Alternators, dynamos, loudspeakers, and microphones (Triple)
  • •Radioactivity: alpha, beta, and gamma radiation and their properties
  • •Atomic models: Dalton, Thomson, Rutherford, and Bohr
  • •Half-life, activity, and detecting radiation
  • •Uses of radioactivity and nuclear medicine (Triple)
  • •Nuclear fission, fusion, and nuclear power (Triple)
  • •Astronomy: solar system, stellar evolution, red shift, and CMBR (Triple)
  • •Particle model: density, specific heat capacity, specific latent heat, and gas pressure
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Two written papers cover the full specification; required practicals are assessed within the papers. We build exam technique and extended response skills.

Paper 1
Energy, Electricity, Particle Model, Atomic Structure
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Energy, electricity, particle model of matter, and atomic structure
  • •Heavy on calculation questions: efficiency, V=IR, energy equations, and half-life
  • •Mix of multiple choice, short answer, and extended response
  • •Required practicals on specific heat capacity, resistance, and density
Paper 2
Forces, Waves, Magnetism, Space
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Forces, waves, magnetism, electromagnetic induction, and space physics (Triple)
  • •Graph interpretation and data analysis questions are common
  • •Extended responses on energy resources and wave applications
  • •Required practicals on acceleration, waves, and radiation

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for GCSE Physics?

KHDA-approved centre in Gold and Diamond Park — teaching Physics in Dubai since 2010
Small groups (a maximum of six students) for personalised attention
Specialist subject tutors with deep exam-board knowledge and access to our proprietary lesson plans
Structured curriculum aligned to AQA, Edexcel, and OCR — every topic mapped week by week across 23–32 sessions
Separate Triple and Combined groups — your child learns at the right depth for their pathway
Three-tier worksheet system — each topic includes practice at three difficulty levels for targeted consolidation
All exam boards covered: AQA, Edexcel, OCR — matched to your child's school requirements
Regular progress reports and flexible scheduling to fit your family's routine
Dedicated learning environment in our 7,000 sq ft facility with 40+ specialist tutors
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Physics at Improve ME

Achieving a Grade 9 requires strong conceptual understanding, confident mathematical skills, and polished exam technique. Our programme teaches every topic with the 'why' built in, builds calculation confidence from Week 1, and teaches how 6-mark questions are structured and how to show working for full marks.
Physics is widely considered the most challenging GCSE science because it combines abstract concepts with significant mathematical demand. Difficulty often comes down to approach: we address foundational maths gaps early and build from straightforward substitution to multi-step problem-solving.
Practise calculations daily; work through past papers under timed conditions (we build this in from early on); revise by topic with our structured lesson plans; and draw diagrams from memory — circuit diagrams, ray diagrams, and force diagrams appear frequently in exams.
Physics has the longest list of required formulae of the three sciences. Formulae you must memorise include speed, acceleration, force, weight, work done, kinetic energy, GPE, power, efficiency, charge, V=IR, P=IV, density, and (Triple) pressure and moments. We introduce each equation when the topic is taught and reinforce through repeated use.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum

  4. /English Language

GCSE English Language Tutoring in Dubai

GCSE English Language is one of the most universally sat qualifications in Dubai's British school community — and one of the most misunderstood. It is not built around a body of content to memorise. There are no units, no set texts, no formulas. The exam tests skills: how well your child reads and analyses unseen material, and how effectively they write under timed conditions. That distinction changes what good preparation looks like.

Book Free AssessmentView All English Language Courses
OVERVIEW

What the Exam Actually Tests

Both papers in GCSE English Language assess the same underlying objectives — reading comprehension and analysis, and the quality and control of a student's own writing. Paper 1 centres on fiction reading and creative writing; Paper 2 on non-fiction reading and transactional writing. The questions where students most commonly lose marks are the higher-tariff ones: Questions 3 and 4 in Paper 1, and Questions 4 and 5 in Paper 2. These require more than surface-level responses. They reward students who can construct an argument, control language deliberately, and demonstrate genuine insight into how a writer works.

GCSE
Qualification
Ages 14-16
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

What We Cover

Skills-based preparation: reading analysis, creative and transactional writing, and exam technique under timed conditions.

Reading & Analysis

  • •Unseen text analysis — language and structure
  • •Identifying writers' methods and effects
  • •Comparing viewpoints and perspectives
  • •Mark scheme alignment

Writing

  • •Creative writing — narrative and descriptive
  • •Transactional writing — letters, speeches, articles
  • •Planning frameworks and timed practice
  • •Vocabulary and sentence control

Exam Technique

  • •Timed writing under realistic conditions
  • •Immediate feedback and mark scheme review
  • •Homework from targeted question banks
  • •Progress tracking and parent updates
ASSESSMENT

How We Teach It

Every lesson is exam-focused. The first 10–15 minutes are spent on text analysis or targeted skill-building. The remaining 45 minutes are given to a timed writing task. Feedback is given immediately. We use worksheets adapted from real past papers alongside structured planning frameworks for the writing questions.

Paper 1
Creative Reading & Writing
  • •Unseen literature extract: analysis of language and structure
  • •Creative writing: narrative or descriptive
  • •Planning and execution under timed conditions
Paper 2
Writers' Viewpoints & Perspectives
  • •Non-fiction reading and comparison of two texts
  • •Transactional writing: letter, speech, article
  • •Evaluation and analysis of writers' methods
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Teaching Approach

01

Text Analysis & Skill Work

Sessions open with 10–15 minutes of text analysis or targeted skill work — breaking down how writers use language and structure, and connecting those choices to mark scheme criteria.

02

Timed Writing Every Session

The remaining 45 minutes are given to a timed writing task. Feedback is immediate. Students leave each session knowing specifically what they did well and where marks were lost.

03

Individual Review

Because our English groups have a maximum of six students, your child's written work is reviewed individually every session — not collected and returned a week later.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for English Language?

Teachers update a progress report after every session
Parents can see which topics were covered and homework submission status
Direct calls or messages follow if anything needs attention between sessions
A common assumption is that English Language suits only humanities-minded students. Reading comprehension at this level is structured analysis — the same kind of systematic thinking applied in other technical subjects. It is a skill, and it can be taught.
When to join: For Year 11, September is the ideal start. Year 10 joining from November are well-placed before mock season. Two sessions per week recommended for closing gaps; one session for consolidating.
GCSE English Language leads directly into A-Level English Language or IB English.
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about English Language at Improve ME

We support AQA, Edexcel, and OCR. AQA is used at schools including Jumeirah College. We will confirm your child's board at the assessment.
10–15 minutes of text analysis or skill work, then 45 minutes of timed writing. Feedback is immediate. Maximum six students per group.
Year 11: September is ideal. Year 10: November is well-placed before mocks. Two sessions per week for closing gaps; one for consolidating.

OVERVIEW

What is IGCSE Mathematics?

IGCSE Mathematics (Cambridge and Edexcel International) is assessed via two calculator papers. It develops number, algebra, geometry, trigonometry, and statistics across Core and Extended tiers. Extended tier targets grades A*–C (Cambridge) or 9–4 (Edexcel). At Improve ME, we teach to the exact specification your child's school uses, with past-paper practice and exam technique for top grades.

OVERVIEW

What is IGCSE Biology?

Qualification
Ages 14-16

COURSE CONTENT

IGCSE Biology Course Content

International specification coverage with clear structure and exam technique for A*–A.

Cell Biology and Infection

  • •Cell structure: animal, plant, bacterial, prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells
  • •Microscopy and calculating magnification
  • •Transport in cells: diffusion, osmosis, and active transport
  • •Cell division: the cell cycle, stages of mitosis, and cancer
  • •Stem cells: types, uses, and ethical considerations
  • •Communicable diseases: viral, bacterial, fungal, and protist pathogens
  • •Immune system: vaccination, antibiotics, and monoclonal antibodies (Triple)

Bioenergetics and Homeostasis

  • •Photosynthesis: rate factors, uses of glucose, and leaf structure

  • •Kidney function: dialysis and kidney failure

Ecology and Genetics

  • •Ecosystems: communities, populations, and interdependence
  • •Abiotic and biotic factors affecting distribution
  • •Nutrient cycles: carbon, water, and nitrogen
  • •Biodiversity, human impact, and conservation strategies
  • •DNA structure, transcription, and translation
  • •Genetic inheritance: Punnett squares, pedigree analysis, and alleles

Paper 2
Homeostasis, Inheritance, Ecology
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Tests homeostasis, genetics, evolution, and ecology
  • •Same question format as Paper 1
  • •Exam-technique focus on 6-mark extended-response questions

OVERVIEW

What is IGCSE Chemistry?

IGCSE Chemistry is assessed through structured papers and practical components. We build specification mastery and exam technique for international pathways.

IGCSE
Qualification
Ages 14-16
Age range
Cambridge • Edexcel
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

IGCSE Chemistry Course Content

Atomic Structure and Bonding

  • •Atomic structure: protons, neutrons, electrons, and isotopes
  • •Electronic configuration and the modern periodic table
  • •Ionic bonding: forming ions, dot-and-cross diagrams, and lattice properties
  • •Covalent bonding: simple molecules and their properties
  • •Giant covalent structures: diamond, graphite, graphene, and fullerenes
  • •Metallic bonding and properties of metals
  • •States of matter and changes of state
  • •Chemical calculations: relative formula mass, moles, and empirical formulae

Reactions and Energy

  • •Acids, alkalis, and the pH scale
  • •Reactions of acids: neutralisation, metal carbonates, and metal oxides
  • •Making salts: titration method, solubility rules, and insoluble salts
  • •Electrolysis: molten compounds, aqueous solutions, and half equations
  • •Reactivity series, displacement reactions, and metal extraction
  • •Rates of reaction: concentration, temperature, surface area, and catalysts
  • •Energy changes: exothermic and endothermic reactions, reaction profiles, and bond energy calculations
  • •Reversible reactions and dynamic equilibrium

Organic Chemistry and Analysis

  • •Crude oil, fractional distillation, and properties of hydrocarbons
  • •Alkanes and alkenes as homologous series
  • •Cracking and its industrial importance
  • •Reactions of alkenes and alcohols, and production of ethanol (Triple)
  • •Polymers and nanoparticles (Triple)
  • •Earth's atmosphere: formation, greenhouse effect, and climate change
  • •Atmospheric pollutants and water treatment
  • •Chemical analysis: flame tests, tests for cations, anions, and common gases
  • •Using resources: life-cycle assessments, ceramics, and composites (Triple)

  • •Heavy on calculation-based questions: moles, concentrations, and reacting masses
  • •Required practicals on titrations, electrolysis, and temperature changes
Paper 2
Rates, Organic, Analysis, Chemistry of Atmosphere
  • •1 hour 45 minutes (Triple) / 1 hour 15 minutes (Combined)
  • •Rates of reaction, organic chemistry, chemical analysis, atmospheric chemistry, and using resources
  • •Data interpretation and graph-reading questions are common

OVERVIEW

What is IGCSE Physics?

IGCSE

Cambridge • Edexcel
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

IGCSE Physics Course Content

Forces and Motion

  • •Scalar and vector quantities, distance, displacement, speed, velocity, and acceleration
  • •Distance-time and velocity-time graphs: interpretation and calculations
  • •Newton's three laws of motion, inertial mass, and weight
  • •Resultant forces, free-body diagrams, and vector resolution
  • •Terminal velocity and air resistance
  • •Momentum: conservation, change in momentum, and collisions
  • •Stopping distances: thinking distance, braking distance, and factors affecting both
  • •Moments, gears, and levers (Triple)

  • •Pressure in fluids and upthrust (Triple)

Energy, Electricity and Waves

  • •Energy stores and transfers, kinetic energy, and gravitational potential energy
  • •Conservation of energy, dissipation, and efficiency calculations
  • •Renewable and non-renewable energy sources and global demand
  • •Work done, power, and energy transfer calculations
  • •Transverse and longitudinal waves, wave equation, and properties
  • •Sound, ultrasound, infrasound, and seismic waves (Triple)
  • •Electromagnetic spectrum: types, uses, dangers, and ionising radiation
  • •Lenses, ray diagrams, and total internal reflection (Triple)
  • •Series and parallel circuits, Ohm's law, V=IR, and I-V characteristics
  • •Mains electricity, fuses, circuit breakers, and electrical safety
  • •Static electricity, electric fields, and earthing (Triple)

Magnetism and Atomic Structure

  • •Permanent and induced magnets, magnetic fields, and field lines
  • •Electromagnets: wire, flat coil, and solenoid
  • •Motor effect, Fleming's left-hand rule, and the electric motor
  • •Electromagnetic induction, transformers, and the National Grid
  • •Alternators, dynamos, loudspeakers, and microphones (Triple)
  • •Radioactivity: alpha, beta, and gamma radiation and their properties
  • •Atomic models: Dalton, Thomson, Rutherford, and Bohr
  • •Half-life, activity, and detecting radiation
  • •Uses of radioactivity and nuclear medicine (Triple)
  • •Nuclear fission, fusion, and nuclear power (Triple)
  • •Astronomy: solar system, stellar evolution, red shift, and CMBR (Triple)
  • •Particle model: density, specific heat capacity, specific latent heat, and gas pressure

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /IGCSE
  4. /English Language

IGCSE English Language Tutoring in Dubai

For the majority of students at international schools across Dubai, IGCSE English Language is the English qualification they will sit. Edexcel International IGCSE is the most widely used board across Dubai's international school landscape, and it is the qualification we prepare the most students for. We also support Cambridge International (CAIE) students. If your child attends a school following the IGCSE pathway and you want targeted, exam-focused support, this is the right page.

Book Free AssessmentView All English Language Courses
OVERVIEW

What the IGCSE English Language Exam Tests

IGCSE English Language is entirely skills-based. There are no set texts to memorise and no content knowledge to recall. The exam assesses your child's ability to read and respond to unseen material, and to produce writing that is clear, controlled, and purposeful. For Edexcel IGCSE specifically, the questions where students most commonly underperform are Questions 4 and 5 in Paper 1. These are the higher-tariff writing questions — and they are where focused preparation makes the most measurable difference. Grades are awarded on the A*–G scale, which differs from the 9–1 system used in GCSE. For students targeting strong UK or international university offers, the benchmark is typically an A or A*.

IGCSE
Qualification
Ages 14-16
Age range
Cambridge • Edexcel
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

What We Cover

Skills-based preparation: reading analysis, creative and transactional writing, and exam technique under timed conditions.

Reading & Analysis

  • •Unseen text analysis — language and structure
  • •Identifying writers' methods and effects
  • •Comparing viewpoints and perspectives
  • •Mark scheme alignment

Writing

  • •Creative writing — narrative and descriptive
  • •Transactional writing — letters, speeches, articles
  • •Planning frameworks and timed practice
  • •Vocabulary and sentence control

Exam Technique

  • •Timed writing under realistic conditions
  • •Immediate feedback and mark scheme review
  • •Homework from targeted question banks
  • •Progress tracking and parent updates
ASSESSMENT

How We Teach It

Every lesson is exam-focused. The first 10–15 minutes are spent on text analysis or targeted skill-building. The remaining 45 minutes are given to a timed writing task. Feedback is given immediately. We use worksheets adapted from real past papers alongside structured planning frameworks for the writing questions.

Paper 1
Creative Reading & Writing
  • •Unseen literature extract: analysis of language and structure
  • •Creative writing: narrative or descriptive
  • •Planning and execution under timed conditions
Paper 2
Writers' Viewpoints & Perspectives
  • •Non-fiction reading and comparison of two texts
  • •Transactional writing: letter, speech, article
  • •Evaluation and analysis of writers' methods
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

Edexcel International
Cambridge CAIE

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Teaching Approach

01

Text Analysis & Skill Work

Sessions open with 10–15 minutes of text analysis or targeted skill work — breaking down how writers use language and structure, and connecting those choices to mark scheme criteria.

02

Timed Writing Every Session

The remaining 45 minutes are given to a timed writing task. Feedback is immediate. Students leave each session knowing specifically what they did well and where marks were lost.

03

Individual Review

Because our English groups have a maximum of six students, your child's written work is reviewed individually every session — not collected and returned a week later.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for English Language?

Teachers update a progress report after every session
Parents can see which topics were covered and homework submission status
Direct calls or messages follow if anything needs attention between sessions
Many parents assume that English Language naturally suits those who lean towards the arts. Students who are primarily Maths or Science learners often take to the analytical dimension effectively once they understand the approach.
When to join: For Year 11, September is the ideal start. Year 10 joining from November are well-placed before mock season. Two sessions per week recommended for closing gaps; one session for consolidating.
GCSE English Language leads directly into A-Level English Language or IB English.
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about English Language at Improve ME

We work with Edexcel International and Cambridge CAIE. Edexcel is the most common board across Dubai's international schools.
10–15 minutes of text analysis or skill work, then 45 minutes of timed writing. Feedback is immediate. Maximum six students per group.
Year 11: September is ideal. Year 10: November is well-placed before mocks. Two sessions per week for closing gaps; one for consolidating.

What is IGCSE Business Studies?

title: A-Level Maths Tutoring Dubai | AQA, Edexcel & OCR
description: Excel in A-Level Mathematics with expert tutoring in Dubai. Small groups tailored to AQA, Edexcel and OCR exam boards. Book a free assessment!
html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Mathematics

A-Level Mathematics

Master Pure and Applied Mathematics — differentiation, integration, statistics and mechanics — with synoptic assessment and university-prep support.

Book Free AssessmentView All Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Mathematics?

A-Level Mathematics is one of the most widely required and respected qualifications for university entry in STEM, Economics, Finance, and Computing. It covers Pure Mathematics, Statistics, and Mechanics across three equally weighted papers, demanding both conceptual understanding and precise exam technique. In Dubai's competitive international school environment, students sitting A-Level Maths through AQA, Edexcel, or OCR face increasingly demanding papers where method marks alone are not enough — full marks require clear working, correct notation, and confident application. At Improve ME, our specialist Maths tutors combine deep subject knowledge with structured past paper practice and mark scheme mastery to consistently deliver A and A* outcomes.

Pure Mathematics

Proof, algebra, calculus (differentiation and integration), trigonometry, and sequences. The core of A-Level Maths and university preparation.

  • •Proof by deduction, exhaustion, and counter-example
  • •Algebra and functions: indices, surds, quadratics, transformations
  • •Coordinate geometry: straight lines, circles, parametric equations
  • •Sequences and series: binomial expansion, sigma notation
  • •Trigonometry: identities, radians, solving equations
  • •Differentiation and integration: chain, product, quotient rules; by parts; differential equations
  • •Numerical methods and vectors

Statistics & Mechanics

Applied mathematics: probability, distributions, hypothesis testing, kinematics, forces, and moments. Synoptic links with Pure.

  • •Statistics: sampling, probability, distributions, hypothesis testing, correlation and regression
  • •Mechanics: kinematics, Newton's Laws, moments, projectiles, connected particles
  • •Large data set preparation (Edexcel/AQA) for Paper 2
  • •Synoptic assessment and mark scheme discipline

Consider A-Level Further Mathematics

Students planning competitive STEM degrees should consider A-Level Further Mathematics alongside A-Level Maths.

A-Level Further Mathematics
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Mathematics Course Content

Advanced depth with step-by-step problem-solving, synoptic assessment preparation, and top-grade strategy for university.

Pure Mathematics

  • •Proof — proof by deduction, proof by exhaustion, disproof by counter-example
  • •Algebra and functions — indices, surds, quadratics, simultaneous equations, inequalities, functions and their graphs, transformations
  • •Coordinate geometry — straight lines, circles, parametric equations
  • •Sequences and series — binomial expansion, arithmetic and geometric sequences and series, sigma notation
  • •Trigonometry — sine and cosine rules, radians, arc length, sector area, trigonometric identities, solving trigonometric equations
  • •Exponentials and logarithms — laws of logarithms, natural log, exponential growth and decay
  • •Differentiation — first principles, chain rule, product rule, quotient rule, implicit differentiation, connected rates of change
  • •Integration — definite and indefinite integrals, integration by substitution, integration by parts, trapezium rule, differential equations
  • •Numerical methods — Newton-Raphson, iteration, locating roots
  • •Vectors — 2D and 3D vectors, vector equations of lines, scalar product

Statistics

  • •Statistical sampling — sampling methods, advantages and limitations
  • •Data presentation and interpretation — histograms, box plots, cumulative frequency, mean, standard deviation, outliers
  • •Probability — mutually exclusive and independent events, conditional probability, Venn diagrams, tree diagrams
  • •Statistical distributions — discrete and continuous distributions, binomial distribution, normal distribution, approximations
  • •Hypothesis testing — null and alternative hypotheses, one and two-tailed tests, critical regions, p-values, Type I and II errors
  • •Correlation and regression — scatter diagrams, product moment correlation coefficient, linear regression, interpolation and extrapolation

Mechanics

  • •Kinematics — constant and variable acceleration, displacement-time and velocity-time graphs, SUVAT equations
  • •Forces and Newton's Laws — resultant forces, equilibrium, Newton's three laws, friction, normal reaction
  • •Moments — taking moments, equilibrium of rigid bodies
  • •Projectiles — horizontal and vertical components, time of flight, range, maximum height
  • •Connected particles — Atwood's machine, particles on inclined planes, tension and thrust
  • •Variable acceleration — calculus methods: differentiating and integrating displacement, velocity, and acceleration functions
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Assessment is via three papers: Pure Mathematics 1, Pure Mathematics 2, and Statistics & Mechanics. We focus on differentiation, integration, and synoptic application.

Paper 1
Pure Mathematics
  • •2 hours, 100 marks — 33.3% of A-Level
  • •All pure topics: proof, algebra, calculus, trigonometry, sequences, vectors
  • •Mix of short and extended questions; clear working and correct notation essential
Paper 2
Pure Mathematics and Statistics
  • •2 hours, 100 marks — 33.3% of A-Level
  • •Further pure topics plus full statistics content
  • •Large data set pre-release (Edexcel/AQA) — familiarity required for statistics questions
Paper 3
Pure Mathematics and Mechanics
  • •2 hours, 100 marks — 33.3% of A-Level
  • •Further pure topics plus full mechanics content
  • •No formula booklet (AQA); formula booklet provided for Edexcel and OCR
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR
International A-Level (IAL) — Edexcel

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Mathematics Teaching Approach

01

Pure Mathematics Mastery

Pure Maths makes up two-thirds of A-Level Maths content. We build deep conceptual understanding of calculus, algebra, and trigonometry — not just procedure-following — so students can tackle the multi-step problem-solving questions that papers increasingly favour.

02

Statistics and Large Data Set Preparation

Statistics questions frequently reference the pre-release large data set. We ensure students are thoroughly familiar with its context and can apply statistical methods confidently to both familiar and unfamiliar data.

03

Mechanics from First Principles

Many students find Mechanics challenging because it requires both physical intuition and mathematical precision. We build understanding from Newton's Laws through to connected particles and projectiles, with regular past paper application.

04

Mark Scheme Discipline

A-Level Maths mark schemes reward specific working formats. We train students to present solutions in exactly the way examiners award method and accuracy marks — eliminating avoidable mark losses.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Mathematics?

Specialist A-Level Mathematics tutors with proven A and A* outcomes
Covers AQA, Edexcel, and OCR specifications in full
Dedicated large data set preparation for Edexcel and AQA Statistics
Further Mathematics support available alongside A-Level Maths
Mechanics taught from physical intuition through to exam application
Past paper practice integrated into every session from Year 12
Mark scheme training to eliminate avoidable working errors
Strong track record for students targeting STEM and Economics university courses
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Mathematics at Improve ME

A-Level Mathematics covers Pure Maths, Statistics, and Mechanics across three papers and is the standard qualification required for most STEM and Economics degrees. Further Mathematics is a separate, additional A-Level that extends into more advanced pure topics (complex numbers, matrices, further calculus), as well as further Statistics, Mechanics, or Decision Maths. Further Maths is highly recommended for students applying to Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, or Computer Science at top universities.
Ideally from the start of Year 12, so foundations are built correctly from the beginning. Many students who start with a solid GCSE grade find A-Level Maths significantly harder than expected — the jump in difficulty is steep. Starting tutoring early prevents bad habits from forming and builds confidence before the harder Year 13 content arrives.
Yes — for Edexcel and AQA students, the large data set is a compulsory component of the Statistics papers and questions that reference it appear every year. We ensure students understand the context of the data set thoroughly and can apply statistical methods to it confidently under exam conditions.
Absolutely. We tailor every student's sessions to their specific weak areas. Many students find Mechanics challenging because it requires a combination of physical reasoning and mathematical precision. We can focus entirely on Mechanics if that is what the student needs, building from Newton's Laws through to projectiles and connected particles.
The majority of our A-Level Maths students who join us at the start of Year 12 achieve A or A* grades. Students who join us in Year 13 for intensive preparation also see significant grade improvements, typically moving up one or two grade boundaries within a term.
