html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Further Mathematics

A-Level Further Mathematics

A-Level Further Mathematics is one of the most demanding and most respected qualifications available at pre-university level. It extends well beyond standard A-Level Maths into complex numbers, matrices, further calculus, polar coordinates, and hyperbolic functions in the Core Pure component, with additional content in Further Statistics, Further Mechanics, or Decision Mathematics depending on the exam board. Top universities including Oxford, Cambridge, Imperial, and UCL strongly prefer or require Further Maths for Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, and Computer Science degree programmes. At Improve ME, we are one of the few tutoring centres in Dubai able to offer specialist Further Mathematics tuition across all major exam boards — including the more demanding Edexcel Further Pure 2 and OCR MEI options.

Book Free AssessmentView All Further Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Further Mathematics?

A-Level Further Mathematics is one of the most demanding pre-university qualifications, extending well beyond standard A-Level Maths into Core Pure (complex numbers, matrices, further calculus, polar coordinates, hyperbolic functions, differential equations) and optional components in Further Statistics, Further Mechanics, or Decision Mathematics. Top universities including Oxford, Cambridge, Imperial, and UCL strongly prefer or require it for Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, and Computer Science. At Improve ME, we are one of the few Dubai centres offering specialist Further Mathematics tuition across all major exam boards, including Edexcel Further Pure 2 and OCR MEI.

University Requirements

Further Maths is essential or highly recommended for competitive STEM degrees and gives a clear advantage for physics, engineering, and computer science.

  • •Essential for: Cambridge/Oxford Mathematics, Imperial Engineering
  • •Highly recommended for: Top 10 university STEM courses
  • •Competitive advantage for: Physics, Engineering, Computer Science
  • •Demonstrates mathematical ability beyond standard A-Level
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Further Mathematics Course Content

Core Pure plus applied options: mechanics, statistics, and decision mathematics.

Core Pure Mathematics

  • •Complex numbers — Cartesian and modulus-argument form, Argand diagrams, De Moivre's theorem, roots of unity, loci in the complex plane
  • •Matrices — matrix algebra, determinants, inverse matrices, transformations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors
  • •Further algebra — proof by induction, roots of polynomials (Vieta's formulas), method of differences, partial fractions with repeated factors
  • •Further calculus — volumes of revolution, improper integrals, reduction formulas, Maclaurin and Taylor series
  • •Polar coordinates — polar curves, area enclosed by polar curves, converting between polar and Cartesian
  • •Hyperbolic functions — definitions, identities, inverse hyperbolic functions, integration involving hyperbolic functions
  • •Differential equations — first and second order ODEs, complementary function and particular integral, systems of differential equations
  • •Further vectors — vector equations of planes, intersection of lines and planes, distances

Further Statistics / Further Mechanics

  • •Further Statistics: Continuous random variables, probability density functions, cumulative distribution functions, expectation and variance; Chi-squared tests (goodness of fit, contingency tables); t-tests and F-tests; Poisson distribution; generating functions; confidence intervals
  • •Further Mechanics: Momentum and impulse — conservation of momentum, coefficient of restitution, oblique collisions; Circular motion — uniform and non-uniform, conical pendulum, banking; Centres of mass — laminas, composite bodies, toppling and sliding; Elastic strings and springs — Hooke's Law, energy in elastic strings, simple harmonic motion

Decision Mathematics (where applicable)

  • •Algorithms — sorting algorithms, bin packing, complexity
  • •Graph theory — Kruskal's and Prim's algorithms, Dijkstra's algorithm, travelling salesman problem, Chinese postman
  • •Linear programming — graphical method, simplex algorithm, integer programming
  • •Networks — critical path analysis, activity networks, float, Gantt charts
  • •Game theory — zero-sum games, mixed strategies, dominance
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Four papers: Core Pure Mathematics 1 & 2 (compulsory) plus two applied modules. Each paper 1.5 hours, 75 marks. We focus on synoptic links and university-prep depth.

Core Pure 1 & 2
Compulsory Core Pure Papers
  • •2 papers, 1 hour 30 minutes each — each worth 25% of the A-Level
  • •Core Pure 1 and Core Pure 2 cover complex numbers, matrices, further algebra and calculus, polar coordinates, hyperbolic functions, differential equations, further vectors
  • •Mix of short and extended questions; university-level mathematical reasoning required
Optional Components
Two Optional Component Papers
  • •2 optional papers — 1 hour 30 minutes each — each worth 25% of the A-Level
  • •Options: Further Statistics, Further Mechanics, or Decision Mathematics (board-dependent)
  • •Edexcel offers Further Pure 1 and 2 as optional components for the most mathematically ambitious students
Total
4 Papers, All Equally Weighted
  • •Total: 4 papers, all equally weighted at 25%
  • •OCR MEI offers an additional Further Pure with Technology paper using computer algebra systems
  • •We tailor preparation to the specific board and option combination
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Edexcel
OCR / OCR MEI

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Further Mathematics Teaching Approach

01

Core Pure Deep Dive

Core Pure is the heart of Further Maths and covers material that many students find genuinely difficult — eigenvalues, complex number loci, second order ODEs. We build understanding from first principles rather than pattern-matching, so students can handle the unfamiliar question styles that Further Maths papers increasingly include.

02

Option Module Specialisation

We identify which optional modules the student's school or exam board requires — Further Statistics, Further Mechanics, or Decision Maths — and build a tailored programme covering every topic in depth.

03

University Application Alignment

Many students taking Further Maths are targeting competitive university courses. We help students connect their Further Maths knowledge to their personal statement and ensure their subject knowledge is at the level top universities expect at interview.

04

Edexcel Further Pure 2 Support

FP2 is one of the most demanding pre-university Maths modules available. We have specialist tutors who can teach this content — a rare capability in Dubai.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Further Mathematics?

One of the very few tutoring centres in Dubai offering specialist Further Mathematics tuition
Covers Core Pure, Further Statistics, Further Mechanics, and Decision Mathematics
Edexcel Further Pure 2 specialist support available
OCR MEI specification support available
Strong track record for students targeting Oxford, Cambridge, Imperial, and UCL
Can be studied alongside standard A-Level Maths tuition in coordinated sessions
University application and interview preparation integrated for top Mathematics applicants
One-to-one sessions only — Further Maths demands individualised attention
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Further Mathematics at Improve ME

Yes — A-Level Further Mathematics must always be taken alongside A-Level Mathematics. The two qualifications share some content and Further Maths assumes full knowledge of the standard A-Level Maths curriculum. Most students study both simultaneously over Years 12 and 13.
Oxford and Cambridge typically require or strongly prefer Further Maths for Mathematics, Physics, and Engineering degrees. Imperial College London and UCL also prefer it for Mathematics and Engineering. Even where it is not a formal requirement, having Further Maths significantly strengthens a STEM university application and demonstrates mathematical maturity.
Alongside the two compulsory Core Pure papers, students choose two optional component papers from Further Statistics, Further Mechanics, and Decision Mathematics. The options available depend on the exam board. Edexcel also offers Further Pure 1 and 2 as optional components for the most able students.
Yes — Further Maths is substantially more demanding. Core Pure covers material such as eigenvalues, complex number loci, and second order differential equations that is typically first encountered in university Mathematics courses. Students need to be genuinely strong at standard A-Level Maths before taking on Further Maths content.
Yes — this is one of the most common situations we encounter. Many Dubai international schools do not have teachers qualified to teach Further Maths to A-Level standard. We provide the complete Further Maths programme from scratch, working through all Core Pure and optional module content with full past paper preparation.

description: Learn A-Level Biology with a specialist tutor in Dubai. Small group lessons tailored to AQA, OCR and Edexcel exam boards. Book a free assessment today.
html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Biology

A-Level Biology

Build fluency in biological molecules, genetics, gene expression, and ecosystems with synoptic and practical assessment support.

Book Free AssessmentView All Biology Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Biology?

A-Level Biology is the gateway qualification for Medicine, Dentistry, Veterinary Science, Biomedical Sciences, and Ecology. It is one of the most content-heavy A-Level subjects, covering cell biology, genetics, human physiology, ecology, and evolution in considerable depth across two years. In addition to content knowledge, A-Level Biology increasingly demands extended written responses, data analysis, and practical skills — all of which require dedicated preparation beyond classroom teaching. At Improve ME, our specialist Biology tutors combine thorough content delivery with the essay technique and data response skills that consistently separate A and A* candidates from the rest of the field.

A-Level
Qualification
Ages 16-18
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Biology Course Content

Advanced depth with step-by-step problem-solving, synoptic assessment preparation, and top-grade strategy for university.

Cells, Exchange & Transport

  • •Cell structure — prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, ultrastructure, cell fractionation, microscopy calculations
  • •Biological molecules — water, carbohydrates, lipids, proteins (structure, function, and testing); enzymes (mechanism, factors affecting rate, inhibition)
  • •Cell membranes and transport — fluid mosaic model, osmosis, diffusion, active transport, endocytosis, exocytosis
  • •Cell division — mitosis and the cell cycle, meiosis, significance of cell division in growth and reproduction
  • •Exchange surfaces — surface area to volume ratio, adaptations of exchange surfaces, gas exchange in mammals, fish, and insects
  • •Mass transport — haemoglobin (structure, oxygen dissociation curves, Bohr shift); xylem and phloem (structure and function); transpiration (cohesion-tension theory)

Genetics, Evolution & Ecosystems

  • •Nucleic acids — DNA and RNA structure, semi-conservative replication, transcription and translation, genetic code
  • •Genetic inheritance — monohybrid and dihybrid crosses, codominance, sex linkage, epistasis, Hardy-Weinberg principle
  • •Gene expression and regulation — totipotency, stem cells, gene expression in prokaryotes and eukaryotes, epigenetics, cancer
  • •Recombinant DNA technology — restriction enzymes, vectors, PCR, gel electrophoresis, genetic engineering applications
  • •Evolution and classification — natural selection, evidence for evolution, speciation, phylogenetics, classification systems
  • •Ecosystems — nutrient cycles (carbon and nitrogen), energy transfer and efficiency, succession, conservation and biodiversity

Control, Homeostasis & Reproduction

  • •Nervous system — neurones (structure and function), action potentials, synaptic transmission, the brain, reflexes
  • •Hormonal system — endocrine glands and hormones, insulin and glucagon in blood glucose regulation, adrenaline
  • •Homeostasis — principles of negative feedback, thermoregulation in mammals, osmoregulation (kidney structure and function, ADH)
  • •Plant responses — tropisms, auxin, gibberellins, commercial applications of plant hormones
  • •Immunity — specific and non-specific defence, B and T lymphocytes, primary and secondary immune responses, vaccines, monoclonal antibodies
  • •Reproduction — sexual and asexual reproduction, meiosis in context, IVF and cloning, ethical considerations

Unit Structure AQA A-Level Biology unit structure: Unit 1: Molecules, Diet, Transport and Health Unit 2: Cells, Development, Biodiversity and Conservation Unit 3: Practical Skills in Biology I Unit 4: Energy, Environment, Microbiology and Immunity Unit 5: Respiration, Internal Environment, Coordination and Gene Technology Unit 6: Practical Skills in Biology II Edexcel and OCR follow different unit structures. Cambridge A-Level Biology has its own paper and practical assessment format.

ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Three papers plus practical endorsement. We support synoptic assessment and the deep understanding examiners reward at A-Level.

Paper 1
Cells, Exchange, Transport, and Genetics
  • •2 hours, 91 marks — approximately 35% of A-Level
  • •Short and long answer, including extended writing
  • •Cell biology, biological molecules, exchange, transport, genetics and gene expression
Paper 2
Control, Homeostasis, Reproduction, and Ecosystems
  • •2 hours, 91 marks — approximately 35% of A-Level
  • •Short and long answer, including extended writing
  • •Nervous and hormonal systems, homeostasis, plant responses, immunity, reproduction, ecosystems
Paper 3
Practical Skills and Synoptic Content
  • •2 hours, 78 marks — approximately 30% of A-Level
  • •Structured questions, critical commentary on data, essay (choice of two) — 15-mark extended essay requires detailed, well-structured biological writing
  • •Practical Endorsement: pass/fail assessed in school, reported separately
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Biology Teaching Approach

01

Content Consolidation

A-Level Biology contains more factual content than almost any other A-Level. We use active recall, spaced repetition, and concept mapping to ensure students retain content reliably rather than losing marks through forgetting.

02

Extended Writing Technique

Papers 1, 2, and 3 all include extended writing questions worth 6–15 marks. We teach students to plan and write these responses with the specific biological vocabulary, logical sequencing, and completeness that mark schemes demand.

03

Data Response and Experimental Analysis

A-Level Biology papers routinely present unfamiliar experimental data for students to interpret. We train students to extract relevant information, apply their biological knowledge, and draw evidence-based conclusions — a skill that requires dedicated practice.

04

Practical Paper and Essay

Paper 3 includes both a critical commentary on experimental data and a choice of 15-mark essays. We prepare students specifically for both — covering common essay titles and the expected content for each.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Biology?

A-Level Biology specialists covering AQA, Edexcel Biology A, Edexcel Biology B, OCR Biology A, and OCR Biology B
Extended writing coaching for all 6-mark, 9-mark, and 15-mark questions
Paper 3 essay preparation — common titles and expected content
Data response and experimental analysis training
Content consolidation through active recall and spaced repetition
Strong Medicine, Dentistry, and Veterinary Science applicant outcomes
Practical Endorsement guidance and preparation
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Biology at Improve ME

Most UK medical schools require or strongly prefer Biology alongside Chemistry. Some accept one of Biology or Chemistry with the other being the remaining A-Level. All medical school applicants should check individual entry requirements carefully, but Biology A-Level is a near-universal expectation for Medicine.
Extended writing questions in A-Level Biology typically range from 6 marks (short paragraph) to 15 marks (full essay in Paper 3). The 15-mark essay in Paper 3 requires students to write a well-structured, comprehensive biological essay in approximately 20 minutes under exam conditions — a skill that requires significant practice.
A-Level Biology is one of the most content-heavy subjects at this level. The most effective approach is active recall — testing yourself rather than re-reading notes — combined with spaced repetition over time. We integrate these techniques into our tutoring sessions and set targeted recall tasks between sessions to maximise retention.
Yes — the 15-mark Paper 3 essay is one of the most distinctive and underperformed elements of A-Level Biology. We prepare students for the most commonly recurring essay titles, teach them the expected structure and content for each, and practise writing under timed conditions so they can produce a complete, well-structured essay within the time available.
Yes — Edexcel Biology B (Salters-Nuffield) is a context-led specification with a different approach to assessment compared to standard Edexcel Biology A. We have tutors familiar with the Salters-Nuffield approach and can support students on this pathway.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Chemistry

A-Level Chemistry

Master thermodynamics, organic synthesis, and spectroscopy with university-prep depth and synoptic assessment.

Book Free AssessmentView All Chemistry Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Chemistry?

A-Level Chemistry is one of the most demanding and most rewarding science qualifications, combining Physical, Organic, and Inorganic Chemistry with significant mathematical content across calculations, data analysis, and mechanisms. It is an essential prerequisite for Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Biochemistry, and Chemical Engineering worldwide, and is increasingly competitive in terms of grade requirements for these courses. In Dubai's international schools, A-Level Chemistry students face some of the most difficult exam papers produced by AQA, Edexcel, and OCR — papers where understanding mechanisms, balancing equations under pressure, and executing multi-step calculations accurately are the difference between grades. At Improve ME, our specialist Chemistry tutors bring both deep subject knowledge and precise exam technique to every session.

A-Level
Qualification
Ages 16-18
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Chemistry Course Content

Advanced depth with step-by-step problem-solving, synoptic assessment preparation, and top-grade strategy for university.

Physical Chemistry

  • •Atomic structure — subatomic particles, mass spectrometry, electron configuration, ionisation energy trends
  • •Amount of substance — the mole, molar mass, empirical and molecular formulae, ideal gas equation, titration calculations
  • •Bonding — ionic, covalent, metallic, intermolecular forces, VSEPR theory, polarisation, hydrogen bonding
  • •Energetics — enthalpy changes, Hess's Law, bond enthalpy calculations, Born-Haber cycles, entropy, Gibbs free energy
  • •Kinetics — rate of reaction, Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution, Arrhenius equation, reaction mechanisms, rate equations, orders of reaction, half-life
  • •Chemical equilibria — Le Chatelier's principle, Kc and Kp expressions and calculations, acid-base equilibria, pH calculations, buffer solutions, Kw and Ka
  • •Electrochemistry — standard electrode potentials, electrochemical cells, electrolysis calculations, Faraday's laws

Organic Chemistry

  • •Nomenclature and isomerism — IUPAC naming, structural, geometric, and optical isomerism
  • •Alkanes and alkenes — free radical substitution, electrophilic addition, Markovnikov's rule, polymerisation
  • •Halogenoalkanes — nucleophilic substitution (SN1 and SN2), elimination reactions, hydrolysis
  • •Alcohols — oxidation, dehydration, esterification, tests for alcohols
  • •Carbonyl compounds — aldehydes and ketones: nucleophilic addition, reduction, oxidation, Tollens' and Fehling's tests
  • •Carboxylic acids and derivatives — acyl chlorides, esters, amides: reactions and synthesis
  • •Aromatic chemistry — electrophilic substitution, nitration, Friedel-Crafts reactions, directing effects
  • •Amines — basicity, nucleophilic properties, formation of ammonium salts, diazonium salts, coupling reactions
  • •Polymers — condensation and addition polymers, biodegradability, amino acids and proteins
  • •Spectroscopy and chromatography — mass spectrometry, IR spectroscopy, NMR (1H), chromatography techniques

Inorganic Chemistry

  • •Periodicity — trends across Period 3 in atomic radius, ionisation energy, electronegativity, melting points, oxides and chlorides
  • •Group 2 — physical and chemical properties, reactions with water and acids, uses of compounds, thermal decomposition of carbonates and nitrates
  • •Group 7 (Halogens) — physical and chemical properties, reactivity and displacement reactions, disproportionation, uses of chlorine, halide tests
  • •Transition metals — electronic configuration, variable oxidation states, formation of coloured ions, complex ions, ligand substitution, catalytic properties
  • •Reactions in aqueous solution — precipitation reactions, complex ion formation, amphoteric hydroxides, qualitative analysis
  • •Industrial chemistry — Haber Process, Contact Process, extraction of metals, green chemistry principles
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Three papers plus practical endorsement. We support synoptic assessment and the deep understanding examiners reward at A-Level.

Paper 1
Physical and Inorganic Chemistry
  • •2 hours, 105 marks — 35% of A-Level
  • •Short and extended answer questions including calculations
  • •Atomic structure, bonding, periodicity, Group 2 & 7, transition metals
Paper 2
Physical and Organic Chemistry
  • •2 hours, 105 marks — 35% of A-Level
  • •Short and extended answer questions including mechanisms and synthesis
  • •Energetics, kinetics, equilibria, organic mechanisms, spectroscopy
Paper 3
General and Practical Principles
  • •2 hours, 90 marks — 30% of A-Level
  • •Multiple choice, practical skills, data analysis, and synoptic questions across the full specification
  • •Practical Endorsement: pass/fail assessed in school, reported separately alongside the A-Level grade
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Chemistry Teaching Approach

01

Mechanism Mastery

Organic reaction mechanisms are the single most commonly examined area in A-Level Chemistry and the area where most marks are lost. We teach every mechanism — nucleophilic substitution, electrophilic addition, electrophilic aromatic substitution — until students can draw them perfectly from memory under exam conditions.

02

Calculation Precision

Physical Chemistry is heavily calculation-based — pH, Kc, Kp, electrode potential, enthalpy, and rate calculations all appear across Papers 1 and 2. We drill these calculations systematically with a focus on method, unit handling, and significant figures.

03

Practical Paper Preparation

Paper 3 practical skills questions are highly predictable but frequently underperformed. We cover experimental design, data analysis, uncertainty calculations, and evaluation techniques as a specific and recurring part of every student's programme.

04

Synoptic Thinking

Paper 3 also includes synoptic questions requiring students to link content from across the specification. We build this connective understanding deliberately rather than treating topics as isolated units.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Chemistry?

Chemistry specialists with A-Level teaching experience across AQA, Edexcel, and OCR
Organic mechanism drilling until exam-ready — the highest-value exam skill at A-Level
Physical Chemistry calculation precision — pH, Kc, Kp, Born-Haber, Arrhenius
Paper 3 practical skills and synoptic question preparation
Strong Medicine, Dentistry, and Pharmacy applicant outcomes
Covers Salters Chemistry (OCR B) as well as standard OCR
Past paper programmes from Year 12 with mark scheme discipline
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Chemistry at Improve ME

Most students find Organic Chemistry mechanisms and Physical Chemistry calculations the most challenging areas. Mechanisms require precise recall and accurate drawing under time pressure, while calculations demand multi-step mathematical reasoning with no margin for unit or rounding errors. These are the areas we prioritise in our sessions.
Yes — Chemistry is required or strongly preferred by virtually all UK medical schools. Most require Chemistry as one of three A-Levels, and many also prefer Biology. The grade requirement for Medicine is typically A*AA or AAA, making the Chemistry grade particularly high-stakes.
Yes — Paper 3 practical skills questions are a distinct and predictable component of A-Level Chemistry that many students underprepare for. We cover experimental design, data processing, uncertainty analysis, and evaluation techniques as a regular part of our Chemistry programme, not as an afterthought.
Ideally from the beginning of Year 12. A-Level Chemistry introduces a significant amount of new content very quickly, and gaps that form in the early months of Year 12 compound as the course progresses. Starting early allows foundations to be built correctly and prevents panic in Year 13.
Yes — Salters Chemistry is a context-led specification that differs significantly from standard OCR Chemistry in its approach and assessment style. We have tutors familiar with the Salters specification and can support students following this pathway.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Physics

A-Level Physics

Build deep understanding in particle physics, fields, simple harmonic motion, and options—with synoptic assessment and practical skills.

Book Free AssessmentView All Physics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Physics?

A-Level Physics is one of the most demanding and most valuable science qualifications available, providing the foundation for university study in Engineering, Medicine, Physics, and Computer Science. The course covers mechanics, waves, electricity, quantum physics, nuclear physics, and fields — combining deep conceptual understanding with significant mathematical application. In Dubai's international schools, students sit A-Level Physics through AQA, Edexcel, or OCR, all of which place increasing emphasis on multi-step calculation problems, practical skills assessment, and synoptic questions in Paper 3. At Improve ME, our specialist Physics tutors build both the conceptual clarity and the mathematical precision that A and A* Physics grades require.

A-Level
Qualification
Ages 16-18
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Physics Course Content

Advanced depth with step-by-step problem-solving, synoptic assessment preparation, and top-grade strategy for university.

Mechanics, Materials & Waves

  • •Measurements and their errors — SI units, scalars and vectors, random and systematic errors, uncertainty calculations
  • •Particles and radiation — atomic structure, stable and unstable nuclei, particles and antiparticles, the standard model, electromagnetic radiation and quantum phenomena
  • •Waves — progressive waves, longitudinal and transverse waves, reflection and refraction, superposition, interference, diffraction, standing waves
  • •Mechanics and materials — moments, equilibrium, projectile motion, Newton's Laws, work, energy, power, materials (stress, strain, Young's modulus, elastic and plastic deformation)
  • •Electricity — current, potential difference, resistance, Ohm's Law, resistivity, I-V characteristics, series and parallel circuits, EMF and internal resistance, potential dividers

Fields, Nuclear & Thermal Physics

  • •Further mechanics and thermal physics — circular motion, simple harmonic motion, forced oscillations and resonance, thermal energy transfer, ideal gases, molecular kinetic theory
  • •Fields — gravitational fields (Newton's Law of gravitation, field strength, gravitational potential, orbital motion); electric fields (Coulomb's Law, field strength, electric potential, capacitance, charging and discharging)
  • •Magnetic fields — magnetic flux density, force on a current-carrying conductor, electromagnetic induction, Faraday's and Lenz's Laws, transformers, AC generation
  • •Nuclear physics — radioactive decay, activity, half-life, nuclear instability, binding energy, fission and fusion, mass-energy equivalence

Practical Skills & Paper 3

  • •Required practical skills — 12 AQA required practicals covering circuits, mechanics, waves, and nuclear physics; assessed indirectly through written papers
  • •Practical skills assessed in Paper 3 — planning investigations, identifying variables, processing and presenting data, evaluating results and conclusions, identifying sources of error
  • •Synoptic questions — Paper 3 includes questions drawing on content from across the full specification; students must connect concepts from different topic areas
  • •Mathematical skills — algebra, trigonometry, logarithms, exponentials, and graphical analysis throughout all papers
  • •Data analysis — interpreting graphs, calculating gradients, understanding logarithmic scales, analysing experimental data
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Three papers plus practical endorsement. We support synoptic assessment and the deep understanding examiners reward at A-Level.

Paper 1
Mechanics, Materials, Waves, and Electricity
  • •2 hours, 85 marks — 34% of A-Level
  • •Short and long answer questions on mechanics, materials, waves, and electricity
  • •Full specification coverage for these topic areas
Paper 2
Further Mechanics, Thermal Physics, Fields, and Nuclear
  • •2 hours, 85 marks — 34% of A-Level
  • •Short and long answer questions on further mechanics, thermal physics, fields, and nuclear physics
  • •Structured and extended response; mathematical application throughout
Paper 3
Practical Skills and Options
  • •2 hours, 80 marks — 32% of A-Level
  • •Section A: practical skills and data analysis (planning, processing data, evaluating results)
  • •Section B: optional topic (Medical Physics, Engineering Physics, Turning Points, or Astrophysics depending on board)
  • •Practical Endorsement: separate pass/fail based on school practical work — reported alongside the A-Level grade
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Physics Teaching Approach

01

Conceptual Clarity First

Physics cannot be learned through procedure-following alone. We build genuine understanding of every concept — from Newton's Laws to quantum phenomena — so students can apply their knowledge to the unfamiliar scenarios that Paper 3 and synoptic questions present.

02

Mathematical Application

A-Level Physics is approximately 40% mathematical in its assessment. We ensure students are fluent in the relevant mathematical skills — rearranging equations, resolving vectors, using logarithms, analysing graphs — so mathematical weakness never costs Physics marks.

03

Practical Paper Preparation

Paper 3 practical skills questions are predictable in format but poorly prepared for by most students. We systematically cover uncertainty calculations, graph analysis, experimental design, and error evaluation until students can tackle any practical question confidently.

04

Option Topic Specialisation

Paper 3 includes an optional topic (Medical Physics, Astrophysics, etc.). We prepare students specifically for their chosen option with targeted content and past paper questions.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Physics?

Specialist A-Level Physics tutors covering all major exam boards
AQA, Edexcel, OCR, and OCR B (Advancing Physics) all supported
Paper 3 practical skills and data analysis preparation
Option topic preparation — Medical Physics, Astrophysics, Engineering Physics
Mathematical skills development integrated into every session
Past paper practice from Year 12 with mark scheme discipline
Strong outcomes for students targeting Engineering and Medicine
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Physics at Improve ME

A-Level Physics is significantly more mathematical than GCSE Physics. Approximately 40% of marks across the three papers involve mathematical skills — algebra, trigonometry, logarithms, exponentials, and graphical analysis. Students who are strong at Maths tend to perform better at Physics, and we integrate mathematical skill development into our Physics sessions wherever relevant.
The Practical Endorsement is a separate pass/fail qualification based on practical work carried out in school. It does not affect the A-Level grade itself but is reported alongside it on UCAS. Universities do check this, and a fail can affect offers. The written papers assess practical skills indirectly through Paper 3.
The optional topics available depend on the exam board. AQA offers Astrophysics, Medical Physics, Engineering Physics, Turning Points in Physics, and Electronics. Edexcel has different options including Astrophysics and Nuclear and Particle Physics. OCR offers similar choices. We prepare students for whichever option their school has selected.
Ideally from the start of Year 12. The jump from GCSE to A-Level Physics is significant — both in content depth and mathematical demand. Starting early allows gaps to be identified and addressed before the harder Year 13 content arrives and before university applications are submitted.
Yes — this is one of the most common situations we encounter. We can deliver the entire A-Level Physics programme from scratch, covering all content, running timed mock papers, and preparing students fully for all three papers and the practical skills assessment.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Business

A-Level Business

Excel in data response and essay papers with evaluation, application to context, and synoptic assessment for university.

Book Free AssessmentView All Business Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Business?

A-Level Business Studies develops analytical, evaluative, and strategic thinking through the study of how real businesses operate, make decisions, and respond to change. It covers marketing, finance, operations, human resources, and strategic management, combining quantitative calculations with extended evaluative writing. A-Level Business is highly valued by universities for Business, Economics, Management, and Finance degree programmes. In Dubai's international schools, the subject is examined through AQA, Edexcel, and OCR — all of which place significant weight on case study analysis, data interpretation, and the ability to construct and justify a well-reasoned argument. At Improve ME, our Business Studies tutors build the analytical frameworks and evaluation skills that distinguish A and A* candidates.

A-Level
Qualification
Ages 16-18
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Business Course Content

Advanced depth with step-by-step problem-solving, synoptic assessment preparation, and top-grade strategy for university.

Business Decisions & Financial Management

  • •Business objectives — profit, growth, market share, social enterprise, stakeholder conflict
  • •Decision making — scientific decision making, decision trees, expected values, investment appraisal (NPV, IRR, payback)
  • •Financial statements — income statements, balance sheets, cash flow statements (construction and interpretation)
  • •Ratio analysis — profitability, liquidity, efficiency, gearing ratios (calculation, interpretation, and limitations)
  • •Sources of finance — internal, external, short and long-term (advantages and limitations)
  • •Financial planning — break-even analysis, budgeting, variance analysis, working capital management
  • •Business strategy — Ansoff Matrix, Porter's Five Forces, SWOT analysis, strategic direction and competitive advantage

Marketing, People & Operations

  • •Market research — primary and secondary, quantitative and qualitative, sampling methods
  • •Marketing strategy — segmentation, targeting, positioning, the marketing mix (7Ps), digital marketing
  • •Product — product life cycle, Boston Matrix, new product development, branding
  • •Pricing strategies — cost-plus, penetration, skimming, predatory, psychological pricing
  • •Human resource management — recruitment and selection, training and development, motivation theories (Taylor, Maslow, Herzberg, McClelland)
  • •Organisational structure — hierarchies, spans of control, delegation, centralisation vs decentralisation, matrix structures
  • •Operations management — lean production, JIT, TQM, capacity utilisation, productivity, quality management

The External Environment & Evaluation Skills

  • •Economic environment — business cycle, inflation, interest rates, exchange rates (impact on business decisions)
  • •Globalisation — multinational corporations, offshoring, trade barriers, ethical trading, emerging markets
  • •Technology — impact of digital technology, e-commerce, automation, big data, artificial intelligence on business
  • •Ethics and corporate social responsibility — stakeholder theory, ethical trading, sustainability, triple bottom line
  • •Legal environment — employment law, consumer protection, competition law (impact on business strategy)
  • •Evaluation and judgement — constructing a justified conclusion, weighing up arguments, considering context, avoiding assertion
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Assessment includes data response and essay papers, with synoptic elements at A-Level. We build evaluation and application to context for university prep.

Paper 1
Business Decisions and Marketing
  • •2 hours, 100 marks — 33%
  • •Data response and essay questions on themes 1 and 2
  • •25-mark essay questions requiring evaluation, justification, and supported conclusion
Paper 2
Business Activities, Decisions, and Strategy
  • •2 hours, 100 marks — 33%
  • •Data response and essay questions on themes 3 and 4
  • •25-mark essays; quantitative skills (investment appraisal, ratio analysis, break-even)
Paper 3
Integrated Business
  • •2 hours, 100 marks — 33%
  • •Pre-released case study on a real or realistic business; all questions based on the case study
  • •All three papers include 25-mark essay questions requiring evaluation and justified conclusion
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Business Teaching Approach

01

Case Study Analysis Technique

The Paper 3 pre-released case study is one of the most important elements of A-Level Business. We work through the case study in depth, identifying the key business issues, financial data, and strategic context — so students enter the exam with confident, informed answers rather than generic ones.

02

25-Mark Essay Framework

25-mark essays account for a significant proportion of total marks across all three papers and require a specific structure — analysis of both sides, use of evidence and context, and a justified conclusion. We teach and practise this framework until students can produce a high-quality 25-mark response reliably within 35 minutes.

03

Quantitative Skills

A-Level Business includes a significant quantitative component — investment appraisal, ratio analysis, break-even, and financial statement construction. We ensure students are confident with all calculation types and can interpret results in a business context.

04

Evaluation and Judgement

The highest marks in Business Studies are reserved for genuine evaluation — considering context, weighing competing arguments, and drawing justified conclusions. We develop this skill deliberately in every session rather than treating it as something students either have or do not.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Business?

A-Level Business Studies specialists covering AQA, Edexcel, and OCR
Pre-released case study preparation for Paper 3
25-mark essay framework training — the highest-value exam skill
Quantitative skills — investment appraisal, ratio analysis, break-even
Evaluation and judgement development integrated into every session
Strong outcomes for students targeting Business and Economics degrees
Past paper programme from Year 12 with mark scheme discipline
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Business at Improve ME

Paper 3 is based on a pre-released case study about a real or realistic business, released several months before the exam. All questions in Paper 3 are based on this business and its context. Students who prepare thoroughly for the case study — understanding the business, its industry, its financials, and its strategic position — have a significant advantage in Paper 3.
Extremely important. Each of the three papers includes at least one 25-mark essay question, meaning they collectively account for a very significant proportion of the total marks available. Consistently performing well on 25-mark essays is the single most important factor in achieving an A or A* in A-Level Business.
There is a quantitative component — typically around 10–15% of marks — covering investment appraisal (payback, ARR, NPV), ratio analysis, break-even, and financial statement interpretation. Students do not need to be strong mathematicians, but they do need to be accurate and confident with these specific calculation types.
Yes — A-Level Business Studies is well-regarded for Business, Management, Marketing, Finance, and Economics degree applications. It demonstrates analytical and evaluative thinking that university admissions tutors value. For students applying to competitive business schools, combining Business with Economics or Mathematics strengthens the application further.
Business Studies focuses on how individual firms operate, make decisions, and compete — with a strong emphasis on management, marketing, and strategy. Economics focuses on broader market and macroeconomic theory — supply and demand, inflation, fiscal policy, international trade. Many strong students take both, as they complement each other well.

title: A-Level Economics Tutor Dubai | AQA, Edexcel & OCR
description: Improve your A-Level Economics grade with expert small group tutoring in Dubai. Exam-focused lessons for AQA, Edexcel and OCR. Book a free assessment!
html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Economics

A-Level Economics

Excel in data response and essay papers with evaluation, application to context, and synoptic assessment for university.

Book Free AssessmentView All Economics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Economics?

A-Level Economics is one of the most intellectually demanding and highly regarded A-Level subjects, combining rigorous microeconomic and macroeconomic theory with real-world application, data analysis, and extended evaluative writing. It is highly valued by universities for PPE, Economics, Finance, Law, and Business degree applications, and is increasingly competitive in terms of entry requirements for these courses. In Dubai's international schools, A-Level Economics students sit papers through AQA, Edexcel, or OCR — all of which reward precise diagram drawing, chain-of-reasoning analysis, and well-evaluated 25-mark essays that go beyond simply describing economic theory. At Improve ME, our Economics tutors build the technical foundations and evaluative sophistication that A and A* Economics grades demand.

A-Level
Qualification
Ages 16-18
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Economics Course Content

Advanced depth with step-by-step problem-solving, synoptic assessment preparation, and top-grade strategy for university.

Microeconomics

  • •Supply and demand — market equilibrium, shifts in supply and demand, price mechanism, consumer and producer surplus
  • •Elasticity — price elasticity of demand and supply, income elasticity, cross elasticity (calculations, determinants, applications)
  • •Market failure — public goods, merit and demerit goods, externalities (positive and negative), information asymmetry, market power
  • •Government intervention — taxes, subsidies, price controls, regulation, tradeable permits (analysis and evaluation)
  • •Theory of the firm — short run and long run production, costs (fixed, variable, total, average, marginal), economies and diseconomies of scale
  • •Market structures — perfect competition, monopolistic competition, oligopoly (game theory, price rigidity), monopoly (profit maximisation, efficiency comparisons)
  • •Labour markets — derived demand, wage determination, monopsony, trade unions, discrimination, minimum wage

Macroeconomics

  • •National income — GDP measurement methods, circular flow, national income statistics and their limitations
  • •Economic growth — actual and potential growth, output gaps, economic cycle, factors affecting long-run growth
  • •Inflation — CPI and RPI measurement, demand-pull and cost-push inflation, quantity theory of money, effects and costs of inflation
  • •Unemployment — types (frictional, structural, cyclical, seasonal), natural rate, costs of unemployment, hysteresis
  • •Balance of payments — current account components, causes of deficits and surpluses, Marshall-Lerner condition, J-curve effect
  • •Exchange rates — determination, fixed vs floating systems, factors causing changes, impact on macroeconomic objectives
  • •Fiscal policy — government spending, taxation, budget deficit and national debt, Laffer curve, multiplier and accelerator
  • •Monetary policy — interest rates, money supply, quantitative easing, inflation targeting, Bank of England independence
  • •Supply-side policies — labour market reforms, privatisation, deregulation, education and training (analysis and evaluation)
  • •International trade and globalisation — comparative advantage, free trade vs protectionism, WTO, trading blocs, current global trade issues

Evaluation, Data Response & Essay Technique

  • •Chain of reasoning — building multi-step economic arguments that connect cause and effect through theory and evidence
  • •Diagram mastery — drawing and annotating AD/AS, supply and demand, cost curves, market structure diagrams accurately under time pressure
  • •Data response technique — extracting relevant information from economic data, applying theory, avoiding description
  • •25-mark essay structure — introduction with definition, balanced analysis of both sides with diagrams, evaluation using real-world context, justified conclusion
  • •Evaluation skills — considering short run vs long run, depends on elasticity/magnitude, ceteris paribus limitations, conflicts between objectives
  • •Real-world application — using current economic events, government policies, and real firms and countries to support arguments
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Assessment includes data response and essay papers, with synoptic elements at A-Level. We build evaluation and application to context for university prep.

Paper 1
Markets and Market Failure (Microeconomics)
  • •2 hours, 80 marks — 33%
  • •Section A: data response (choose 1 of 2); Section B: essay questions (choose 1 of 3), including a 25-mark essay
  • •Diagram and chain-of-reasoning technique
Paper 2
National and International Economy (Macroeconomics)
  • •2 hours, 80 marks — 33%
  • •Same format as Paper 1
  • •Macroeconomic theory, data response, and 25-mark essay
Paper 3
Themes in Economics
  • •2 hours, 80 marks — 33%
  • •Section A: multiple choice (30 marks); Section B: data response drawing on both micro and macroeconomics; 30-mark essay
  • •Board-specific weighting and question format
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Economics Teaching Approach

01

Diagram Precision Training

Economics diagrams are worth marks in virtually every question. We drill students on drawing and annotating all key diagrams — AD/AS, supply and demand shifts, cost curves, market structure diagrams — accurately and quickly until they can produce them perfectly under time pressure.

02

Chain of Reasoning

The difference between a C and an A in Economics is almost always in the quality of analytical chains. We teach students to build multi-step arguments that move from cause through mechanism to effect, supported by theory and evidence — the structure that examiners explicitly reward.

03

25-Mark Essay Coaching

25-mark essays appear in all three papers and require a specific approach — balanced analysis, integrated diagrams, real-world examples, and a justified evaluative conclusion. We practise these essays regularly from Year 12, building speed and quality simultaneously.

04

Real-World Current Affairs

Top Economics responses use real-world context — recent government policies, current inflation data, trade disputes. We help students build a bank of relevant examples and keep their knowledge of current economic events up to date.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Economics?

A-Level Economics specialists covering AQA, Edexcel, and OCR
Diagram mastery training — all key micro and macro diagrams
Chain of reasoning development — the core analytical skill in Economics
25-mark essay framework coaching across all three papers
Data response technique for Section A questions
Real-world current affairs integration into every session
Strong outcomes for PPE, Economics, and Finance applicants
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Economics at Improve ME

Chain of reasoning is the ability to build a multi-step economic argument — connecting a cause to its effects through clear economic logic, theory, and evidence. It is the single most important analytical skill in A-Level Economics because mark schemes explicitly reward it. Students who describe economic events or theories without building analytical chains consistently underperform relative to their knowledge.
Yes — while the exam does not formally test current affairs, the highest-scoring responses use real-world examples to support and illustrate economic arguments. Students who can reference actual government policies, real inflation data, or genuine trade examples consistently score higher than those who rely only on theoretical scenarios.
Diagrams are fundamental to A-Level Economics. They appear in data response questions, essays, and short-answer questions across all three papers. A well-drawn, correctly annotated diagram can earn multiple marks on its own. A diagram that is incorrect or missing in a response where one is expected can cost marks significantly. We treat diagram mastery as a non-negotiable foundation skill.
There is a quantitative element — particularly in microeconomics (elasticity calculations, cost calculations, multiplier) and macroeconomics (index numbers, balance of payments data). Paper 3 includes multiple choice questions that test quantitative reasoning. However, Economics is primarily an analytical and evaluative subject rather than a mathematical one.
Economics focuses on broader market theory and macroeconomic policy — supply and demand, market failure, inflation, fiscal and monetary policy. Business Studies focuses on how individual firms operate and make decisions. They are highly complementary and many students take both. Economics is generally considered the more theoretically rigorous of the two.

html:
1. Home
  2. /Secondary Curriculum
  3. /A-Level
  4. /Psychology

A-Level Psychology

Build confidence and achieve strong results in A-Level Psychology with expert support.

Book Free AssessmentView All Psychology Courses
OVERVIEW

What is A-Level Psychology?

A-Level Psychology is one of the most popular A-Level subjects, combining scientific research methodology with genuinely fascinating content — social influence, memory, attachment, psychopathology, forensic psychology, and the science of human behaviour. Despite its perceived accessibility, A-Level Psychology is demanding at the higher grades because it requires students to demonstrate detailed study knowledge, critical evaluation, and the ability to apply psychological concepts to novel scenarios — all within timed conditions. At Improve ME, our Psychology tutors build the study knowledge, evaluative depth, and essay structure that distinguish A and A* candidates from those who know the content but cannot translate it into marks.

A-Level
Qualification
Ages 16-18
Age range
AQA • Edexcel • OCR
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

A-Level Psychology Course Content

Advanced depth with step-by-step problem-solving, synoptic assessment preparation, and top-grade strategy for university.

Core Topics (all exam boards)

  • •Social influence — Asch's conformity research, Milgram's obedience studies, types and explanations of conformity and obedience, minority influence, social change
  • •Memory — multi-store model, working memory model, types of long-term memory, eyewitness testimony, cognitive interview
  • •Attachment — caregiver-infant interactions, Schaffer's stages, animal studies (Lorenz, Harlow), Strange Situation, Bowlby's theory, effects of institutionalisation
  • •Psychopathology — definitions of abnormality, characteristics and explanations of phobias, depression, and OCD; treatments (systematic desensitisation, CBT, drug therapy)
  • •Approaches in Psychology — Learning, Cognitive, Biological, Psychodynamic, Humanistic, Evolutionary (origins, assumptions, strengths and limitations)
  • •Biopsychology — nervous system, neurons and synaptic transmission, endocrine system, fight or flight, localisation of brain function, lateralisation, split-brain research
  • •Research Methods — experimental design, variables, sampling, ethics, data types, statistical tests, levels of measurement, inferential statistics, report writing

Optional Topics (Year 13, AQA)

  • •Issues and Debates — gender and culture bias, free will vs determinism, nature vs nurture, holism vs reductionism, idiographic vs nomothetic, ethical implications
  • •Relationships — evolutionary explanations of partner preference, factors affecting attraction, theories of romantic relationships, virtual and parasocial relationships
  • •Schizophrenia — classification, clinical characteristics, biological and psychological explanations, drug therapy, CBT, family therapy, token economy
  • •Forensic Psychology — defining and measuring crime, offender profiling, biological and psychological explanations of offending, custodial sentencing, rehabilitation
  • •Addiction — describing addiction, risk factors, neurological and learning theory explanations, smoking and gambling case studies, reducing addiction
  • •Cognition and Development — Piaget, Vygotsky, Baillargeon, theory of mind, autism, Selman's levels of perspective-taking

Research Methods & Exam Technique in Depth

  • •Experimental methods — lab, field, natural, and quasi-experiments (advantages, limitations, when to use each)
  • •Non-experimental methods — observations, case studies, interviews, questionnaires, correlations (design, strengths, limitations)
  • •Statistical testing — choosing the correct test, interpreting results, critical values, Type I and II errors
  • •AO1 / AO2 / AO3 distinction — describing studies and theories (AO1), applying to novel scenarios (AO2), evaluating and analysing (AO3)
  • •16-mark essay structure — AO1 description, AO3 evaluation (breadth and depth), specialist terminology, effective use of evidence
  • •Application questions — using psychological knowledge to explain novel scenarios (key AO2 skill)
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

Assessment is built around topic mastery, regular testing, and exam-style practice where applicable.

Paper 1
Introductory Topics in Psychology
  • •2 hours, 96 marks — 33%
  • •Social Influence, Memory, Attachment, Psychopathology
  • •16-mark extended essays appear across all three papers
Paper 2
Psychology in Context
  • •2 hours, 96 marks — 33%
  • •Approaches, Biopsychology, Research Methods
  • •Research Methods questions can appear anywhere across all three papers
Paper 3
Issues and Options in Psychology
  • •2 hours, 96 marks — 33%
  • •Issues and Debates plus three optional topics
  • •16-mark essays are the most mark-dense questions
EXAM BOARDS

Exam Boards We Cover

AQA
Pearson Edexcel
OCR

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our A-Level Psychology Teaching Approach

01

Study Knowledge and Application

A-Level Psychology requires students to know specific studies in detail — not just names and findings, but methodology, results, conclusions, and evaluative points. We teach these systematically and then train students to apply them to novel scenarios (AO2) — the skill that most distinguishes A from A* candidates.

02

Evaluative Depth (AO3)

The highest marks in Psychology require genuine evaluation — identifying methodological limitations, considering alternative explanations, discussing real-world applications, making links between approaches. We develop evaluative depth as a specific and recurring skill in every session.

03

16-Mark Essay Structure

16-mark essays are the highest-mark questions in A-Level Psychology and require a specific format — AO1 description, AO3 evaluation, specialist terminology, and evidence. We practise these essays regularly with timed conditions and mark-scheme feedback.

04

Research Methods Mastery

Research Methods questions can appear across all three papers and are among the most commonly underperformed areas. We cover every element systematically — experimental design, statistical testing, mathematical skills, and report writing — until students can answer any Research Methods question confidently.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for A-Level Psychology?

A-Level Psychology specialists covering AQA, Edexcel, and OCR
All core topics covered in depth — Social Influence, Memory, Attachment, Psychopathology, Approaches, Biopsychology
All optional topics supported — Forensic, Relationships, Schizophrenia, Addiction, Cognition and Development
16-mark essay coaching with mark scheme precision
AO2 application question training — the most commonly underperformed skill
Research Methods mastery including statistical test selection and mathematical skills
Issues and Debates preparation — the essential Year 13 synoptic topic
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Psychology at Improve ME

Yes — A-Level Psychology is classified as a science and assessed as one. It includes significant content on research methods, experimental design, statistical testing, and mathematical skills. Students are expected to approach Psychology empirically and scientifically. Many universities accept Psychology as a science A-Level for relevant degree courses.
These are the three assessment objectives. AO1 is knowledge and understanding — describing studies, theories, and concepts. AO2 is application — using psychological knowledge to explain novel scenarios and situations. AO3 is analysis and evaluation — critically assessing studies, theories, and methods. The highest marks require all three to be demonstrated in a single response.
Very important — they make up approximately one third of the total marks. Paper 3 covers Issues and Debates plus three optional topics, and students need to know their chosen options in the same depth as the core topics. We support all commonly chosen optional topics and tailor the Year 13 programme to each student's specific options.
Yes — Research Methods is one of the most learnable areas of A-Level Psychology once it is taught systematically. Many students find it confusing because it is taught alongside other content rather than as a standalone unit. We teach the full Research Methods content from scratch if necessary, covering experimental design, non-experimental methods, statistical test selection, and mathematical skills in a logical sequence.
Yes — we support AQA, Edexcel, and OCR Psychology. While AQA is the most widely studied specification, we have tutors familiar with all three boards and can tailor sessions to whichever specification your child is following.

IB Mathematics

IB Mathematics AA and AI at HL and SL — specialist tutors for all four pathways, IA mentoring, Paper 3 problem-solving, and past paper programmes aligned to the current syllabus.

Book Free AssessmentView All Mathematics Courses
OVERVIEW

What is IB Mathematics?

IB Mathematics offers two distinct pathways — Mathematics: Analysis and Approaches (AA) for students who enjoy algebraic rigour, proof, and calculus, and Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation (AI) for those who prefer statistics, modelling, and real-world application. Both are available at Higher Level (HL) and Standard Level (SL), giving four distinct courses with significantly different content and difficulty levels. Maths AA HL is widely regarded as the most demanding IB subject and is strongly preferred by top universities for Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, and Computer Science degrees. At Improve ME, our specialist IB Maths tutors cover all four pathways — AA HL, AA SL, AI HL, and AI SL — with dedicated Internal Assessment mentoring and timed past paper preparation aligned to the current IB syllabus.

IB
Qualification
Ages 16-18
Age range
SL & HL
Exam boards
COURSE CONTENT

IB Mathematics Course Content

SL & HL coverage plus Internal Assessment (IA) support and exam strategy.

Mathematics AA (Analysis and Approaches)

  • •Number and Algebra — sequences and series (arithmetic and geometric), binomial theorem, proof by induction (HL), complex numbers (HL), systems of equations
  • •Functions — domain and range, composite and inverse functions, transformations, rational functions, odd and even functions
  • •Geometry and Trigonometry — triangle trigonometry, unit circle, trigonometric identities and equations, radians, arc length and sector area, vectors (HL: vector equations of lines and planes, cross product)
  • •Statistics and Probability — descriptive statistics, probability rules, discrete distributions (binomial, Poisson HL), continuous distributions (normal), hypothesis testing, confidence intervals (HL)
  • •Calculus — limits and continuity, differentiation (product, quotient, chain rules), integration (by substitution, by parts HL), differential equations (HL), Maclaurin series (HL), kinematics applications
  • •HL Extensions — further proof techniques, complex number loci and De Moivre's theorem, further calculus techniques, solving systems of differential equations

Mathematics AI (Applications and Interpretation)

  • •Number and Algebra — scientific notation, arithmetic and geometric sequences, financial mathematics (compound interest, annuities, amortisation), logarithmic functions
  • •Functions — modelling with linear, quadratic, exponential, logarithmic, sinusoidal, and logistic functions; regression analysis; GDC-dependent graphing and analysis
  • •Geometry and Trigonometry — volumes and surface areas of 3D shapes, right-angle and non-right-angle trigonometry, Voronoi diagrams (HL), graph theory (HL)
  • •Statistics and Probability — sampling techniques, data presentation, measures of dispersion, regression, r and r² values, probability distributions (normal and binomial), hypothesis testing (chi-squared, t-test), Markov chains (HL)
  • •Calculus — differentiation and integration for optimisation and area under curves, numerical methods, Euler's method (HL), coupled differential equations (HL)
  • •HL Extensions — network graphs, transition matrices, further statistical analysis, calculus modelling with technology

Internal Assessment and eAssessment

  • •Mathematics IA (Internal Assessment) — Mathematical Exploration worth 20% of final grade; student-chosen topic; assessed on five criteria: Presentation, Mathematical Communication, Personal Engagement, Reflection, Use of Mathematics
  • •Topic selection guidance — choosing a topic with sufficient mathematical depth, personal engagement, and exploration potential
  • •IA structure — introduction, mathematical content (the bulk of the IA), personal engagement, reflection, conclusion
  • •Common IA pitfalls — insufficient mathematical complexity, lack of personal voice, poor reflection, over-reliance on GDC output without mathematical explanation
  • •Paper 1 (no GDC) — tests algebraic fluency, manipulation, and recall without calculator assistance; heavily weighted toward pure mathematical reasoning
  • •Paper 2 (GDC allowed) — tests application, modelling, and interpretation; GDC technique is a mark-earning skill in itself
  • •Paper 3 (HL only) — problem-solving paper with two extended questions on unfamiliar scenarios; requires synthesis of multiple topic areas
ASSESSMENT

Assessment Structure

We support both Internal Assessment (IA) and final exams with a structured plan, feedback cycles, and timed practice. SL and HL requirements are clearly differentiated.

Paper 1
No GDC — Pure Mathematical Reasoning
  • •AA SL / AI SL: 80 mins, 40% — short and extended response; no calculator; tests algebraic fluency and manipulation.
  • •AA HL / AI HL: 120 mins, 30% — short and extended response; no GDC; heavily weighted toward pure mathematical reasoning and recall.
  • •All papers externally marked by IB examiners; grade boundaries vary by session; 70%+ typically achieves a 7.
Paper 2
GDC Allowed — Application and Modelling
  • •AA SL / AI SL: 90 mins, 40% — GDC permitted; tests application, modelling, and interpretation.
  • •AA HL / AI HL: 120 mins, 30% — GDC permitted; GDC technique is a mark-earning skill in itself.
  • •AI students use GDC in both papers; AA students only in Paper 2 (and Paper 3).
Paper 3 (HL only) + IA
Problem-Solving + Mathematical Exploration (20%)
  • •HL only: Paper 3 — 60 mins, 20% — two extended questions on unfamiliar scenarios; requires synthesis of multiple topic areas.
  • •IA (Internal Assessment): Mathematical Exploration — 20% of final grade; student-chosen topic; assessed on Presentation, Mathematical Communication, Personal Engagement, Reflection, Use of Mathematics.
  • •IA is internally marked and externally moderated; topic selection and write-up mentoring are essential for high marks.
EXAM BOARDS

Assessment Framework

IB DP (SL)
IB DP (HL)
Internal Assessment (IA)

Not sure which board your child is on? We'll check at your free assessment.

TEACHING APPROACH

Our IB Mathematics Teaching Approach

01

Pathway-Specific Instruction

AA and AI require fundamentally different approaches — AA prioritises algebraic manipulation and proof while AI prioritises modelling, statistics, and GDC application. We never teach these interchangeably and ensure every session is precisely targeted at the student's specific pathway and level.

02

Paper 3 HL Problem-Solving Preparation

Paper 3 presents two extended unfamiliar problems that require students to synthesise knowledge across multiple topic areas under time pressure. We prepare students specifically for this paper with dedicated problem-solving sessions that build the flexible mathematical thinking it demands.

03

IA Topic Selection and Mentoring

The IA is worth 20% of the final grade and many students underperform because they choose topics with insufficient mathematical depth or fail to demonstrate personal engagement and reflection. We guide students through every stage — topic selection, mathematical development, write-up, and review — to maximise IA marks.

04

GDC Technique

For AI students especially, GDC technique is a mark-earning skill. We train students to use their calculator efficiently for regression, solving equations, and graphing — skills that directly earn marks in Paper 2.

WHY CHOOSE US

Why Improve ME for IB Mathematics?

Covers all four IB Maths pathways — AA HL, AA SL, AI HL, AI SL
Specialist tutors for Maths AA HL — one of the most demanding IB subjects
IA topic selection, development, and write-up mentoring
Paper 3 HL dedicated problem-solving preparation
GDC technique training for AI students
Past paper programmes aligned to the current IB syllabus (2019 onwards)
Grade boundary targeting — identifying exact mark targets for 6 and 7
Aligned with all IB World Schools in Dubai
one-to-one and small group sessions available
KHDA-approved tutoring centre at Gold & Diamond Park, Dubai
FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about Mathematics at Improve ME

Mathematics AA (Analysis and Approaches) focuses on algebraic rigour, proof, and calculus — it is the traditional mathematics pathway and is strongly preferred by universities for Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, and Computer Science. Mathematics AI (Applications and Interpretation) focuses on statistics, modelling, and real-world application with significant GDC use. AA HL is significantly more demanding than AI SL. The right choice depends on the student's strengths and university intentions.
Yes — Maths AA HL is widely regarded as the most demanding IB subject. The HL content includes complex numbers, proof by induction, further calculus, and a Paper 3 that presents genuinely novel mathematical problems. Grade 7 boundaries are typically around 75–80%, meaning students need to perform very consistently across all three papers. Strong students with specialist tuition regularly achieve 6s and 7s, but it requires sustained effort and expert support.
Ideally from the start of IB Year 1. The IB Maths curriculum covers a significant amount of content across two years, and starting tutoring early means foundations are built correctly before the harder Year 2 content arrives. For HL students especially, the Year 1 content is already significantly more demanding than GCSE or MYP, and early support prevents compounding gaps.
Yes — IA support is one of our most requested services for IB Maths. We help students select a topic with appropriate mathematical depth and personal engagement, develop the mathematical content, structure the exploration correctly, and review drafts against the IB assessment criteria. The IA is worth 20% of the final grade and should not be left to chance.
We teach the current IB Mathematics syllabus introduced in 2019 (first examined 2021), which replaced the previous Mathematics SL, Mathematics HL, and Mathematical Studies courses. All our past paper resources, teaching materials, and IA guidance are aligned to the current syllabus.
